MATCHED PLAY BATTLEPACK
BORDER WAR
1. PICK YOUR ARMIES
Each player picks an army using the rules in Army Composition (pg 35).
2. SET UP THE BATTLEFIELD
Te players roll oΟ·¬§β²§. Te winner chooses which player is the attacker and 
which is the defender.
Next, the defender sets up objectives in the locations indicated by gold circles 
on the battleΟ·¬§¬¹eld map 
p
. Ten, the defender sets up terrain features. Each 
terrain feature must be set up more than 3" from the battleΟ·¬§¬¹eld edge, more 
than 6" from all other terrain features and more than 3" from all objectives.
Afer objectives and terrain have been set up, the attacker picks which 
territory is their territory. Te other territory is the defenderΟΔβ²§β³Δs territory. Te 
players then resolve the deployment phase. Te attacker begins deployment
(CoreΟ²¬†Rules, 10.0).
VICTORY POINTS
Each player scores victory points at the end of each of their turns as follows:
ΟΔβ²§¬Δ Score 2 victory points if you control at least 1 objective.
ΟΔβ²§¬Δ Score 2 victory points if you control 2 or more objectives.
ΟΔβ²§¬Δ Score 2 victory points if you control more objectives than your opponent.
ADVANCED RULES
In addition to the Core 
Rules, Matched Play: Border 
War uses the following 
Advanced Rules:
ΟΔβ²§¬Δ Commands (pg 29)
ΟΔβ²§¬Δ Terrain (pg 31)
ΟΔβ²§¬Δ Magic (pg 33)
ΟΔβ²§¬Δ Army Composition (pg 35)
ΟΔβ²§¬Δ Command Models (pg 39)
ΟΔβ²§¬Δ Battle Tactics (pg 40)
BATTLEFIELD SIZE
For a 1000-point battle, 
we recommend a 
30"Ο²¬†ΟÉβÄîΟ²¬†44" battleΟ·¬§¬¹eld with 
4Ο²¬†terrain features. 
For a 2000-point battle, 
we recommend a 
44"Ο²¬†ΟÉβÄîΟ²¬†60" battleΟ·¬§¬¹eld with 
8Ο²¬†terrain features.
If you have agreed on a 
points limit outside these 
bounds, feel free to adjust the 
battleΟ·¬§¬¹eld size and number of 
terrain features appropriately.

GLORIOUS VICTORY

playerΟΔβ²§β³Δs general has survived the battle, that player wins a minor victory. If 
the players are tied on victory points and both generals or neither general 
survived the battle, the battle is a draw.

SPEARHEAD

---

## Page 46

Te Age of Sigmar grows fraught. On horizons yonder, the forces of Chaos 
loom, threatening to engulf the Mortal Realms in mayhem and violence 
once more. In the wake of these troubling times, Spearhead forces are 
dispatched across the realms to seek out ancient sites of power that still 
reside deep within the uncharted hinterlands. Will you take up the call to 
battle and lead a Spearhead force to victory?
SPEARHEAD
Welcome to the world of Spearhead. In this game mode, players engage in 
small-scale battles that are fast and tactical, suitable for newcomers and 
veterans alike. In Spearhead, each army is available as a self-contained 
boxΟ²¬†set, allowing players to quickly muster a force. In addition, Spearhead 
battles are fought upon a compact battleΟ·¬§¬¹eld, meaning you can set up a game 
even with limited space. Spearhead uses specially tailored rules to ensure 
that every battle is a tense and exciting aΟ·¬§β²§air that comes right down to the 
Ο·¬§¬¹nal moments.
SPEARHEAD BATTLEPACKS
Veteran players will notice similarities between the rules on Spearhead 
warscrolls and those found in battletomes. However, they have been tweaked 
to make sure they work speciΟ·¬§¬¹cally with Spearhead. Tis means aΟ²¬†battletome 
is not required to play Spearhead ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ all the rules you need to play are available 
to download for free from warhammer-community.com. Tis makes 
Spearhead the ideal format for newcomers to Warhammer Age of Sigmar, 
as well as for players looking for a quick and casual format with lots of re-
playability and tactical depth.
SPEARHEAD BOX SETS
Fighting a Spearhead battle could not be easier. Each player picks their 
army by choosing one of the many Spearhead box sets available. Each of 
these is a complete army, including all the miniatures a player will need 
to do battle. Te rules for 25 diΟ·¬§β²§erent Spearhead box sets are available to 
download for free from warhammer-community.com, allowing you to 
get stuck in as soon as your models are assembled.

---

## Page 47

SPEARHEAD AND BEYOND
Once you have mastered one Spearhead army, you 
may Ο·¬§¬¹nd yourself looking for a fresh challenge. Some 
players choose to ΟΔβ²§Υ€collect broadΟΔβ²§β³Δ ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ this means picking 
up diΟ·¬§β²§erent Spearhead box sets to use with Spearhead 
battlepacks. Perhaps you want to see how a diΟ·¬§β²§erent 
faction plays in Spearhead, or you want to collect a 
Spearhead army from each of the Grand Alliances: 
Order, Chaos, Death and Destruction.
Other players choose to ΟΔβ²§Υ€collect deepΟΔβ²§β³Δ. Tis means 
picking up further units from their Spearhead armyΟΔβ²§β³Δs 
faction to build an army they can use with diΟ·¬§β²§erent 
battlepacks, such as Path to Glory and Matched Play 
battlepacks. If you choose to do so, picking up your 
factionΟΔβ²§β³Δs battletome should be your Ο·¬§¬¹rst port of call.
BATTLETOMES
Battletomes are the ultimate guide to the diΟ·¬§β²§erent 
factions of the Mortal Realms. Each is Ο·¬§¬¹lled with 
detailed lore, inspiring artwork and awesome 
showcases of miniatures. Crucially, they contain the 
faction rules and warscrolls youΟΔβ²§β³Δll need to use your 
army in most games of Warhammer Age of Sigmar.
HOW SHOULD I BUILD MYΟ²¬†MODELS?
When assembling the miniatures in your Spearhead 
box, you may Ο·¬§¬¹nd options to build one of two 
diΟ·¬§β²§erent units. Check the Spearhead rules for the 
army to see which unit to build.
Many more units will have options, such 
as adding a champion, standard 
bearer and musician, or 
perhaps a special weapon 
or two. While the rules 
for these options have 
in most cases been 
simpliΟ·¬§¬¹ed in Spearhead, 
we still recommend you 
assemble your units with these 
extras. Not only will they look 
glorious on the battleΟ·¬§¬¹eld, if 
you decide to use the army 
in other battlepacks, youΟΔβ²§β³Δll 
be able to take advantage 
of the additional rules 
they provide!
Tree-Revenant Musician
Sylvaneth
With Spearhead, setting up a game of Warhammer Age of Sigmar with friends has never been quicker or simpler. 
JustΟ²¬†put your Spearhead force on the table, shuΟ·¬§βÄûe your cards and get playing!

3. PICK YOUR REGIMENT ABILITY AND ENHANCEMENT
Each Spearhead army has a choice of 2 diΟ·¬§β²§erent regiment abilities and 4 diΟ·¬§β²§erent enhancements for your 
general. At the start of the battle, you must pick 1 of each for your army (see Spearhead Armies, pg 49).
5. FIGHT THE BATTLE!
Spearhead battles usually last around an hour and a half. During the battle, the players will each try to score 
victory points, and at the end of the battle, the player with the most is declared the winner!
4. PREPARE YOUR WARSCROLLS
Each set of Spearhead rules includes the warscrolls for all the units used in your Spearhead army. A warscroll 
details how fast and tough a unit is, as well as what weapons it is armed with and any special abilities it has.
ΟΔβ²§¬Δ Every Spearhead army has a set of 
battle traits, regiment abilities
and enhancements.
ΟΔβ²§¬Δ Te battle traits are rules and 
abilities for your army that you will 
use in every battle.
ΟΔβ²§¬Δ Te regiment abilities and 
enhancements are picked at the start 
of every battle. You can experiment 
with diΟ·¬§β²§erent combinations to 
see what works best for you. Some 
regiment abilities and enhancements 
are better against certain foes, so 
it pays to study the strengths and 
weaknesses of the enemy before 
making your choice!
ΟΔβ²§¬Δ Every unit in a Spearhead army has 
its own warscroll, where you can see 
its characteristics and weapons.
ΟΔβ²§¬Δ Any unique abilities the unit can use 
will also be found on its warscroll.
ΟΔβ²§¬Δ At the bottom of every warscroll you 
can Ο·¬§¬¹nd its keywords, such as Hero, 
Wizard, Monster or Infantry.

SPEARHEAD BATTLEPACK
FIRE AND JADE
1.0 SPEARHEAD ARMIES
Each player in a Spearhead battle is the commander of a Spearhead army. 
A Spearhead army is a set of units that comes with its own rules and 
warscrolls. You can download the rules for 25 diΟ·¬§β²§erent Spearhead armies for 
free from warhammer-community.com.
1.1 ARMY COMPOSITION
On the Ο·¬§¬¹rst page of every Spearhead armyΟΔβ²§β³Δs rules, you will Ο·¬§¬¹nd the army 
composition. Tis lists which units are included in the Spearhead army 
and who the general is. Unlike other Warhammer Age of Sigmar armies, 
the composition of a Spearhead army is Ο·¬§¬¹xed and cannot be changed.
1.2 BATTLE TRITS
Every Spearhead army has one or more battle traits. Tese are powerful 
and thematic abilities that apply to some or all of the units in the army. 
For example, the ΟΔβ²§Υ€Holy OrdersΟΔβ²§β³Δ battle trait enables the Stormcast Eternals 
to use powerful once-per-battle abilities to smite their foes, while the 
ΟΔβ²§Υ€Gnawhole AmbushΟΔβ²§β³Δ battle trait allows Skaven warriors to arrive on the 
battleΟ·¬§¬¹eld behind enemy lines.
1.3 REGIMENT ABILITIES
Every Spearhead army has a choice of 2 regiment abilities, each of which 
provides the army with a diΟ·¬§β²§erent strength in battle.
1.4 ENHANCEMENTS
Every Spearhead army has a set of 4 enhancements to pick from. Tese 
are upgrades for the armyΟΔβ²§β³Δs general that make them more powerful in a 
variety of ways.
1.5 SPEARHEAD WARSCROLLS
Te warscrolls for the units in each Spearhead army are included with 
the other rules for the army. Although Spearhead warscrolls are similar 
to the warscrolls found in battletomes, they have been tailored to work 
speciΟ·¬§¬¹cally with Spearhead and cannot be used with other battlepacks.
WIZARDS AND 
PRIESTS IN 
SPEARHEAD
Some units in Spearhead 
have the Wizard or Priest
keyword. If you are familiar 
with other battlepacks, 
it is important to note 
the Magic module in the 
Advanced Rules is not used in 
Spearhead. As a result, there 
are no unbinding rolls and no 
chance of miscasts (among 
other things). Spells and 
prayers are resolved like any 
other ability.
THE CORE RULES
To play a game using this 
battlepack, youΟΔβ²§β³Δll need 
the Core Rules. Te Core 
Rules explain the basics of 
Warhammer Age of Sigmar, 
such as how to move your 
models and how to use 
abilities. You can Ο·¬§¬¹nd the 
Core Rules on pages 5-27.
You donΟΔβ²§β³Δt need the Advanced 
Rules to use this battlepack.
49
SPEARHEAD

PRE-BATTLE SEQUENCE
1. Roll oΟ·¬§β²§. Te winner chooses who is the attacker and who is the defender.
2. Te attacker picks their regiment ability and their enhancement. 
TenΟ²¬†the defender does the same.
3. Te defender chooses which side of the realm battleΟ·¬§¬¹eld the players will 
Ο·¬§¬¹ght on: Aqshy or Ghyran.
4. Te defender picks 1 of the deployment maps on the right and chooses 
which territory belongs to which player.
5. Te defender sets up their terrain features, followed by the attacker. 
Each terrain feature must be set up wholly within friendly territory, more 
than 6" from all other terrain features and more than 3" from both long
battleΟ·¬§¬¹eld edges and enemy territory. Terrain features cannot be set up 
on top of the objectives (either wholly or partially).

BATTLEPLAN
FIRE AND JADE
As the Hour of Ruin dawns, the Ο·¬§¬¹ght to secure sacred sites and 
geomantic nexuses rages Ο·¬§¬¹ercer than ever. It is in Aqshy and Ghyran 
that the Ο·¬§¬¹rst battles of this new age are fought, each faction sending out 
vanguard forces to take and hold these places of power in the name of 
their deities ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ or even the godbeasts of myth. So unpredictable are these 
frontiers of war that the land itself can lend its blessing to the chosen or 
send wracking curses against those who incur its wrath.
DEPLOYMENT
Do not deploy armies as described in the Core Rules. Instead, the attacker
sets up all the units in their army Ο·¬§¬¹rst, followed by the defender. Each 
unit must be set up wholly within friendly territory and more than 6" from 
enemy territory.
BATTLE LENGTH
Te battle lasts for 4 battle rounds.
SPECIAL RULES
PREPARING THE SPEARHEAD DECK
Tis battle uses the Fire and Jade Spearhead deck. One player takes the 
twist deck that corresponds to the side of the battleΟ·¬§¬¹eld the players are 
Ο·¬§¬¹ghting on, shuΟ·¬§βÄûes it and places it face down next to the battleΟ·¬§¬¹eld (the 
other twist deck is not used).
Ten, each player takes 1 battle tactic deck, shuΟ·¬§βÄûes it and places it face 
down where they can reach it during the battle.
55
SPEARHEAD

START OF THE BATTLE ROUND SEQUENCE
Te following sequence is resolved at the start of 
eachΟ²¬†battle round:
1. If it is the Ο·¬§¬¹rst battle round, the attacker chooses who 
will take the Ο·¬§¬¹rst turn. In any other battle round, the 
players make a priority roll (CoreΟ²¬†Rules, 12.0).
2. Determine the underdog (CoreΟ²¬†Rules, 12.0).
3. Te twist card is drawn (pg 50).
4. Te players draw battle tactic cards (see below).
5. Any abilities with the Ο²¬†StartΟ²¬†ofΟ²¬†BattleΟ²¬†RoundΟ²¬† timing 
are used.
TWIST CARDS
One twist card is drawn at the start of each battle 
round. Follow the rules on it as soon as it is drawn.
BATTLE TACTIC 
CARDS
At the start of the Ο·¬§¬¹rst 
battle round, each 
player draws a hand of 
3Ο²¬†battleΟ²¬†tacticΟ²¬†cards.
At the start of each 
subsequent battle round, 
each player draws battle 
tactic cards until they once 
more have a hand of 3 battle tactic cards. Before they do 
so, they can discard any number of battle tactic cards 
inΟ²¬†their hand. Cards are discarded face-up.
VICTORY POINTS
Each player scores victory points at the end of each of 
their turns as follows:
ΟΔβ²§¬Δ Score 1 victory point if you control at least 
one objective.
ΟΔβ²§¬Δ Score 1 victory point if you control two or 
more objectives.
ΟΔβ²§¬Δ Score 1 victory point if you control more objectives 
than your opponent.
ΟΔβ²§¬Δ Score 1 victory point for each battle tactic you 
completed this turn.
GLORIOUS VICTORY
Te player with the most victory points at the end of 
the battle is the winner. If the players are tied on victory 
points, the battle is a draw.
SEIZING THE INITIATIVE
If the player who went second in the previous battle 
round wins the priority roll and chooses to go Ο·¬§¬¹rst, 
it is called seizing the initiative. When a player 
seizes the initiative, they do not draw any battle 
tactic cards for that battle round unless they are 
the underdog and the diΟ·¬§β²§erence in victory points 
between the two players is 5 or greater.

2.0 REALM BATTLEFIELDS
Spearhead battles are fought on realm battleΟ·¬§¬¹elds. Once the players have 
picked their Spearhead armies, they must agree on a realm battleΟ·¬§¬¹eld to use. 
Each realm battleΟ·¬§¬¹eld comes with a matching SpearheadΟ²¬†deck, along with a
battleplan that explains how to Ο·¬§¬¹ght a battle on that realm battleΟ·¬§¬¹eld.
A realm battleΟ·¬§¬¹eld is 30"Ο²¬†ΟÉβÄîΟ²¬†22" and double-sided, with each side 
corresponding to a diΟ·¬§β²§erent Mortal Realm. Te Ο·¬§¬¹rst realm battleΟ·¬§¬¹eld is Fire
and Jade, which has one side representing Aqshy, the Realm of Fire, and the 
y,y
reverse representing Ghyran, the Realm of Life.
3.0 SPEARHEAD DECKS
Spearhead decks are sets of cards that are used in Spearhead battles. Each
realm battleΟ·¬§¬¹eld has its own Spearhead deck. Each SpearheadΟ²¬†deck consists 
of 2 twist decks (one for each side of the realm battleΟ·¬§¬¹eld) and 2 battle tactic 
decks (one for each player).
3.1 TWIST CARDS
Each twist deck is made up of 
k
twist cards. A twist is a special rule
that applies to the battle round being played. Each deck of twist cards
corresponds to a speciΟ·¬§¬¹c side of the realm battleΟ·¬§¬¹eld.
3.2 BATTLE TACTIC CARDS
Each battle tactic deck is made up of 
k k
battle tactic cards. Te rules on
each are split into 2 parts: a battle tactic and a command. You can either 
score the battle tactic at the end of your turn if you meet its conditions (to
gain victory points) or you can use the command on the card for a more
immediate beneΟ·¬§¬¹t. You cannot do both, so choose wisely!
Each command will say when it can be used and what eΟ·¬§β²§ect it has. Afer
the eΟ·¬§β²§ect has been resolved, the card is discarded. You can issue multiple
commands to the same unit in the same phase.
4.0 SPEARHEAD BATTLEPLANS
Each realm battleΟ·¬§¬¹eld has a corresponding battleplan. Te battleplan is a set 
of instructions on how to Ο·¬§¬¹ght the battle and how to determine the winner.
You can Ο·¬§¬¹nd the battleplan for the Fire and Jade realm battleΟ·¬§¬¹eld on 
pages 55-56.

SPEARHEAD

## Page 52

5.0 TERRIN
In every Spearhead battle, the armies Ο·¬§¬¹ght around 2Ο²¬†large terrain features and 2 small terrain features.
Before the armies are deployed, each player will set up 1 large terrain feature and 1 small terrain feature wholly 
within their territory. Each terrain feature has certain passive abilities depending onΟ²¬†its size:
Large Terrain Feature: Cover, Obscuring, Unstable
Small Terrain Feature: Cover, Unstable
COVER: Subtract 1 from hit rolls for attacks that target a unit that is behind or wholly on this terrain feature, 
unless that unit charged or has the Fly keyword.
OBSCURING: A unit cannot be targeted by shooting attacks if it is behind or wholly on this terrain feature, unless 
it has the Fly keyword.
UNSTABLE: Models can move across but cannot be set up on or end any type of move on any part of this terrain 
feature that is more than 1" tall.

7.0 REINFORCEMENTS
Some units have the Reinforcements keyword. Tese units can be 
brought back to the battleΟ·¬§¬¹eld afer being destroyed as replacement units
(CoreΟ²¬†Rules, 24.2).
If any of your units have the Reinforcements keyword, you can use the 
following ability:
CALL FOR REINFORCEMENTS: A second wave of troops enters 
the fray, replacing those lost to the rigours of battle.
Declare: Pick a friendly Reinforcements unit that has been destroyed.
EΟ·¬§β²§ect: Set up an identical replacement unit on the battleΟ·¬§¬¹eld, wholly 
within friendly territory, wholly within 6" of the battleΟ·¬§¬¹eld edge and 
notΟ²¬†in combat.
Each Reinforcements unit can only be replaced once. Replacement 
units cannot themselves be replaced.

6.0 OBJECTIVES
In Spearhead battles, objectives are printed on the surface of each realm 
battleΟ·¬§¬¹eld (the objective is the whole circle, not just the symbol in the centre).
6.1 CONTESTING OBJECTIVES
In Spearhead battles, while a model is on an objective (wholly or 
partially), it is contesting it.
Units with the 
Reinforcements 
keyword have this 
icon in the top-right 
corner of their warscrolls for 
ease of use.

RUN
Declare: Pick a friendly unit that is not inΟ²¬†combat

RETREAT
Declare: Pick a friendly unit that is inΟ²¬†combat to

Declare: Pick a friendly unit that is not inΟ²¬†combat
and has not used a Run or Retreat ability this

of 2D6.
EΟ·¬§β²§ect: Tat unit can move a distance up to the

must end that move within Ο²¬Ϋ" of an enemy unit. If

Core, Move, Charge
FIGHT
Declare: Pick a friendly unit that is inΟ²¬†combat or

as the target(s) of that unitΟΔβ²§β³Δs attacks.

SHOOT
Declare: Pick a friendly unit that has not used

UNIVERSAL WEAPON ABILITIES
Anti-X (+1 Rend): Add 1 to this weaponΟΔβ²§β³Δs Rend characteristic if the target has the keyword afer ΟΔβ²§Υ€Anti-ΟΔβ²§β³Δ or 
fulΟ·¬§¬¹ls the condition afer ΟΔβ²§Υ€Anti-ΟΔβ²§β³Δ. Multiples of this ability are cumulative. For example, if a weapon has both 
Anti-charge (+1 Rend) and Anti-Hero (+1 Rend), then add 2 to the Rend characteristic of the weapon for 
attacks that target a Hero that charged in the same turn.
Charge (+1 Damage): Add 1 to this weaponΟΔβ²§β³Δs Damage characteristic if the attacking unit charged this turn.
Companion: Unless otherwise speciΟ·¬§¬¹ed, this weapon is not aΟ·¬§β²§ected by friendly abilities that aΟ·¬§β²§ect weapon 
characteristics or the attack sequence, except for those that apply negative modiΟ·¬§¬¹ers to it (e.g.Ο²¬†ΟΔβ²§Υ€Covering FireΟΔβ²§β³Δ).
Crit (2 Hits): If an attack made with this weapon scores a criticalΟ²¬†hit, that attack scores 2 hits on the target 
unit instead of 1. Make a wound roll for each hit.
Crit (Auto-wound): If an attack made with this weapon scores a criticalΟ²¬†hit, that attack automatically wounds 
the target. Make a save roll as normal.
Crit (Mortal): If an attack made with this weapon scores a criticalΟ²¬†hit, that attack inΟ·¬§βÄöicts mortal damage on 
the target unit equal to the Damage characteristic of that weapon and the attack sequence ends.
Shoot in Combat: Tis weapon can be used to make shooting attacks even if the attacking unit is in combat.

army: All of a playerΟΔβ²§β³Δs units are

(Core Rules, 1.0)
army composition: Te step before

At the Double: Command,

Phase, 30", banishment roll of 2D6.

the manifestation to remove it from

battle proΟ·¬§¬¹le: Important

size. (Army Composition, 1.3)
battle round: Te battle lasts a

2 turns (1 for each player), and each

battle tactics: Attempt by using the 
ΟΔβ²§Υ€Tactical GambitΟΔβ²§β³Δ ability at the start 
of your turn. Complete at the end

(Battle Tactics, 1.0)

ΟΔβ²§Υ€the battleΟ·¬§¬¹eld edgeΟΔβ²§β³Δ. It is divided

Tis glossary is an alphabetised list of rules deΟ·¬§¬¹nitions. It is intended to be a useful resource if you need a quick

Charge (+1 Damage): Weapon

combat attack: An attack made with

combat range: ModelΟΔβ²§β³Δs combat

any distance vertically from

command points: Each player 
gets 4 command points at start

commander: You are the

(CoreΟ²¬†Rules, 5.1)
Counter-charge: Command, 2CP. 
Charge in enemy charge phase. 
(Commands, 5.0)

Crit (2 Hits): Weapon ability.

Crit (Auto-wound): Weapon ability.

D3: Roll a D6 and halve the result,

modiΟ·¬§¬¹ers (characteristics):

phases: Tere are 7 phases in each

Rally: Command, Any Hero

Save characteristic: Te number

set up: When setting up  a unit, all

Shoot: Attack with ranged weapons.

Shoot in Combat: Weapon ability.

spell: A special type of ability that

spell lore: If taken, all eligible

Standard Bearer: Add 1 to the 
control score of units with any

Models, 3.0)
straight line: If a rule asks you to 
draw a line between two points, it 
is assumed to be an imaginary line 
1mm wide.
Strike-first: Units with this 
keyword use Fight abilities before
units that donΟΔβ²§β³Δt have this keyword. 
If both players have units with 
Strike-first, they alternate 
Ο·¬§¬¹ghting with those units before 
other units get to Ο·¬§¬¹ght, starting 
with the active player. Afer those 
units have fought, the active player

(CoreΟ²¬†Rules, 19.0)
Strike-last: Units with this 
keyword use Fight abilities afer all 
units that donΟΔβ²§β³Δt have this keyword.  
(CoreΟ²¬†Rules, 19.0)
summon: Manifestations are 
summoned to the battleΟ·¬§¬¹eld using 
the spell or prayer on their warscroll. 
(Magic, 7.0)
Tactical Gambit: You must use 
this ability at the start of your turn 
in order to attempt a battle tactic. 
(Battle Tactics, 2.0)
target (attacks): You must declare 
the targets of all the attacks you are 
making with a unit before following 
the attack sequence for those 
attacks, and you can split attacks 
freely between eligible target units. 
(CoreΟ²¬†Rules, 16.0)
terrain features: Models that 
reΟ·¬§βÄöect the battleΟ·¬§¬¹eld environment. 
Tey ofen have their own rules. 
(Terrain, 1.0)
territory (within/wholly within):
A unit is within a territory if any 
part of the base of any model in the 
unit is within that territory. A unit 
is wholly within a territory if all 
parts of the bases of all models in 
the unit are within that territory.

token: Tokens are not considered 
models for rules purposes. 
Teir main purpose is to act as 
reminders for persisting eΟ·¬§β²§ects 
that apply to speciΟ·¬§¬¹c units. Tey 
are otherwise ignored and can be 
freely moved if they get in the way. 
(CoreΟ²¬†Rules, 23.0)
timing: Te part of an ability 
that speciΟ·¬§¬¹es when it can be used. 
Usually appears as a colour-
coded bar at the top of the ability. 
(CoreΟ²¬†Rules, 5.2)
turns: Each battle round consists 
of 2 turns (1 for each player), each 
comprising 7 phases. Te player 
whose turn is taking place is the 
active player. (CoreΟ²¬†Rules, 11.0)
Unbind: Reaction to a Spell ability. 
Make an unbinding roll of 2D6. If 
the roll beats the casting roll, the 
Spell ability fails. (Magic, 4.0)
underdog: Player with fewest 
victory points. If tied, there is no 
underdog for that battle round. 
(CoreΟ²¬†Rules, 12.0)

Ο²¬°
CORE RULES & GENERALΟΔβ²§β³ΔS 
HANDBOOK
ΟΔβ²§¬Δ	Regimented Forces: If one player has 
more regiments than their opponent at 
the start of the battle, then once per battle, 
that player can re-roll their priority roll 
after seeing the result of both playersΟΔβ²§β³Δ rolls 
but before determining priority for that 
battle round.
ΟΔβ²§¬Δ	Split battle profiles and rules updates into 
two documents.
ΟΔβ²§¬Δ	Added a free Tournament OrganiserΟΔβ²§β³Δs 
Pack download.
ΟΔβ²§¬Δ	Added seasonal variant Scourge of 
Ghyran faction rules and warscrolls 
and instructions for how to use them in 
your armies.
BATTLE PROFILES
ΟΔβ²§¬Δ	A limited number of dominant 
enhancement choices, faction terrain 
features, spell lores and manifestation 
lores now cost points.
ΟΔβ²§¬Δ	Points reductions for nearly every faction, 
focused on non-Hero Monsters, War 
Machines (including chariots), and 
non-Wizard/Priest Infantry and 
Cavalry Heroes.
ΟΔβ²§¬Δ	Updated regiment options on several 
Warhammer Legends units to allow 
heroes to join other regiments and/or 
allow other heroes to join their regiments.
SPEARHEAD
ΟΔβ²§¬Δ	Castelite Company (Cities of Sigmar): 
Added Reinforcements keyword to 
Freeguild Cavaliers. Freeguild Steelhelms 
are now 2 units of 5 models.
ΟΔβ²§¬Δ	Skyhammer Taskforce (Kharadron 
Overlords): Tweaked Arkanaut FrigateΟΔβ²§β³Δs 
ΟΔβ²§Υ€Bomb RacksΟΔβ²§β³Δ ability to be more reliable 
and effective. 
ΟΔβ²§¬Δ	Heartflayer Troupe (Daughters of 
Khaine): Tweaked Melusai IronscaleΟΔβ²§β³Δs 
ΟΔβ²§Υ€AllΟΔβ²§βÄ‰out SlaughterΟΔβ²§β³Δ ability to allow it to 
combine with Blood Rites bonus. 
ΟΔβ²§¬Δ	Fluxblade Coven (Disciples of Tzeentch): 
Added Reinforcements keyword to 
Kairic Acolytes.
ΟΔβ²§¬Δ	Blades of the Lurid Dream (Hedonites 
of Slaanesh): Reduced DP thresholds for 
ΟΔβ²§Υ€Sadistic SpiteΟΔβ²§β³Δ and ΟΔβ²§Υ€Oblivious IndulgenceΟΔβ²§β³Δ.
USING THE SCOURGE OF GHYRAN RULES 
The Scourge of Ghyran rules are a set of seasonal rules that 
include additional faction rules and alternative warscrolls for 
every faction. These rules are for use in battles that use the 
GeneralΟΔβ²§β³Δs Handbook 2025-26 battlepack.
SCOURGE OF GHYRAN WARSCROLLS
If you wish to use a Scourge of Ghyran warscroll, every instance of 
that unit in your army must use that warscroll. You cannot use the 
printed (i.e. battletome or faction pack) version of the warscroll for 
one unit and the Scourge of Ghyran warscroll for another.
If you decide to use the Scourge of Ghyran version of a warscroll, 
you do not have to use all other Scourge of Ghyran warscrolls for 
your faction.
In addition, other rules that interact with the printed version of that 
unit interact with the Scourge of Ghyran version too. For example, 
a rule (including regiment options) that calls out a unit type by the 
name on its warscroll interacts with both the printed version of that 
unit and the Scourge of Ghyran version; they are synonymous in 
this regard.
SCOURGE OF GHYRAN AND RULES OF RENOWN
Armies of Renown can include Scourge of Ghyran units if the 
normal version of the unit is listed in their roster options, but 
they cannot use Scourge of Ghyran faction rules or include 
Scourge of Ghyran enhancements. Regiments of Renown 
cannot include Scourge of Ghyran units.
SCOURGE OF GHYRAN FACTION RULES
The additional faction rules consist of a variety of battle 
formations, artefacts of power tables, heroic traits tables, 
special enhancement tables (such as monstrous traits), 
spell lores and prayer lores.
When picking your armyΟΔβ²§β³Δs battle formation, you can 
pick 1 of the Scourge of Ghyran battle formations 
available to your faction instead of any others available 
to it. In other words, you can still only pick 1 battle formation for 
your army.
If you pick from a Scourge of Ghyran artefacts of power or heroic 
traits table, you pick from that table instead of other such tables 
available to your faction, not in addition to. This is stated in the 
rules that accompany the relevant table.
Some of the Scourge of Ghyran enhancement tables are special 
enhancements unique to that faction, for example monstrous traits. 
This is an enhancement table available to your faction like any 
other; therefore, you can pick 1 enhancement from it for your army, 
as per the Army Composition rules, 4.1.
We hope that you enjoy using the Scourge of Ghyran rules and that 
they add a fun new dimension to your faction!
JUNE 2025
BATTLESCROLL SUMMARY

Ο²¬°
BATTLE PROFILES
DECEMBER 2025
BATTLE PROFILES
On the following pages, you will find the battle profiles for most of the units that can be used in games of Warhammer Age 
of Sigmar. The battle profiles are organised first by Grand Alliance and then alphabetically by faction. There are four Grand 
Alliances: Order, Chaos, Death and Destruction. The Grand Alliance to which a unit belongs is determined by the keyword 
listed on its warscroll. For example, the Vindictors warscroll has the Order keyword, so the battle profile for a Vindictors 
unit can be found in the ΟΔβ²§Υ€OrderΟΔβ²§β³Δ section. As the unit belongs to the Stormcast Eternals faction, its entry is in the Stormcast 
Eternals battle profile table.
All units in your army must be from the same faction, with the exception of Regiments of Renown (see below).
When we republish a set of battle profiles, the new version takes precedence over versions with an earlier publication date or 
no publication date. Battle profiles that have changed since the previous version will be marked with a star (ΟΔ≈™¬Ι).
REGIMENTS OF RENOWN
Regiments of Renown are pre-built regiments made up of legendary units from across the Mortal Realms that you can ally into 
your armies. In this document, you can find a list of Regiments of Renown that contains their points values, a summary of the 
units that make up each regiment and any other notes, including which factions can take them. The rules for these Regiments 
of Renown can be found on warhammer-community.com.
The following restrictions apply to Regiments of Renown:
ΟΔβ²§¬Δ	 You cannot include more than 1 Regiment of Renown in your army unless otherwise specified in the notes column of that 
regimentΟΔβ²§β³Δs battle profile.
ΟΔβ²§¬Δ	 Units in a Regiment of Renown cannot use (but can be picked as the target of, if otherwise eligible) any faction rules from the faction they 
are allied into, including enhancements and lores, unless they have a keyword that matches that factionΟΔβ²§β³Δs name.
ΟΔβ²§¬Δ	 If an ability allows you to set up a replacement unit (Core Rules, 24.2) for a unit in a Regiment of Renown, that unit also counts as being 
part of that Regiment of Renown.
WARHAMMER LEGENDS
Every Citadel Miniature is a unique piece of the ever-evolving narrative of Warhammer. It is, 
then, an unfortunate truth that we canΟΔβ²§β³Δt continue to sell and support every model weΟΔβ²§β³Δve ever 
made indefinitely. As we make new models, and new books to explore their background and 
rules, we have to stop producing and featuring some older models. But just like many of you, 
we still treasure our collections of older Citadel Miniatures, and we still want to be able to 
use them in games and forge glorious narratives on the tabletop!
This is where Warhammer Legends comes in: it gives us a place to publish rules that allow you 
to play games with your older miniatures. In order to maintain a healthy game balance and a fair 
playing field, Warhammer Legends units are not intended for competitive play and so will not 
be legal at official Warhammer Age of Sigmar tournaments. Nevertheless, we encourage you to 
continue to use your collection for casual play. Warhammer Legends battle profiles are organised 
first by Grand Alliance and then alphabetically by faction. The rules for these units can be found 
on warhammer-community.com.
KEY
Rules are updated regularly, with the type of change made indicated by one of the 
following ΟΔβ²§Υ€badgesΟΔβ²§β³Δ:
	
NEW	
	
This shows that a new battle profile(s) has been added since the last 
time this document was published.
	
UPDATED	 	
This shows that an existing profile(s) has changed since the last 
time this document was published.
More recent rules updates always take precedence over earlier versions.

KEY
Rules are updated regularly, with the type of change made indicated 
by one of the following ΟΔβ²§Υ€badgesΟΔβ²§β³Δ:
	
NEW	
	
This shows that a rules update is new since the last 
time this document was published.
	
UPDATED	 	
This shows that an existing rules update has 
changed since the last time this document was 
published.
	
DELETED	
	
This shows that the rules update no longer applies 
(the text will also be scored out like this).
More recent rules updates always take precedence over earlier 
versions.

## Page 2

Ο²¬°
FEBRUARY 2026
CORE RULES
RULES UPDATES
2.2 DICE
Add the following:
ΟΔβ²§Υ€ΟΔβ²§¬Δ Some rules allow you to re-roll a dice 
roll, which means you get to roll some or 
all of the dice again. You cannot re-roll a 
dice more than once, and re-rolls happen 
before modifiers to the roll (if any) are 
applied.ΟΔβ²§β³Δ
7.0 COMBAT RANGE
Add the following:
ΟΔβ²§Υ€A model is considered to be in combat with 
an enemy unit if that unit is within the 
modelΟΔβ²§β³Δs combat range and visible to it.ΟΔβ²§β³Δ
18.3 SLAIN MODELS
Add the following:
ΟΔβ²§Υ€You must remove the fewest models 
possible to make the unit a single coherent 
group.ΟΔβ²§β³Δ
27.0 MODIFIER ORDER
Add the following:
ΟΔβ²§Υ€The effects of some abilities (e.g. ΟΔβ²§Υ€An 
Excess of DepravityΟΔβ²§β³Δ) allow you to replace a 
roll with a fixed value. When doing so, you 
must replace the roll before rolling the dice 
for it.ΟΔβ²§β³Δ
30.0 ORDER OF EFFECTS
Add the following:
ΟΔβ²§Υ€Some abilities have a delayed effect 
(e.g.Ο²¬†ΟΔβ²§Υ€each time a friendly unit uses a Fight 
ability, after that ability has been resolved, 
Heal (D3) that unitΟΔβ²§β³Δ). If more than one of 
these effects would be resolved at the same 
time, the active player resolves the delayed 
effects of their abilities first, in an order of 
their choosing, then their opponent does 
the same.ΟΔβ²§β³Δ
5.2 USING ABILITIES
Change point 2 (Use Reactions) to:
ΟΔβ²§Υ€Starting with the player using the ability, the players alternate 
using any abilities with an appropriate Reaction timing. Players 
can choose to pass instead of using a reaction, but once both 
players consecutively pass, no further reactions to that ability can 
be used.ΟΔβ²§β³Δ
6.0 VISIBILITY
Add the following:
ΟΔβ²§Υ€If a rule or ability requires a target to be both within a given 
range of and visible to the unit using that ability, or to a model in 
that unit, both conditions must be met by the same model in the 
target. You could not, for instance, target a unit where one model is 
within range but not visible and another model is not in range but 
is visible.ΟΔβ²§β³Δ
14.4 COMBAT PHASE
In the declare step of the ΟΔβ²§Υ€FightΟΔβ²§β³Δ ability, change ΟΔβ²§Υ€pile-in move (see 
15.4)ΟΔβ²§β³Δ to ΟΔβ²§Υ€pile-in move (see 15.3)ΟΔβ²§β³Δ.
Add the following section to the Core Rules:
ΟΔβ²§Υ€15.5 OVERHANGING MODELS
Some models overhang their base. When moving models, 
overhanging parts should be treated as if they do not exist when 
determining where a model could be placed. Where possible, 
a model should be rotated to allow for another model to end a 
move in proximity to it. When rotating a model this way, the 
base must occupy the same position ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ a circular base may be 
rotated any amount, while an oval base may only be rotated 180Ο²¬Α. 
Otherwise, mark down the location of where the model should 
end its move (such as with an empty base of the same size) and 
place the model to the side. Return the model to the table as soon 
as it is physically possible to do so.ΟΔβ²§β³Δ
ADDENDA
The following rules updates add text in order to clarify ambiguities and/or avoid unintended interactions.
ERRATA
The following rules updates correct errors in order to clarify ambiguities and/or avoid unintended interactions.

## Page 4

Ο²¬°
FEBRUARY 2026
CORE RULES
RULES UPDATES
FREQUENTLY ASKED QUESTIONS
In this series of questions and answers, the rules-writing team respond to playersΟΔβ²§β³Δ queries and explain how the rules are intended to be used.
2.2 DICE
Q: If an ability allows me to re-roll one dice from an XD6 roll (e.g. 
the Blades of the Hollow King ΟΔβ²§Υ€AureliasΟΔβ²§β³Δ ability), could I then use a 
different ability to re-roll the entire XD6 roll?
A: No.
4.0 WARSCROLLS
Q: How should I resolve an ability that refers to an enemyΟΔβ²§β³Δs Control 
characteristic (e.g. UshoranΟΔβ²§β³Δs ΟΔβ²§Υ€Shroudcage FragmentΟΔβ²§β³Δ) if the target 
does not have a Control characteristic (e.g. a manifestation)?
A: The target is treated as having a Control characteristic of 0.
5.0 ABILITIES
Q: Some abilities (e.g. ΟΔβ²§Υ€All-out AttackΟΔβ²§β³Δ) have a red timing bar. Can 
these abilities only be used in the combat phase?
A: No. The words in the timing bar or, in the case of reactions 
and passive abilities, the specific wording of the ability will let 
you know exactly when you can use it; the colour is just there 
as a play aid. If a phase is not specified, the colour indicates the 
most common phase it is used in or, if it is used in multiple phases 
equally, the timing bar is black.
Q: Some abilities have a green timing bar. What does this mean?
A: The green timing bar is used to indicate defensive abilities, 
many of which can be used in multiple phases.
Q: Are non-passive abilities such as ΟΔβ²§Υ€Burning WyrdflameΟΔβ²§β³Δ optional 
to use?
A: Yes. You must apply the effects of passive abilities and abilities 
that state that they must be used if it is possible to do so, but all 
other abilities are optional to use.
5.1 KEYWORDS
Q: In ΟΔβ²§Υ€Pick a friendly non-Hero Legion of the First Prince 
Daemon Infantry or Cavalry unit that has been destroyed to 
be the targetΟΔβ²§β³Δ (and similar wordings with multiple keywords), does 
ΟΔβ²§Υ€Cavalry unitΟΔβ²§β³Δ mean just that (i.e. with no other keywords) or 
does it mean ΟΔβ²§Υ€ friendly non-Hero Legion of the First Prince 
Daemon Cavalry unitΟΔβ²§β³Δ?
A: It means ΟΔβ²§Υ€friendly non-Hero Legion of the First Prince 
Daemon Cavalry unitΟΔβ²§β³Δ.
Q: Where an ability references multiple keywords but has ΟΔβ²§Υ€non-ΟΔβ²§β³Δ 
at the start of the sequence of keywords (for example, the Skaven 
Brood TerrorΟΔβ²§β³Δs Lend a Claw ability which targets ΟΔβ²§Υ€non-Skryre 
MoulderΟΔβ²§β³Δ unitsΟΔβ²§β³Δ), does this mean the target must not have any 
of the keywords listed (in this example, the target couldnΟΔβ²§β³Δt have 
Skryre or Moulder keywords), or does it mean the target cannot 
have the first keyword but must have the second (in this example, it 
cannot have Skryre but must have Moulder)?
A: The ΟΔβ²§Υ€non-ΟΔβ²§β³Δ only relates to the keyword it is directly attached 
to. Therefore, in the example given, the target cannot have the 
Skryre keyword but must have the Moulder keyword. If the 
intent were to exclude both keywords, then it would be expressed 
as ΟΔβ²§Υ€non-Skryre non-MoulderΟΔβ²§β³Δ.
5.2 USING ABILITIES
Q: When resolving the effect of an ability that refers to a Save 
characteristic of 3+ or better (e.g. the ΟΔβ²§Υ€Cloying QuagmireΟΔβ²§β³Δ spell), 
what does ΟΔβ²§Υ€or betterΟΔβ²§β³Δ mean?
A: It means any Save characteristic with a lower value than 3+, 
such as 2+, that better protects against incoming damage.
Q: If an ability allows another friendly unit to be picked to use a 
Fight ability immediately after a friendly unit has used a Fight 
ability (for example, the Crypt Haunter CourtierΟΔβ²§β³Δs ΟΔβ²§Υ€Knightly 
ExemplarΟΔβ²§β³Δ ability), does the second unit being activated have to meet 
the requirements of the Declare step of the Fight ability it is using?
A: Yes. So, for example, if the second unit being activated was using 
the ΟΔβ²§Υ€FightΟΔβ²§β³Δ ability, it would need to be in combat or have charged in 
the same turn.
5.3 THE RULES OF ONE
Q: Are reaction abilities subject to the Rules of One? For example, if 
a unit has a reaction ability on their warscroll, could they only use it 
once per phase?
A: Yes.
6.0 VISIBILITY
Q: If a non-Fly Infantry Hero (e.g. a Scinari Cathallar) is a 
Shrine Guardian of a Shrine Luminor, and that Shrine Guardian 
is within 1" of an obscuring terrain feature, is the Shrine Guardian 
visible to enemy abilities that target the Shrine Guardian?
A: Yes.
4

## Page 5

Ο²¬°
FEBRUARY 2026
RULES UPDATES
CORE RULES
7.0 COMBAT RANGE
Q: If a model in base-to-base contact with an enemy model retreats 
3" directly away from the enemy model, is it still in combat with that 
enemy model?
A: No.
9.1.1 SETTING UP OBJECTIVES AND 
TERRAINΟ²¬†FEATURES
Q: If a battleplan instructs you to set up an objective on a corner of 
the battlefield, should you place the entire 40mm objective marker 
on the battlefield, or should the centre of the objective marker be on 
the corner?
A: The centre of the objective marker should be on the corner.
9.1.2 TERRITORIES
Q: If a rule requires that a unit be ΟΔβ²§Υ€outside of friendly territoryΟΔβ²§β³Δ or 
ΟΔβ²§Υ€wholly outside of friendly territoryΟΔβ²§β³Δ, would a unit that is not on the 
battlefield (for example a destroyed unit or a unit in reserve) count?
A: No.
10.1 UNIVERSAL DEPLOYMENT PHASE ABILITIES
Q: Can you choose to deploy units that are in a regiment using the 
ΟΔβ²§Υ€Deploy UnitΟΔβ²§β³Δ ability instead of ΟΔβ²§Υ€Deploy RegimentΟΔβ²§β³Δ?
A: Yes. However, once a unit in a regiment has been deployed in 
this manner, you can no longer use ΟΔβ²§Υ€Deploy RegimentΟΔβ²§β³Δ to deploy 
the remaining units in that regiment, since the declare step of that 
ability specifies that ΟΔβ²§Υ€No units in that regiment can have already 
been deployed.ΟΔβ²§β³Δ
Q: Is it mandatory for players to set up a faction terrain feature (if 
one is included on their roster) during the deployment phase?
A: No. A player can choose not to use the ΟΔβ²§Υ€Deploy Faction TerrainΟΔβ²§β³Δ 
ability. However, if both players choose to set up a faction terrain 
feature, the player who begins deployment must set up their faction 
terrain features first (as specified in Step 1 of 10.0).
12.0 START OF BATTLE ROUND
Q: When determining the active player, how do you determine when 
a player has ΟΔβ²§Υ€ finished setting up their armyΟΔβ²§β³Δ?
A: A player has finished setting up their army when all units in 
their army have been deployed. This means that non-Deploy 
Deployment Phase abilities (e.g. The MasqueΟΔβ²§β³Δs ΟΔβ²§Υ€The Endless DanceΟΔβ²§β³Δ 
ability) happen after your army has ΟΔβ²§Υ€finished setting upΟΔβ²§β³Δ.
14.3 CHARGE PHASE
Q: When using the ΟΔβ²§Υ€ChargeΟΔβ²§β³Δ ability, does my unit need to end the 
charge move within Ο²¬Ϋ" of an enemy unit that was visible to the 
charging unit at the start of that charge move?
A: No. It must end the charge move within Ο²¬Ϋ" of an enemy unit 
that is visible to the charging unit when it finishes that charge 
move.
Q: If an ability adds or subtracts to the number of dice that make up 
a charge roll, is that a modifier to the charge roll?
A: No.
15.2 MOVING ACROSS TERRAIN
Q: The rules for moving across terrain state that a model cannot 
end a move mid-climb. How can you tell if a model is mid-climb?
A: A model is mid-climb if it is not possible to rest it on its base in 
that location without additional support. As there is a wide variety 
of terrain and models, it is acceptable for a model to end a move 
with its base at a slight angle because of uneven terrain beneath it, 
but at least half of its base must be within 1" of the terrain feature 
that the model is on or it will be considered mid-climb.
15.4 FLYING
Q: If an ability that allows a unit to move states that the unit cannot 
move into combat (e.g. Normal Move), does Fly allow that unit to 
move across an enemy unitΟΔβ²§β³Δs combat range?
A: Yes.
16.0 PICKING TARGETS
Q: When making shooting attacks, can all models in the attacking 
unit shoot even if the target unit is not visible to some of those 
models?
A: No, the only models in the attacking unit that can make 
shooting attacks are those that the target unit is visible to.
Q: When a unit uses a Shoot ability, does it have to shoot with all 
of the ranged weapons that it is armed with?
A: Yes.
17.0 THE ATTACK SEQUENCE
Q: If an ability allows a unit to score critical hits on unmodified rolls 
of 5+, but that unit needs a 6 to hit (e.g. as a result of subtracting 1 
from hit rolls), would unmodified hit rolls of 5 hit the target? If so, 
would they still trigger any critical hit effects?
A: Although the hits would count as critical hits, the attacks would 
not score a successful hit. As the attack sequence ends if an attack 
fails, effects such as Crit (2 Hits) or Crit (Auto-wound) would 
have no effect. However, effects that are resolved immediately, such 
as Crit (Mortal), would still be triggered by those critical hits.
18.3 SLAIN MODELS
Q: Do models removed as a result of a unit being out of coherency 
count as having been slain?
A: Yes.

## Page 6

Ο²¬°
FEBRUARY 2026
RULES UPDATES
CORE RULES
19.0 STRIKE-FIRST AND STRIKE-LAST
Q: Can I use an ability that allows a friendly unit that does not 
have StrikeΟΔβ²§βÄ‰first to fight immediately after a friendly unit 
that has StrikeΟΔβ²§βÄ‰first if there are one or more enemy units with 
StrikeΟΔβ²§βÄ‰first that have not yet been picked to fight?
A: No. As mentioned in the sidebar next to 19.0, abilities that allow 
a unit to use a Fight ability immediately after another unit do not 
override the StrikeΟΔβ²§βÄ‰first constraints, so you cannot pick a unit 
that does not have StrikeΟΔβ²§βÄ‰first to fight until all other units that 
have StrikeΟΔβ²§βÄ‰first have fought.
Q: If a friendly unit is the only unit that has Strike-first on the 
battlefield and it has an ability that allows a friendly unit to fight 
immediately after it, in what order would units be picked to fight?
A: If you are the active player, the unit that has Strike-first 
would fight first, then you could use the ability to allow another 
friendly unit to fight immediately after it, and then you would pick 
the next unit to fight (i.e. three friendly units would fight back 
to back). If your opponent is the active player, the unit that has 
Strike-first would fight first, you could still use the ability to 
allow another friendly unit to fight, and then your opponent would 
pick the next unit to fight.
Q: In the combat phase, if all eligible non-Strike-last units have 
used a Fight ability, which player gets to pick the first Strike-
last unit to fight?
A: The alternating sequence of picking units to use a Fight ability 
would continue and so the player who did not pick the last non-
Strike-last unit would get to pick the first eligible Strike-last 
unit in their army to use a Fight ability.
20.0 WEAPON ABILITIES
Q: Can I combine multiple instances of the same weapon ability? 
For example, if a weapon already has Anti-Infantry (+1 Rend) 
and it can also gain that ability from another source, would it now 
have +2 Rend against Infantry units?
A: No.
Q: If a weapon has the Companion weapon ability, would it be 
affected by friendly abilities that grant new weapon abilities or that 
modify weapon abilities that the weapon already has?
A: No, unless the ability specifically names the weapon or states 
that it affects Companion weapons.
Q: Can I make a shooting attack with a weapon with the Shoot in 
Combat weapon ability if the unit using that weapon is in combat 
and that unit or the target of that shooting attack is affected by an 
ability such as the Daughters of Khaine ΟΔβ²§Υ€Prophecy of ShelterΟΔβ²§β³Δ 
effect of the Scourge of Ghyran Krethusa the CroneseerΟΔβ²§β³Δs ΟΔβ²§Υ€Blood 
RitualΟΔβ²§β³Δ ability, which has an effect that results in weapon abilities 
other than Companion having no effect?
A: No.
21.0 HEALING
Q: If an ability (e.g. ΟΔβ²§Υ€Seed of RebirthΟΔβ²§β³Δ) would allow a unit that would 
be destroyed to negate all remaining damage points then heal to 
prevent that unit from being destroyed, and another ability (e.g. 
ΟΔβ²§Υ€Quenching the FlamesΟΔβ²§β³Δ) prevents that unit from healing, would that 
unit still be destroyed?
A: Yes.
24.0 SETTING UP UNITS
Q: Can a unit use a Move ability and then be affected by an 
ability which removes them from the battlefield and sets them up 
again in the same movement phase? For example, could a friendly 
nonΟΔβ²§βÄ‰Monster Stormcast Eternals unit move to be wholly 
within 6" of a Stormreach Portal, and then in the same movement 
phase use the ΟΔβ²§Υ€Step Into the StormΟΔβ²§β³Δ ability?
A: Yes. Units cannot use Move abilities in the movement phase 
after being set up on the battlefield that turn, but are able to use 
Move abilities before doing so.
24.2 REPLACEMENT UNITS
Q: If a destroyed unit had an enhancement, and an ability allows 
me to set up a replacement of that unit, would the replacement unit 
also have that enhancement?
A: No.

## Page 7

Ο²¬°
FEBRUARY 2026
RULES UPDATES
CORE RULES
Q: If an ability allows a replacement unit to be set up with half the 
models from the original unit, and the original unit has to have a 
certain proportion of models equipped with specific weapons, does 
the new unit also need to adhere to the same restrictions?
A: No. As stated in 24.2, you can pick any models from the original 
unit to be set up in the replacement unit.
Q: If an ability allows a replacement unit to be set up with half the 
models from the original unit, could I then use an ability such as 
ΟΔβ²§Υ€RallyΟΔβ²§β³Δ to return models to that unit until it reached the unit size of 
the original unit?
A: No. A replacement unit is treated as a new unit with a new unit 
size.
25.0 GUARDED HEROES
Q: If an ability allows me to ignore the effects of the ΟΔβ²§Υ€Guarded HeroΟΔβ²§β³Δ 
ability when picking targets (e.g. the Warlock EngineerΟΔβ²§β³Δs ΟΔβ²§Υ€Sniper-
masterΟΔβ²§β³Δ ability), would the attacking unit still be affected by the -1 to 
hit penalty from ΟΔβ²§Υ€Guarded HeroΟΔβ²§β³Δ?
A: Yes.
26.0 RANDOM CHARACTERISTICS
Q: Do you roll a random characteristic roll before or after declaring 
a Run ability?
A: After declaring it and after all reactions are used.
28.0 ADVANCED ABILITY RULES
Q: If a rule says to pick a number of units with one keyword or 
another (e.g. ΟΔβ²§Υ€Pick up to 3 friendly Skink Infantry or Cavalry 
unitsΟΔβ²§β³Δ), could you pick a combination of units with either keyword, 
or do all the units you pick need to have the same keyword?
A: You can mix and match. In the example given, you could pick 1 
Skink Infantry unit and 2 Skink Cavalry units.
Q: If a persisting effect applies to a unit that is destroyed, and 
another ability allows that unit to return to the battlefield (e.g. the 
ΟΔβ²§Υ€ResurrectionΟΔβ²§β³Δ prayer from the Path to Glory: Ascension battlepack), 
does the persisting effect apply to the returned unit?
A: No.
Q: If a part of an abilityΟΔβ²§β³Δs effect does not state that you ΟΔβ²§Υ€canΟΔβ²§β³Δ or 
ΟΔβ²§Υ€mustΟΔβ²§β³Δ do it, is it mandatory to resolve that part of the effect?
A: Yes. Any part of an effect that is not optional is mandatory. If 
you cannot resolve one part of an effect, none of it applies. For 
instance, if a player picked the Wizard casting ΟΔβ²§Υ€The Hand of 
GorkΟΔβ²§β³Δ to also be the target of the spell, the effect could not be fully 
resolved ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ it would be impossible for the target to be removed from 
the battlefield and set up again wholly within 24" of the caster ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ 
and so the spell would have no effect.
28.2 ΟΔβ²§Υ€ONCE PERΟΔβ²§β³Δ TIMINGS
Q: If an ability that is not on a unitΟΔβ²§β³Δs warscroll has the ΟΔβ²§Υ€Once Per 
TurnΟΔβ²§β³Δ, ΟΔβ²§Υ€Once Per BattleΟΔβ²§β³Δ or ΟΔβ²§Υ€Once Per Battle RoundΟΔβ²§β³Δ timing (without 
ΟΔβ²§Υ€(Army)ΟΔβ²§β³Δ afterwards), can multiple friendly units use that ability in 
that time period?
A: It depends on who is using the ability (see the sidebar next to 
28.2). If a unit is using the ability (i.e. the declare step specifically 
says to pick a unit to use it), then each unit could use that ability 
in that time period. If the player is using the ability (i.e. nothing in 
the ability specifically says that it is used by a unit), it can only be 
used once in that time period. Note that picking the target of an 
ability is not the same as picking a unit to use that ability.

ΟΔβ²§Υ€ΟΔβ²§¬Δ If a terrain feature has any melee or ranged weapons, it can use

Add the following:
ΟΔβ²§Υ€Some faction terrain features allow you to place a unit on them

faction they are allied into, including enhancements and lores, 
unless they have a keyword that matches that factionΟΔβ²§β³Δs name.
ΟΔβ²§¬Δ If an ability allows you to set up a replacement unit (Core Rules, 
24.2) for a unit in a Regiment of Renown, that unit also counts as

Change the third paragraph to:

unbound (see 4.0), then it is successfully cast: resolve its effect.ΟΔβ²§β³Δ
MAGIC, 3.0 PRAYERS
In the second paragraph, change ΟΔβ²§Υ€On a chanting roll of 2 or moreΟΔβ²§β³Δ 
to ΟΔβ²§Υ€On an unmodified chanting roll of 2 or moreΟΔβ²§β³Δ.
MAGIC, 7.0 MANIFESTATIONS
Change the sixth bullet point to:
ΟΔβ²§Υ€Manifestations that have a Move characteristic of 0" (ΟΔβ²§Υ€-ΟΔβ²§β³Δ) cannot 
move. For the purposes of movement, combat range, being in 
combat and setting up other units, they are only treated as if they 
were units in the combat phase. Units can finish a charge move 
within Ο²¬Ϋ" of an enemy manifestation that has a Move characteristic 
of 0" (ΟΔβ²§Υ€-ΟΔβ²§β³Δ) instead of within Ο²¬Ϋ" of an enemy unit.ΟΔβ²§β³Δ
ARMY COMPOSITION, 1.3 BATTLE PROFILES
Change the final sentence to:
ΟΔβ²§Υ€Battle profiles for each unit are available at 
warhammerΟΔβ²§βÄ‰community.com.ΟΔβ²§β³Δ
ARMY COMPOSITION, 3.2 THE GENERAL
Change the first sentence to:
ΟΔβ²§Υ€You must pick 1 Hero in your army that is leading a regiment to 
be your general.ΟΔβ²§β³Δ

## Page 10

Ο²¬°
FEBRUARY 2026
ADVANCED RULES
RULES UPDATES
COMMANDS, 3.0 MOVEMENT PHASE COMMANDS
Q: If an ability modifies a run roll, does that ability modify the 
roll made in the ΟΔβ²§Υ€RedeployΟΔβ²§β³Δ ability, since ΟΔβ²§Υ€RedeployΟΔβ²§β³Δ has the Run 
keyword?
A: No, named rolls such as run roll are only modified by abilities 
that specifically call out that type of roll.
COMMANDS, 4.0 SHOOTING PHASE COMMANDS
Q: If a friendly unit is in combat and is armed with a weapon that 
has Shoot in Combat, can it use the ΟΔβ²§Υ€Covering FireΟΔβ²§β³Δ command?
A: No, you can only pick units that are not in combat to use that 
ability.
Q: If a unit cannot be picked to be the target of shooting attacks 
(e.g. an Infantry Hero affected by the ΟΔβ²§Υ€Guarded HeroΟΔβ²§β³Δ ability) 
but is the closest unit to a friendly unit using the ΟΔβ²§Υ€Covering FireΟΔβ²§β³Δ 
command, do I still have to target that unit?
A: No, you must pick the nearest eligible unit that can be picked to 
be the target of shooting attacks.
Q: If a faction terrain feature is closer to a friendly unit using the 
ΟΔβ²§Υ€Covering FireΟΔβ²§β³Δ command than any other units, do I have to target 
that faction terrain feature? Can I choose to?
A: No to both. However, some faction terrain features (such as the 
Shrine Luminor) allow a unit to be placed on them. Since, in these 
cases, you measure range and visibility to and from the terrain 
feature instead of the unit, you would have to target that terrain 
feature if it was closest.
Q: If a manifestation is closer to a friendly unit using the ΟΔβ²§Υ€Covering 
FireΟΔβ²§β³Δ command than any other units, do I have to target that 
manifestation? Can I choose to?
A: No to both.
COMMANDS, 8.0 END OF TURN COMMANDS
Q: When using the ΟΔβ²§Υ€Power ThroughΟΔβ²§β³Δ command, can my models pass 
through enemy models in the target unit or only through the target 
unitΟΔβ²§β³Δs combat range?
A: Your models can only pass through the target unitΟΔβ²§β³Δs combat 
range, unless your unit has Fly or is otherwise allowed to pass 
through models.
Q: Can faction terrain or manifestations be picked as the target for 
ΟΔβ²§Υ€Power ThroughΟΔβ²§β³Δ?
A: Yes, if it has a Move characteristic greater than ΟΔβ²§Υ€-ΟΔβ²§β³Δ.
TERRAIN, 1.5 FACTION TERRAIN
Q: Are faction terrain features still subject to the restrictions in Core 
Rules, 5.3 The Rules of One?
A: Yes.
Q: If an ability requires you to set up a unit more than 9" from all 
enemy units, could you set up a unit within 9" of a faction terrain 
feature?
A: Yes. However, some faction terrain features (such as the Shrine 
Luminor) allow a unit to be placed on them. Since, in these cases, 
you measure range and visibility to and from the terrain feature 
instead of the unit, you would not be able to set up a unit within 9" 
of a faction terrain feature that has a unit on it.
Q: Are faction terrain features affected by friendly abilities (e.g. 
those that give units the Ward keyword)?
A: No.
Q: Can faction terrain features use command abilities?
A: No, unless that command is on the faction terrain featureΟΔβ²§β³Δs 
warscroll.
Q: If an ability or battle tactic requires a unit to be destroyed (e.g. 
ΟΔβ²§Υ€The Blood TitheΟΔβ²§β³Δ), does destroying a faction terrain feature count?
A: No.
Q: Can a unit placed on a faction terrain feature be picked as the 
target of enemy abilities (as opposed to the target of attacks)? For 
example, if I were to pick a Shrine Luminor as the target of the 
Realmshaper EngineΟΔβ²§β³Δs ΟΔβ²§Υ€Power UnleashedΟΔβ²§β³Δ ability, could I pick the 
hero placed on the Shrine Luminor as a target of that ability?
A: Yes.
Q: Do I need to use a Retreat ability to move away from a faction 
terrain feature that has a Move characteristic of 0"?
A: No, you can use any Move ability. However, some faction 
terrain features (such as the Shrine Luminor) allow a unit to be 
placed on them. Since, in these cases, you measure range and 
visibility to and from the terrain feature instead of the unit, you 
would have to use a Retreat ability to move away from a terrain 
feature that has a unit on it.
MAGIC
Q: Can units in my army use the example spell and example prayer 
from the sidebar?
A: No.
MAGIC, 1.1 POWER LEVEL
Q: If a unit has multiple power levels (e.g. a Hero that is both a 
Wizard and a Priest), and another ability modifies that unitΟΔβ²§β³Δs 
power level (e.g. the ΟΔβ²§Υ€Witchbane CurseΟΔβ²§β³Δ prayer), which power level 
does it modify?
A: If that ability only targets Wizards or only targets Priests, it 
modifies the corresponding power level. If it could target either or 
does not specify the target, it modifies both.
FREQUENTLY ASKED QUESTIONS
In this series of questions and answers, the rules-writing team respond to playersΟΔβ²§β³Δ queries and explain how the rules are intended to be used.
10

## Page 11

Ο²¬°
FEBRUARY 2026
ADVANCED RULES
RULES UPDATES
MAGIC, 2.0 SPELLS
Q: Is mortal damage inflicted by a miscast considered to be ΟΔβ²§Υ€mortal 
damage inflicted by a Spell abilityΟΔβ²§β³Δ for the purposes of abilities like 
the Tzeentch effect of the ΟΔβ²§Υ€Marks of ChaosΟΔβ²§β³Δ ability?
A: No.
MAGIC, 7.0 MANIFESTATIONS
Q: Are manifestations still subject to the restrictions in Core Rules, 
5.3 The Rules of One?
A: Yes.
Q: Are manifestations affected by friendly abilities that would 
normally affect units (e.g. those that give units the Ward keyword)?
A: No, except for those permitted in Magic, 7.0, and those that 
specifically affect manifestations (such as ΟΔβ²§Υ€Banish ManifestationΟΔβ²§β³Δ).
Q: Can manifestations use command abilities?
A: No, unless that command is on the manifestationΟΔβ²§β³Δs warscroll.
Q: If an ability or battle tactic requires a unit to be destroyed (e.g. 
ΟΔβ²§Υ€The Blood TitheΟΔβ²§β³Δ), does destroying a manifestation count?
A: No.
Q: Do I need to use a Retreat ability to move away from a 
manifestation that has a Move characteristic of 0"?
A: No, you can use any Move ability.
Q: If a manifestation has a Move characteristic that is greater than 
0", can it use Move abilities in the movement phase of the same 
turn in which it was set up?
A: No.
Q: If a power level 2 or higher wizard miscasts their first spell, could 
they still use the ΟΔβ²§Υ€Banish ManifestationΟΔβ²§β³Δ ability in that phase?
A: Yes. Miscasting only prevents that wizard from using any more 
spells in that phase.
Q: If a manifestation has multiple parts (e.g. Geminids of UhlΟΔβ²§βÄ‰Gysh) 
and is in combat, can one part of the manifestation be ΟΔβ²§Υ€in combatΟΔβ²§β³Δ 
while the other is not? If one part is in combat can the other part 
make a pile-in move?
A: If any part of a manifestation is considered to be in combat, the 
entire manifestation is considered to be in combat. If one part can 
pile in, all parts can pile in (but must all pile in towards the same 
enemy unit as per the rules for piling in).
Q: How does the Prismatic PalisadeΟΔβ²§β³Δs ΟΔβ²§Υ€Blinding LightΟΔβ²§β³Δ ability 
interact with picking targets for shooting attacks?
A: A model in the attacking unit can only make shooting attacks 
against the target unit if a straight line can be drawn from that 
model to any model in the target unit without that line crossing the 
Prismatic Palisade.
Q: Can manifestations use Move abilities in the movement phase of 
the turn they were set up?
A: No.
Q: Can ΟΔβ²§Υ€Magical InterventionΟΔβ²§β³Δ be used to use the ΟΔβ²§Υ€Banish 
ManifestationΟΔβ²§β³Δ ability after gaining the ability to use ΟΔβ²§Υ€Banish 
ManifestationΟΔβ²§β³Δ from a Place of Power.
A: No.
Q: Are manifestations affected by the ΟΔβ²§Υ€ObscuringΟΔβ²§β³Δ terrain ability?
A: No.
ARMY COMPOSITION, 2.2 ARMIES OF RENOWN
Q: When using an Army of Renown, can I pick a faction terrain 
feature to include on my roster?
A: Yes, unless that Army of RenownΟΔβ²§β³Δs rules specifically state that 
you cannot include a faction terrain feature.

## Page 12

Ο²¬°
FEBRUARY 2026
ADVANCED RULES
RULES UPDATES
Q: When ability A references ability B, where B is in a different 
ruleset from the one I am using, can I use ability B? For example, if 
I am playing the Big Waaagh! army of renown and I have Gordrakk 
in my army, can I use WAAAGH! abilities (i.e. the ΟΔβ²§Υ€Ironjawz 
Waaagh!ΟΔβ²§β³Δ ability) referenced by GordrakkΟΔβ²§β³Δs ΟΔβ²§Υ€Voice of GorkΟΔβ²§β³Δ ability?
A: No. In the example above, ΟΔβ²§Υ€Voice of GorkΟΔβ²§β³Δ is a reaction to a 
Waaagh! ability, and as there are no such abilities in the Big 
Waaagh! faction rules, then the ΟΔβ²§Υ€Voice of GorkΟΔβ²§β³Δ ability cannot be 
used in a Big Waaagh! army, and neither does it grant access to 
Waaagh! abilities. 
ARMY COMPOSITION, 3.1 REGIMENTS
Q: If a Hero is able to join another HeroΟΔβ²§β³Δs regiment (e.g. The 
Shadow Queen joining Morathi-Khaine or an Assassin joining a 
Dreadlord on Black Dragon), do they take the place of a non-Hero 
unit in that regiment?
A: Yes.
Q: Can I add units from other factions to my HeroesΟΔβ²§β³Δ regiments?
A: No. The only way to add units from other factions to your army 
is by taking an eligible Regiment of Renown.
Q: If a HeroΟΔβ²§β³Δs regiment options contain multiple excluded keywords 
(e.g. non-Beast non-Monster), can it include units in its regiment 
that have only one of those keywords?
A: No, it cannot include units with any of those keywords.
Q: Can my army include a Warmaster (e.g. Sigvald, Prince of 
Slaanesh) in another heroΟΔβ²§β³Δs regiment?
A: Yes, but only if your army includes another Warmaster unit, 
since your general must lead a regiment, and you must pick a 
Warmaster as your general if there are any Warmaster units 
in your army.
ARMY COMPOSITION, 3.5 REGIMENTS OF RENOWN
Q: If a Regiment of Renown includes a wizard, could that wizard 
cast spells from a universal manifestation lore that was picked for 
that army?
A: Yes.
Q: If a Regiment of Renown (e.g. the Coven of Thryx) includes one 
or more manifestations, do those manifestations replace picking a 
manifestation lore for my army?
A: No, they are in addition.
Q: Does a Regiment of Renown require a Hero to lead it?
A: No. As stated in 3.5, Regiments of Renown are pre-built 
regiments, so they only include the units stated in their unit 
summary/organisation section.
ARMY COMPOSITION, 4.1 ENHANCEMENTS
Q: If an ability allows a unit to gain an enhancement during a battle 
(for example, the Sons of Behemat ΟΔβ²§Υ€More Stuff for me CollectionΟΔβ²§β³Δ 
ability) and I pick an enhancement that costs points, do I need to 
have sufficient spare points in my army roster in order to pay for 
that enhancement?
A: No. Enhancements added to your army during a battle do not 
cost any points.

SEASON RULES (2025-26)

ΟΔβ²§Υ€REGIMENTED FORCES

priority roll after seeing the result of both playersΟΔβ²§β³Δ rolls but before 
determining priority for that battle round.ΟΔβ²§β³Δ

## Page 22

Ο²¬°
RULES UPDATES
FEBRUARY 2026
GENERALΟΔβ²§β³ΔS HANDBOOK 2025-26
Q: Can I complete the same battle tactic multiple times in a battle?
A: No, unless specified otherwise.
1.0 BATTLE TACTICS OVERVIEW
Q: If I have met the conditions to complete a battle tactic can I 
choose not to complete that tactic at the end of my turn?
A: No. If the conditions are fulfilled and you have not already 
scored a battle tactic from that card this turn, you must score that 
battle tactic.
6.0 ATTACKING (SHOOTING AND COMBAT) 
COMMANDS
Q: If a unit uses ΟΔβ²§Υ€All-out AttackΟΔβ²§β³Δ in the shooting phase and the 
combat phase, do I subtract 2 from save rolls for that unit?
A: No. Persisting effects (see 28.1) count as the effects of passive 
abilities for their duration, so units cannot be affected by the same 
persisting effect more than once.
1.2 UNIVERSAL TERRAIN ABILITIES
Q: While every model in a friendly non-Monster unit is within 1" 
of an Obscuring terrain feature, are they visible to friendly units?
A: Yes. They are just not visible to enemy units outside of their 
combat range.
Q: When a Hero uses ΟΔβ²§Υ€Activate Place of PowerΟΔβ²§β³Δ and picks Tap the 
Ley Lines, can they use both the ΟΔβ²§Υ€UnbindΟΔβ²§β³Δ ability and the ΟΔβ²§Υ€Banish 
ManifestationΟΔβ²§β³Δ ability in the same phase?
A: No.
1.5 FACTION TERRAIN
Q: If a hero is garrisoning a faction terrain feature, can it still be 
picked as the target of abilities other than attacks?
A: Yes.
2.2 ARMIES OF RENOWN
Q: Can I use Scourge of Ghyran enhancements, lores and battle 
formations in an Army of Renown?
A: No.
BATTLEPLAN: CREEPING CORRUPTION
Q: How does damage inflicted by ΟΔβ²§Υ€Pulsing Life EnergiesΟΔβ²§β³Δ interact 
with The Shadow QueenΟΔβ²§β³Δs ΟΔβ²§Υ€Iron Heart of KhaineΟΔβ²§β³Δ ability?
A: Since the damage is inflicted in the Start of Battle Round timing 
window, before either playerΟΔβ²§β³Δs turn has started, ΟΔβ²§Υ€Iron Heart of 
KhaineΟΔβ²§β³Δ would have no effect on those damage points. In addition, 
those damage points would not count towards the limit on damage 
allocation from ΟΔβ²§Υ€Iron Heart of KhaineΟΔβ²§β³Δ in the next turn.
BATTLEPLAN: ROILING ROOTS
Q: In the Roiling Roots battleplan, if a unit that has Strike-last 
because it is contesting an objective that has been picked as the 
target of the ΟΔβ²§Υ€Tangling TendrilsΟΔβ²§β³Δ ability, what happens if the models 
in that unit that are contesting that objective are slain?
A: The unit only has Strike-last while any models in that unit 
are contesting that objective. It loses the Strike-last effect 
immediately after all of the models that were contesting that 
objective have been slain and removed from the battlefield.
BATTLE TACTICS CARD 3: INTERCEPT AND RECOVER
Q: If your opponent has picked the Intercept and Recover battle 
tactic card and they destroy 2 or 3 of your units that are carrying 
Ghyranite Treasure in the same turn, and then they choose to go 
first after going second in the previous battle round, can you remove 
a Ghyranite Treasure that has not been scored yet from a destroyed 
unit?
A: Yes.
Q: If both players have the Intercept and Recover battle tactic 
card, what is the order in which players pick units to be carrying 
Ghyranite Treasures?
A: The attacker picks all their units first, then the defender picks 
all their units.
FREQUENTLY ASKED QUESTIONS
In this series of questions and answers, the rules-writing team respond to playersΟΔβ²§β³Δ queries and explain how the rules are intended to be used.

ORACLE OF ETERNITY
Effect: Pick 1 battle tactics card that is on your roster and discard it. Then, 
for the rest of the battle, replace it with a battle tactics card that is not on 
your roster.
If that new battle tactics card requires either player to pick something, they 
must do so immediately when the new battle tactics card is picked.
The combined total of victory points earned by both the discarded battle 
tactics card and the new battle tactics card is 15. Any additional victory 
points beyond 15 that are earned by completing the battle tactics on both of 
those battle tactics cards are lost.

HEDONITES OF SLAANESH
BATTLE TRAITS
Change ΟΔβ²§Υ€An Excess of DepravityΟΔβ²§β³Δ to:
AN EXCESS OF DEPRAVITY: Wherever the Hedonites of 
Slaanesh roam, reality is unmade. Those who stand against 
them are assailed by titillating phantasms and mind-bending 
illusions until they become slaves to their own base urges.
Declare: Pick up to 3 friendly Hedonites of Slaanesh 
units to be the targets.
Effect: The targets have the Euphoric keyword for the rest 
of the battle round.
In addition, for each friendly unit you pick, give your 
opponent 1 temptation dice. During this battle round, 
instead of making a wound roll, save roll, ward roll or run 
roll, your opponent can use 1 temptation dice to replace the 
roll they would have made with a 6, or instead of making 
a charge roll they can use 2 temptation dice to replace 2 of 
the dice in a charge roll with two 6s (other dice could then 
be rolled normally). Rolls that have been replaced count as 
unmodified rolls and cannot be re-rolled. You cannot use 
temptation dice to replace a re-roll.
Each time your opponent uses a temptation dice, they must 
roll it. On a 1-2, your opponentΟΔβ²§β³Δs roll is replaced by that value 
instead of a 6, and you must allocate D3 damage points to 
the unit for which the roll was replaced immediately after the 
ability used by that unit has been resolved (ward rolls cannot 
be made for those damage points).
At the end of the battle round, any temptation dice that have 
not been used are lost.
Once Per Battle Round (Army), Start of Battle Round

or Faction Terrain).ΟΔβ²§β³Δ

## Page 30

Ο²¬°
FEBRUARY 2026
RULES UPDATES
FACTION PACKS
CITIES OF SIGMAR
Q: Can a Luminark of Hysh use the ΟΔβ²§Υ€Covering FireΟΔβ²§β³Δ command to 
shoot with its Searing Beam of Light?
A: No, because attacks made with Searing Beam of Light require 
you to pick a point on the battlefield to be the target rather than an 
enemy unit.
DAUGHTERS OF KHAINE
Q: Can I use the ΟΔβ²§Υ€All-out SlaughterΟΔβ²§β³Δ ability once per battle with each 
eligible unit?
A: Yes. This ability is used by the unit, not the player, and it does 
not have the ΟΔβ²§Υ€Once Per Battle (Army)ΟΔβ²§β³Δ timing, so each eligible unit 
can use it once per battle.
Q: Can a Sisters of Slaughter with Sacrificial Knives unit use the 
ΟΔβ²§Υ€Dance of DeathΟΔβ²§β³Δ ability to move out of combat?
A: Yes.
Q: Can Morathi-Khaine or the Shadow Queen use abilities that 
would heal them (for example, ΟΔβ²§Υ€RallyΟΔβ²§β³Δ)?
A: Yes. Even though damage points would not be healed by doing 
so, keywords such as Burning or Diseased would be removed 
from them by the use of such an ability.
HEDONITES OF SLAANESH
Q: When using the ΟΔβ²§Υ€Joyous Battle FuryΟΔβ²§β³Δ ability, does Dexcessa need 
to have slain any models in a turn in order to add 1 to the Attacks 
characteristic of their Impaling Talons for each battle fury token 
they have?
A: No. Battle fury tokens are gained (and Dexcessa can heal) on 
any turn in which Dexcessa slew any enemy models, but can gain 
the additional attacks on any turn that they have any battle fury 
tokens.
Q: The effect of the Scourge of Ghyran ΟΔβ²§Υ€Master of TemptationΟΔβ²§β³Δ heroic 
trait states that units can only use Core abilities. How does this 
affect passive abilities?
A: It doesnΟΔβ²§β³Δt. Passive abilities are not ΟΔβ²§Υ€usedΟΔβ²§β³Δ, so this ability doesnΟΔβ²§β³Δt 
have any effect on them.
Q: When using the Keeper of SecretsΟΔβ²§β³Δ ΟΔβ²§Υ€Dark TemptationsΟΔβ²§β³Δ ability, 
can I pick the ΟΔβ²§Υ€If the target is damaged, Heal (D3) the targetΟΔβ²§β³Δ option 
if the target is not damaged?
A: No.
LUMINETH REALM-LORDS
Q: If my battle formation allows me to use a Facet of War ability 
in addition to the one I pick each battle round, can I pick the same 
Facet of War ability in order to use it twice that battle round?
A: No.
Q: If a Scinari Calligrave miscasts a spell when attempting to 
summon a manifestation, can they use their ΟΔβ²§Υ€RealmscribeΟΔβ²§β³Δ ability to 
avoid the miscast?
A: No.
Q: When Ellania and Ellathor use the ΟΔβ²§Υ€Total EclipseΟΔβ²§β³Δ ability, does 
your opponent need to spend an additional command point for the 
first command used by any unit or for the first command used by 
each unit?
A: The first command used by any unit.
Q: Can models end a move in the open spaces underneath a Shrine 
Luminor?
A: Yes. However, if the Shrine Luminor has a Shrine Guardian, an 
enemy unit would need to use an ability that would allow them to 
move into combat with the Shrine Luminor to do so, such as the 
ΟΔβ²§Υ€ChargeΟΔβ²§β³Δ ability.
FREQUENTLY ASKED QUESTIONS
In this series of questions and answers, the rules-writing team respond to playersΟΔβ²§β³Δ queries and explain how the rules are intended to be used.

I successfully cast the ΟΔβ²§Υ€Beam of HyshΟΔβ²§β³Δ spell in a turn?

MAGGOTKIN OF NURGLE
Q: Does the ΟΔβ²§Υ€Disease and Pestilence PersonifiedΟΔβ²§β³Δ ability require 
at least one mortal damage to be inflicted by the ΟΔβ²§Υ€Wracked with 
DiseaseΟΔβ²§β³Δ ability in order for it to inflict 1 additional mortal damage?
A: Yes, as indicated by ΟΔβ²§Υ€add 1 to the amount of mortal damage 
inflicted, if anyΟΔβ²§β³Δ.
Q: If an ability (such as ΟΔβ²§Υ€RallyΟΔβ²§β³Δ) would heal one or more damage 
points allocated to a unit, but that unit does not have any damage 
points allocated to it or has an ability that prevents it from healing 
any damage points, would the Diseased keyword be removed from 
that unit? Similarly, if an ability would return one or more slain 
models to a unit, but no models from that unit are slain, would the 
Diseased keyword be removed from that unit?
A: Yes to both.
OGOR MAWTRIBES
Q: Does the ΟΔβ²§Υ€Great GutlordΟΔβ²§β³Δ heroic trait ignore negative modifiers 
to hit rolls and wound rolls for attacks made with Companion 
weapons?
A: No.
Q: ΟΔβ²§Υ€Trampling ChargeΟΔβ²§β³Δ allows Monsters to add 2 to a D3 roll. How 
does this work?
A: If a rule requires you to roll a D3, roll a dice and halve the total, 
rounding up. For example, if the Ogor Mawtribes player rolled a 5 
on a regular 6-sided dice, that would result in a D3 roll of 3. Then, 
if the charging unit was a Monster, you would add 2 to the roll, 
and the charging Monster would inflict 5 mortal damage. Note 
this means that Monsters will always inflict some amount of 
damage when picked as a target of the ΟΔβ²§Υ€Trampling ChargeΟΔβ²§β³Δ ability, 
as their roll will never be less than a 2.
Q: Does the ΟΔβ²§Υ€Great GutlordΟΔβ²§β³Δ heroic trait ignore negative modifiers 
to hit rolls and wound rolls for attacks made with Companion 
weapons?
A: No.
OSSIARCH BONEREAPERS
Q: At the 2+ stage of the effect of ΟΔβ²§Υ€Curse of YearsΟΔβ²§β³Δ, if I roll 1 or more 
2+ΟΔβ²§β³Δs, can I keep rolling extra dice (and inflicting mortal damage on 
the target) until a 1 is rolled?
A: No.
SYLVANETH
Q: When placing scenery pieces while setting up an Awakened 
Wyldwood, can the ends of each base overlap?
A: No. The tips at the end of each base must touch without 
overlapping part of the base of a different scenery piece.
Q: Can I set up an Awakened Wyldwood so the parts under 1" tall 
are under friendly models?
A: No. You cannot move a model to put the Awakened Wyldwood 
parts underneath it, but the Awakened Wyldwood can be set up 
around a model so that the model is within the ΟΔβ²§Υ€emptyΟΔβ²§β³Δ circle 
formed within the parts of the Awakened Wyldwood.
Q: Does the ΟΔβ²§Υ€Growing WoodsΟΔβ²§β³Δ ability increase the range measured 
from an Awakened Wyldwood that a Branchwych must be wholly 
within in order to use its ΟΔβ²§Υ€Fury of the ForestΟΔβ²§β³Δ ability, or the range 
measured from the Branchwych that the target of the ΟΔβ²§Υ€Fury of the 
ForestΟΔβ²§β³Δ ability must be wholly within, or both ranges?
A: It only increases the range measured from an Awakened 
Wyldwood that a Branchwych must be wholly within in order to 
use ΟΔβ²§Υ€Fury of the ForestΟΔβ²§β³Δ.

FREQUENTLY ASKED QUESTIONS
In this series of questions and answers, the rules-writing team respond to playersΟΔβ²§β³Δ queries and explain how the rules are intended to be used.
ARMIES OF RENOWN
Q: Is the Pyrofane Cult Army of Renown in White Dwarf issue 514 
legal for Matched Play?
A: No, it is a Legends Army of Renown.
SCOURGE OF GHYRAN
Q: Can I include Scourge of Ghyran units in an Army of Renown 
instead of their standard version?
A: Yes.
Q: Can I include Scourge of Ghyran units in a Regiment of Renown 
instead of their standard version?
A: No.
DAUGHTERS OF KHAINE, BLOODSHADOW RITES
Q: When using ΟΔβ²§Υ€ShadowstepΟΔβ²§β³Δ, if my chanting roll was 8+, could I 
pick the priest themselves as the first target, move them, then pick a 
another eligible unit that is now within range as the second targetΟΔβ²§β³Δ?
A: Yes.
DISCIPLES OF TZEENTCH, BATTLE FORMATIONS
Q: Can the target of ΟΔβ²§Υ€Sorcerers of WarΟΔβ²§β³Δ attempt to cast a spell in the 
combat phase if that wizard already attempted to cast in the hero 
phase of the same turn?
A: Yes.
FYRESLAYERS, AURIC RUNESON ON MAGMADROTH
Q: Can more than one Scourge of Ghyran Auric Runeson on 
Magmadroth pick the same Auric Runefather on Magmadroth as 
the target of their ΟΔβ²§Υ€Fire, Blood and VengeanceΟΔβ²§β³Δ?
A: Yes. That Runefather had a big family.
IRONJAWZ, BATTLE FORMATIONS
Q: When using the ΟΔβ²§Υ€Mightiest DestroyersΟΔβ²§β³Δ ability, can I pick a second 
target if there is a Megaboss on Maw-Krusha on the battlefield?
A: No. The ability specifies the unit name, so only a Megaboss unit 
is eligible.
KRULEBOYZ, KILLABOSS ON GREAT GNASHTOOF
Q: Can I use the ΟΔβ²§Υ€Drag ΟΔβ²§β³ΔEm OutΟΔβ²§β³Δ ability to remove an enemy 
Infantry Hero from garrisoning a faction terrain feature?
A: Yes.
SKAVEN, GREY SEER ON SCREAMING BELL
Q: If I successfully cast ΟΔβ²§Υ€Gnaw Through RealityΟΔβ²§β³Δ on a Scourge of 
Ghyran Grey Seer on Screaming Bell, could I then use ΟΔβ²§Υ€Tunnels 
Through RealityΟΔβ²§β³Δ to remove that unit and set it up wholly within 6" 
of another friendly Gnawhole?
A: No. ΟΔβ²§Υ€Gnaw Through RealityΟΔβ²§β³Δ does not allow the Grey Seer on 
Screaming Bell to use abilities from the Gnawhole warscroll, only 
to be treated as a Gnawhole when using those abilities.
SONS OF BEHEMAT
Q: If a Scourge of Ghyran Kraken-eater Mega-Gargant is in reserve 
beneath the waves at the start of battle round 4, is it destroyed or 
are 6 damage points allocated to it?
A: It is destroyed.

Declare: If the friendly Ushoran is not in combat and has not

Knights units within his combat range that are not in 
combat and have not used a Run or Retreat ability this turn 
to be his royal retinue. Then, make a charge roll of 2D6.
