value of the charge roll. He can move through the combat 
ranges of enemy units and must end that move within Ο²¬Ϋ" of a 
visible enemy unit. If he does so, he has charged. If he charged, 
then after this ability has been resolved, each unit in his royal 
retinue can immediately use the ΟΔβ²§Υ€ChargeΟΔβ²§β³Δ ability even if it is 
not your charge phase and you can use the charge roll from 
this ability instead of making a charge roll when it does so, 
but it must end the charge move within 3" of the friendly 
Ushoran.

Core, Move, Charge

## Page 43

Ο²¬°
RULES UPDATES
FEBRUARY 2026
BATTLETOME: GLOOMSPITE GITZ
Q: When using the Lunar Squigs effect of ΟΔβ²§Υ€Under the Light of the 
Bad MoonΟΔβ²§β³Δ or the ΟΔβ²§Υ€The Moon and the LoonΟΔβ²§β³Δ ability to use a value 
of 4 for the Move characteristic of a friendly Squig unit, does the 
4 replace the entire Move characteristic of the unit with a 4, or just 
the dice roll?
A: It replaces the dice roll. For example, if a unit of Squig Hoppers 
(with a Move characteristic of D6+8") were affected by one of 
these abilities, it could use a value of 4 instead of rolling the D6, 
resulting in a Move characteristic of 12" (4+8").
Q: Can the attacker choose to resolve their attacks one at a time for 
the Doom Diver Catapult? If so, how does the Rend reduction apply?
A: Yes. As described in the rules for the attack sequence (17.0), 
attacks are resolved one at a time unless you choose to use fast dice 
rolling. HereΟΔβ²§β³Δs an example attack sequence for the Doom Diver 
Catapult:
1. The Gloomspite Gitz player successfully hits and wounds for the 
first attack, then their opponent makes a save roll, subtracting 
the Rend (3) of the weapon from the roll.
2. The Gloomspite Gitz player fails the hit roll for the second 
attack, re-rolls it to be successful and successfully wounds. Then, 
their opponent makes a save roll subtracting the now-reduced 
Rend (2) from the roll.
3. The third attack fails to hit and fails again after the re-roll. The 
Rend is now reduced to 1.
4. The fourth attacks successfully hits and wounds. The opponent 
of the Gloomspite Gitz player subtracts the Rend (1) from their 
save roll.
FREQUENTLY ASKED QUESTIONS
In this series of questions and answers, the rules-writing team respond to playersΟΔβ²§β³Δ queries and explain how the rules are intended to be used.
43

Q: When using ΟΔβ²§Υ€Harness Daemonic PowerΟΔβ²§β³Δ, when daemonic power 
points are removed from units, are they retained to be reallocated to 
friendly units or are they lost?
A: They are lost.

and the TaarΟΔβ²§β³Δs Grand Forgehost spell ΟΔβ²§Υ€Reinforce DaemonsteelΟΔβ²§β³Δ, both 
of which allow a unit to ignore the first damage point allocated to it 
in a phase, do those abilities allow that unit to ignore just the first 
damage point allocated to it or does that unit ignore the first and 
second damage points allocated to it?
A: Only the first damage point would be ignored.

Q: Can an Arkanaut Frigate use the ΟΔβ²§Υ€Assault BoatΟΔβ²§β³Δ ability if it used 
a Run ability in the same turn but was picked as a target of Brokk 
GrungssonΟΔβ²§β³Δs ΟΔβ²§Υ€Command the FleetΟΔβ²§β³Δ ability earlier in the same turn?
A: Yes.
Q: Can I use the ΟΔβ²§Υ€Assault BoatΟΔβ²§β³Δ ability in combination with 
ΟΔβ²§Υ€Counter-chargeΟΔβ²§β³Δ?
A: Yes.
Q: Can I picked a reinforced unit of Scourge of Ghyran Grundstok 
Gunhaulers to be the target of an Arkanaut AdmiralΟΔβ²§β³Δs ΟΔβ²§Υ€Bring Every 
Gun to Bear!ΟΔβ²§β³Δ ability? If so, do all models in the unit gain the benefit 
of the effect or would only 1 model in the unit?
A: Yes, and all models in the unit would gain the benefit of 
theΟ²¬†effect.
Q: If I use the ΟΔβ²§Υ€Blazebeard and Sons Bamboozling FlakgunΟΔβ²§β³Δ artefact 
of power to remove a dice from my opponentΟΔβ²§β³Δs charge roll, and then 
my opponent uses the ΟΔβ²§Υ€Forward to VictoryΟΔβ²§β³Δ command, how many 
dice does my opponent re-roll for that charge roll?
A: They would roll the number of dice left in the charge roll after 
1 dice has been removed. For example, if the original charge 
roll was made with 2 dice and 1 of those dice was removed by 
the Blazebeard and Sons Bamboozling Flakgun, your opponent 
would re-roll that 1 remaining dice to determine their re-rolled 
chargeΟ²¬†roll.

Q: When using the ΟΔβ²§Υ€Notorious BossesΟΔβ²§β³Δ ability, can I give a target a 
heroic trait that another unit already has?
A: No.
Q: Can each part of the Foot of Gork use the ΟΔβ²§Υ€Wandering 
DestructionΟΔβ²§β³Δ ability each turn?
A: No. This is a single manifestation with multiple parts, so it can 
only use that ability once in each turn.
Q: In the ΟΔβ²§Υ€Ironjawz BigmobΟΔβ²§β³Δ Spearhead army, are the Brute Ragerz 
set up during the deployment phase?
A: Yes. Only the Brutes are not set up on the battlefield during the 
deployment phase. The Brute Ragerz are a different unit type, so 
are set up normally.

Q: The prayer ΟΔβ²§Υ€Get ΟΔβ²§β³ΔEm, Gork!ΟΔβ²§β³Δ allows a terrain feature to become 
trampled by Gork. If an enemy unit starts and ends a move within 
6" of that terrain feature, would you roll for damage twice? If it 
ended a move within 6" of two different terrain features that were 
trampled by Gork, would you roll for damage for each such terrain 
feature?
A: Yes to both questions.
Q: Is damage inflicted on a unit that finished a move within 6" of 
a trampled by Gork terrain feature damage inflicted by a Prayer 
ability?
A: Yes.

The following rules updates correct errors in order to clarify ambiguities and/or avoid unintended interactions.
Q: If I pick a friendly Stormfiends unit as the target of the 
ΟΔβ²§Υ€Filth-crustΟΔβ²§β³Δ prayer with a chanting roll of 8+, how would the 
CritΟ²¬†(Mortal) weapon ability interact with the ΟΔβ²§Υ€Shock GauntletsΟΔβ²§β³Δ 
ability?
A: The Crit (Mortal) weapon ability would inflict mortal damage 
on the target unit equal to the Damage characteristic and the 
attack sequence would end, so ΟΔβ²§Υ€Shock GauntletsΟΔβ²§β³Δ would have no 
further effect.
Q: If an enemy unit is affected by the ΟΔβ²§Υ€Gaze of the GnawΟΔβ²§β³Δ ability and 
must attack Vizzik Skour, can the models in that unit that are not 
within Vizzik SkourΟΔβ²§β³Δs combat range after piling in attack?
A: No.
Q: If a Warp-Grinder picks another Warp-Grinder unit to be set up 
in the tunnels below using the ΟΔβ²§Υ€Tunnel SkulkersΟΔβ²§β³Δ ability, can both of 
those units use the ΟΔβ²§Υ€Warp-AmbushΟΔβ²§β³Δ ability to attempt to set up the 
units on the battlefield?
A: Yes.
Q: When using the Grey SeerΟΔβ²§β³Δs ΟΔβ²§Υ€Warpstone ShardsΟΔβ²§β³Δ ability, if the 3D6 
casting roll includes 2 or more rolls of 1 (before removing one of the 
dice), is that spell miscast?
A: Yes.
Q: If a flying unit passes across a Gnawhole, does it ignore the effect 
of the ΟΔβ²§Υ€Volatile GroundΟΔβ²§β³Δ ability since the ΟΔβ²§Υ€FlyΟΔβ²§β³Δ ability means that 
units ignore terrain features while moving?
A: No. The unit still counts as having passed across the Gnawhole, 
so the effect applies.

## Page 55

Ο²¬°
FEBRUARY 2026
RULES UPDATES
BATTLETOME: SLAVES TO DARKNESS
Q: When using the ΟΔβ²§Υ€Dark ApotheosisΟΔβ²§β³Δ ability to set up a Daemon 
Prince, does that Daemon Prince have to be included on my army 
roster and do I need to spend points on it?
A: No to both.
Q: If Archaon the Everchosen uses the ΟΔβ²§Υ€Eye of SheerianΟΔβ²§β³Δ ability and, 
as a result, the Slaves to Darkness player has to take the first turn 
in a battle round after taking the second turn in the previous battle 
round (i.e., they end up taking a ΟΔβ²§Υ€double turnΟΔβ²§β³Δ), does the Slaves to 
Darkness player suffer the penalties for taking a ΟΔβ²§Υ€double turnΟΔβ²§β³Δ, for 
instance not being able to pick a battle tactic or their opponent 
counting as the underdog for the rest of the battle?
A: No. The penalties for taking a double turn only apply if you 
ΟΔβ²§Υ€choseΟΔβ²§β³Δ to go first in the current battle round.
Q: Can a unit use the ΟΔβ²§Υ€Pledge to ChaosΟΔβ²§β³Δ ability while it is not on the 
battlefield, e.g. if it is set up in reserve in a Silver Tower?
A: No.
Q: Can a Gaunt Summoner use the ΟΔβ²§Υ€Minions of the Silver TowerΟΔβ²§β³Δ 
ability more than once to set up multiple units in a Silver Tower?
A: No, as stated in Core Rules, 5.3.
Q: Can I include more than 1 Ensorcelled Banner in my Slaves to 
Darkness army?
A: No. Ensorcelled Banners are enhancements, and as stated in 
Army Composition, 4.1, you can only take 1 enhancement from 
each enhancement table in your faction rules.
Q: Can Regiments of Renown be included in a Legion of the First 
Prince army?
A: Yes.
Q: Can a Daemon Prince from the Godmarked Ascendant Regiment 
of Renown be given enhancements?
A: Yes. In Army Composition 3.5 it states that a unit cannot use 
faction rules (including enhancements) unless they have a keyword 
that matches up with the factionΟΔβ²§β³Δs name. Since the Godmarked 
Ascendant Regiment of Renown adds such a keyword, that unit is 
eligible to be given enhancements.
FREQUENTLY ASKED QUESTIONS
In this series of questions and answers, the rules-writing team respond to playersΟΔβ²§β³Δ queries and explain how the rules are intended to be used.
55

Q: Can I use the ΟΔβ²§Υ€Ruination ChamberΟΔβ²§β³Δ ability to react to enemy 
abilities that target a friendly unit with that ability if that ability 
takes place outside of the regular turn sequence (e.g. a Deployment

Q: If I use the ΟΔβ²§Υ€Ruination ChamberΟΔβ²§β³Δ ability to react to an ability 
that targets multiple units and/or has multiple effects (e.g. ΟΔβ²§Υ€Power 
ThroughΟΔβ²§β³Δ), and the roll is successful, should my opponent still 
resolve the parts of the ability that affect other units?
A: Yes. The ΟΔβ²§Υ€Ruination ChamberΟΔβ²§β³Δ ability only allows you to ignore 
the effect of that ability on that unit; it does not stop the effect 
from being resolved for other units.

## Page 60

FEBRUARY 2026
PATH TO GLORY: RAVAGED COAST
RULES UPDATES
Q: When building my Path to Glory roster, I can include regiments 
with units from different factions that are from the same grand 
alliance as my general. When I do so, can I use the battle traits or 
enhancements from that other faction?
A: No, you can only use battle traits and enhancements from the 
faction that you picked for your Path to Glory army or from the 
universal enhancements included in Path to Glory: Ravaged Coast.
Q: Can I add spells or prayers from a manifestation lore to my 
Arcane Tome when first creating my army roster?
A: No.
Q: Can I add spells from one of the universal manifestation lores 
(such as Forbidden Power) to my Arcane Tome?
A: Yes.
Q: Do you have to include faction terrain (e.g. Gnawholes or 
awakened Wyldwoods) as part of your roster to use abilities that 
place them on the battlefield (i.e. ΟΔβ²§Υ€Splinters of the VermindoomΟΔβ²§β³Δ or 
ΟΔβ²§Υ€TreesongΟΔβ²§β³Δ)?
A: No.
Q: Do battle formations, enhancements and lores cost points?
A: No.
Q: Does the ΟΔβ²§Υ€HeadshotΟΔβ²§β³Δ ability of the Vanguard-Raptors with 
Longstrike Crossbows allow that unit to ignore the effect of the 
ΟΔβ²§Υ€Protect at all CostsΟΔβ²§β³Δ ability on the Twisted by Rage battleplan?
A: No.
Q: Can an army use a battle trait (such as ΟΔβ²§Υ€TreesongΟΔβ²§β³Δ or ΟΔβ²§Υ€Splinters 
of the VermindoomΟΔβ²§β³Δ) or an ability on a warscroll of a unit in their 
army during a battle to set up a faction terrain feature that is not on 
their roster?
A: Yes.

## Page 3

Tis download explains how to play games of Warhammer Age of Sigmar 
with your collection of Citadel Miniatures. It is divided into 4 sections:
THE RULES: 
DIGITAL DOWNLOAD
CORE RULES (pg 5-27)
Te Core Rules explain the basic concepts of the game, including how your 
units move, shoot, charge and Ο·¬§¬¹ght. Tese rules are used in all games of 
Warhammer Age of Sigmar.
ADVANCED RULES (pg 28-40)
Te Advanced Rules explain how to use special unit types like Wizards and 
Priests, how to Ο·¬§¬¹ll your army roster, how to score battle tactics, and more. 
Tis section also covers expanded rules for terrain.
GLOSSARY (pg 59-64)
Te Glossary is an alphabetised list of important rules terms along with 
summarised deΟ·¬§¬¹nitions. It is a handy reference tool if you need a quick 
reminder of what a particular rule does.
CITADEL TERRIN LIST (pg 65-66)
Te Citadel Terrain List shows you which terrain types apply to which sets 
of Citadel Terrain as well as clarifying how many scenery pieces each terrain 
feature comprises.
Te rules are numbered to make it easy to look up a rule referenced 
elsewhere. For example, if a rule told you to see ΟΔβ²§Υ€Core Rules, 1.0ΟΔβ²§β³Δ, you 
would look up the rule numbered 1.0 in the Core Rules, which happens 
to be ΟΔβ²§Υ€Core ConceptsΟΔβ²§β³Δ.

## Page 4

While some battlepacks focus on balance and competitive play, and others put the emphasis on the story, every 
Warhammer Age of Sigmar battle is an awe-inspiring spectacle with fully painted armies and terrain.
BATTLEPACKS 
While the rules explain how to play Warhammer Age of Sigmar, 
battlepacks provide a unique experience for each of your games.
Te battlepack you choose will tell you which Advanced Rules to use, and it 
may include additional special rules. In addition, each battlepack contains a 
collection of scenarios called battleplans that explain things like where the 
armies deploy and how to win the battle. WeΟΔβ²§β³Δd recommend reading through 
the Core Rules Ο·¬§¬¹rst to understand the mechanics of the game and then 
picking a battlepack to play.
SPEARHEAD: FIREΟ²¬†AND JADE (pg 43-58)
Spearhead is the simplest way to play Warhammer Age of Sigmar, yet it 
has amazing tactical depth and re-playability. Te armies used in games 
of Spearhead are pre-made, so you can walk straight into your nearest 
Warhammer store and grab a Spearhead army oΟ·¬§β²§ the shelf! 
Te Spearhead battlepack Fire and Jade includes rules for you and your 
opponent to Ο·¬§¬¹ght battles in the lush, overgrown jungles of Ghyran and on the 
Ο·¬§¬¹ery lava plains of Aqshy.
MATCHED PLAY: BORDER WAR (pg 41-42)
Matched Play is a gaming mode designed for balance and equal victory 
conditions for both players. Te Border War battlepack (a truncated version 
of the First Blood battlepack in the Core Book) introduces you to Matched 
Play, providing conditions that give no particular advantage to either side in 
order to see which player is the canniest commander.
4
CORE RULES

## Page 5

CORE RULES
1.0 CORE CONCEPTS
Battles in Warhammer Age of Sigmar are fought on a surface that is referred 
to as the battleΟ·¬§¬¹ eld. Each battle uses a scenario called a battleplan, which
will explain how to set up the battleΟ·¬§¬¹ eld and how to determine the winner.
Each player is the commander of an army. Each army belongs to a
y.y
faction
and is made up of groups of models called units. Models and units in your
army are referred to as friendly models and units, and models and units in 
y y
your opponentΟΔβ²§β³Δs army are referred to as enemy models and units.
y y
2.0 TOOLS OF WAR
To Ο·¬§¬¹ ght a battle, you will need:
ΟΔβ²§¬Δ A tape measure  ΟΔβ²§¬Δ  A surface to play on (the battleΟ·¬§¬¹ eld)
ΟΔβ²§¬Δ Some dice 
ΟΔβ²§¬Δ 2 armies of Citadel Miniatures
2.1 MEASURING DISTANCES
Distances in Warhammer Age of Sigmar are measured
in inches ("), between the closest points on the bases 
of the models youΟΔβ²§β³Δre measuring to and from. You 
can measure distances whenever you wish. When 
measuring the distance between units, always
measure the distance between the closest 
points on the bases of the closest 
models in each unit. If a model does
not have a base, measure to and from
the closest point on the model instead.
2.1.1 WITHIN AND WHOLLY WITHIN
A model is within a certain distance of something (e.g. another unit, a
territory or an objective) if any part of its base is within that distance.
A model is wholly within a certain distance of something if every part
of its base is within that distance.
A unit is within a certain distance of something if any part of the 
base of any model in the unit is within that distance. A unit is wholly 
within a certain distance of something if every part of the bases of all 
of the models in the unit is within that distance.
2.2 DICE
Warhammer Age of Sigmar uses six-sided dice, of en abbreviated to D6.
ΟΔβ²§¬Δ  Some rules refer to ΟΔβ²§Υ€2D6ΟΔβ²§β³Δ, ΟΔβ²§Υ€3D6ΟΔβ²§β³Δ and so on ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ in such 
cases, roll a number of dice equal to the number
before ΟΔβ²§Υ€D6ΟΔβ²§β³Δ and add the results together.
ΟΔβ²§¬Δ  A roll of ΟΔβ²§Υ€2+ΟΔβ²§β³Δ means a roll of 2 or more, a roll of ΟΔβ²§Υ€3+ΟΔβ²§β³Δ 
means a roll of 3 or more, and so on.
ΟΔβ²§¬Δ  If a rule requires you to roll a D3, roll a dice and 
halve the total, rounding up. For D3 rolls, a roll of ΟΔβ²§Υ€2+ΟΔβ²§β³Δ means a result of 
2 or more af er halving the total and rounding up.
ΟΔβ²§¬Δ  EΟ·¬§β²§ ects that allow you to modify a dice roll are called modiΟ·¬§¬¹ ers.
ΟΔβ²§¬Δ  Some rules allow you to re-roll a dice roll, which means you get to roll 
some or all of the dice again. You cannot re-roll a dice more than once,
and re-rolls happen before modiΟ·¬§¬¹ ers to the roll (if any) are applied.
ΟΔβ²§¬Δ  If you are instructed to roll oΟ·¬§β²§ , each player should roll a dice.

3.0 FACTIONS
Each army belongs to a faction, e.g. Stormcast Eternals or Skaven.
Each faction has a set of faction rules, which include battle traits,
battleΟ²¬†formations, enhancements and lores. T ese can be found in various 
publications but most commonly in the factionΟΔβ²§β³Δs battletome.

T e vast majority of things that units can do in Warhammer Age of Sigmar 
are called abilities. T is is an example of an ability:

5.1 KEYWORDS
Some abilities have one or more keywords listed at the bottom of the
ability on their keywords bar, e.g. the ΟΔβ²§Υ€ShootΟΔβ²§β³Δ ability shown above has the
Core, Attack and Shoot keywords. Units also have a keywords bar on 
their warscroll.
Keywords let you know which abilities can be used or which units can be 
picked as targets for an ability. For example, the ΟΔβ²§Υ€ChargeΟΔβ²§β³Δ ability can only 
be used by a unit if it did not use an ability with the Run or Retreat
keyword earlier in the turn. T e singular and plural forms of a keyword 
are synonymous for rules purposes.

5.2 USING ABILITIES
T e timing of an ability tells you when it can be used. When using an
g g
ability, follow these steps:
1. Declare the Ability:y  Tell your opponent which ability is being used. If 
the ability has Declare instructions, resolve them at this step.
2.  Use Reactions: Starting with the active player (see 12.0), the players
alternate using any abilities with an appropriate Reaction timing.
3.  Resolve the EΟ·¬§β²§ ect: Follow the instructions in the EΟ·¬§β²§ ect part of 
the ability.
5.3 THE RULES OF ONE
ΟΔβ²§¬Δ  A unit cannot use more than 1 Core ability per phase (see 14.0).
ΟΔβ²§¬Δ  A unit cannot use the same ability more than once per phase, unless
y y
speciΟ·¬§¬¹ ed otherwise.
ΟΔβ²§¬Δ  A unit cannot be aΟ·¬§β²§ ected by the same passive ability more than once 
y y
at the same time. For example, if a unit is within range of two diΟ·¬§β²§ erent
terrain features that have the ΟΔβ²§Υ€CoverΟΔβ²§β³Δ passive ability, the eΟ·¬§β²§ ect only 
applies to it once.
5.4 PASSIVE ABILITIES
Abilities that have the Passive timing are called passive abilities. Passive 
abilities are not declared. T e eΟ·¬§β²§ ects of passive abilities always apply if the
conditions of the ability are met, and they must be applied if it is possible 
to do so.

6.0 VISIBILITY
A target model is visible to another model (which weΟΔβ²§β³Δll call the ΟΔβ²§Υ€observing 
modelΟΔβ²§β³Δ) if you can draw a straight line through the air (whether horizontal, 
diagonal or vertical) from any point on the observing model to any point
on the target model that does not intersect any objects except for other
models in the observing modelΟΔβ²§β³Δs unit. A target unit is visible to an observing 
unit if at least 1 model in the target unit is visible to at least 1 model in the 
observing unit. A model is always considered to be visible to itself.
Ο·¬¹¬¥If any part of another 
model can be seen by an 
observing model, both 
the target model and its 
unit are visible to that 
observing model.

donΟΔβ²§β³Δt block the visibility of other models in their unit ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ it is assumed that they 
Ο·¬§¬¹ ght in such a way as to not obstruct one anotherΟΔβ²§β³Δs attacks.

7.0 COMBAT R NGE
Each model has a combat range that extends 3" horizontally from its base
and any distance vertically from that circle to form a cylinder. T e combat
range of a unit extends 3" horizontally and any distance vertically from every 
model in that unit. Units from opposing armies that are within each otherΟΔβ²§β³Δs
combat range and that are visible to each other are inΟ²¬†combat with each
other. When a unit that is not in combat enters the combat range of a visible
enemy unit, it movesΟ²¬†into combat.
A modelΟΔβ²§β³Δs combat range extends 3" horizontally 
outwards from it in all directions and an inΟ·¬§¬¹ nite distance
vertically. Note that this is measured from the edge of its
base, not the centre.
Ο·¬¹¬¥A unitΟΔβ²§β³Δs combat range 
extends 3" out from every 
model in that unit.
Ο·¬¹¬¥If any enemy models are 
within a unitΟΔβ²§β³Δs combat 
range and visible to it, that 
unit is in combat.
A unitΟΔβ²§β³Δs combat range extends 3" horizontally and any 
distance vertically from all models in the unit. As you can
see, this forms a sort of ΟΔβ²§Υ€cloudΟΔβ²§β³Δ.
Even though both Rat Ogors are within the combat range 
of the Liberator, the Rat Ogor behind the wall is not 
visible to the Liberator and is therefore not in combat.
A unit is in combat with a visible enemy unit if any of its
nit if 
models are within the combat range of that enemy unit. 
Even though 1 Rat Ogor is not within the LiberatorsΟΔβ²§β³Δ 
combat range, its unit is still in combat.

9.0 BATTLEPLANS
Before any battle, youΟΔβ²§β³Δll need to pick a battleplan. A variety of battleplans are 
available in each battlepack, and additional battleplans are available in other
publications. Each battleplan is a scenario to play ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ it outlines territories,
objectives and victory conditions, in addition to any special rules that apply 
to that battle.

battleΟ·¬§¬¹ eld size based on the size of game you are playing.

10.0 THE DEPLOYMENT PHASE
Once the battleΟ·¬§¬¹ eld is ready, the players must deploy their armies. T is is
y y
done in a special phase called the deployment phase. T e deployment phase 
happens once per battle, before the start of the Ο·¬§¬¹ rst battle round (see 12.0).
STEP 1 ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ DEPLOY FACTION TERR IN FEATURES
Some factions have faction terrain features, which have their own 
warscrolls and are set up using Deploy Terrain abilities. T e player 
who begins deployment must use Deploy Terrain abilities Ο·¬§¬¹ rst, 
followed by their opponent.
STEP 2 ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ DEPLOY ARMIES
Unless otherwise speciΟ·¬§¬¹ ed in the battleplan, the players alternate using 
Deploy abilities to deploy their units, starting with the player who 
begins deployment. Once one player has no more Deploy abilities to 
use, their opponent must continue to use Deploy abilities until they 
also have no more to use.
STEP 3 ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ USE DEPLOYMENT PHASE ABILITIES
Af er both players have Ο·¬§¬¹ nished deploying their units, the player who 
begins deployment can use any Ο²¬†DeploymentΟ²¬†PhaseΟ²¬† abilities that are not
Deploy or Deploy Terrain abilities, in the order of their choosing, 
then their opponent can do the same.
10.1 UNIVERSAL DEPLOYMENT PHASE ABILITIES
DEPLOY FACTION TERR IN: T e army has
taken to the Ο·¬§¬¹ eld surrounding an important and 
powerful landmark.

that has not been deployed to be the target.

wholly within friendly territory, more than 3" from
all objectives and other terrain features. Af er you 
have done so, it has been deployed.

their retinue into battle.

been deployed. You cannot pick units that are
not in that regiment as the target of any of those 
Deploy abilities.

the Ο·¬§¬¹ eld.

11.0 BATTLE SEQUENCE
Each battle lasts a number of battle rounds, speciΟ·¬§¬¹ ed in the battleplan.
During each battle round, each player will take a turn. Each turn is broken 
down into multiple phases (see 13.0).
12.0 START OF BATTLE ROUND
At the start of each battle round, follow these steps:
1.  Determine the Active Player:
y
 If it is the Ο·¬§¬¹ rst battle round, the player who
Ο·¬§¬¹ nished setting up their army Ο·¬§¬¹ rst decides who will take the Ο·¬§¬¹ rst turn
(unless otherwise speciΟ·¬§¬¹ ed in the battlepack or battleplan).
If it is not the Ο·¬§¬¹ rst battle round, the players make a roll-oΟ·¬§β²§  called the 
priorityΟ²¬†roll and the winner decides who will take the Ο·¬§¬¹ rst turn. If the 
roll-oΟ·¬§β²§  is a tie, the player who took the Ο·¬§¬¹ rst turn in the previous battle
round decides who will take the Ο·¬§¬¹ rst turn in the current battle round.
When it is a playerΟΔβ²§β³Δs turn, regardless of whether they take the Ο·¬§¬¹ rst or
second turn, they are referred to as the active player.
2.  Determine the Underdog:g Whichever player has the fewest victory points 
is the underdog for the battle round. If the players are tied (e.g. in the Ο·¬§¬¹ rst
g
battle round), then there is no underdog, unless otherwise speciΟ·¬§¬¹ ed.
3. Start of Battle Round Abilities: T e active player can use 
any Ο²¬†StartΟ²¬†ofΟ²¬†BattleΟ²¬†RoundΟ²¬† abilities Ο·¬§¬¹ rst, then their opponent can do
the same.
13.0 TURN PHASES
Each playerΟΔβ²§β³Δs turn is broken down into 7 phases, as shown on the lef . In
each phase, the active player can use any abilities with the ΟΔβ²§Υ€YourΟ²¬†(ΟΔβ²§¬Π)Ο²¬†PhaseΟΔβ²§β³Δ
or ΟΔβ²§Υ€Any (ΟΔβ²§¬Π) Phase
ΟΔβ²§Υ€A
ΟΔβ²§Υ€
ΟΔβ²§β³Δ timing in the order of their choosing. For example, 
in the Movement Phase, the active player could use abilities with
the Ο²¬†YourΟ²¬†MovementΟ²¬†PhaseΟ²¬† or Ο²¬†AnyΟ²¬†MovementΟ²¬†PhaseΟ²¬† timing.
Once the active player has Ο·¬§¬¹ nished using abilities, their opponent can then 
use any abilities with an ΟΔβ²§Υ€Enemy (ΟΔβ²§¬Π) PhaseΟΔβ²§β³Δ or ΟΔβ²§Υ€Any (ΟΔβ²§¬Π) Phase
ΟΔβ²§Υ€A
ΟΔβ²§Υ€
ΟΔβ²§β³Δ timing, in 
the order of their choosing.
13.1 FIGHT ABILITIES
Abilities with the Fight keyword follow a diΟ·¬§β²§ erent sequence to other 
abilities. When the players are using  CombatΟ²¬†Phase  abilities as described 
in 13.0, they cannot use Fight abilities.
Af er the players have Ο·¬§¬¹ nished using  CombatΟ²¬†Phase abilities that are not
Fight abilities, they must alternate picking 1 eligible unit to use a Fight
ability, starting with the active player. Each unit in combat must use a 
Fight ability if it is able to.
Once a player has no more units that are eligible to use a Fight ability, 
the other player continues to pick units that are eligible to use a Fight
ability, one af er another, until there are no more units that are eligible to 
use a Fight ability.

MOVEMENT
15.0 MOVEMENT 
Some abilities allow a unit to move. When a unit moves, each model in that
unit can move, one at a time, in the order chosen by that unitΟΔβ²§β³Δs commander.
When a model moves, it can pivot and change direction as of en as you like, 
but no part of that modelΟΔβ²§β³Δs base can move a greater total distance than the
maximum allowed by the ability used. Models cannot move beyond the edge
of the battleΟ·¬§¬¹ eld or through other models, and they can never end a move
standing on top of another model (the base counts as part of the model).
A modelΟΔβ²§β³Δs base is assumed to remain parallel with the battleΟ·¬§¬¹ eld as it moves.
If a model ends its move on an uneven surface, it must be able to stand up by 
itself with its base as close to parallel with the battleΟ·¬§¬¹ eld as possible (in other
words, models cannot lie Ο·¬§βÄö at on the battleΟ·¬§¬¹ eld or lean against terrain to stop 
them from falling over).
Ο·¬¹¬¥When moving a unit, move

15.1 COHERENCY
Any time a unit is set up or ends a move, it must be in a single group. A
unit is considered to be in a coherent group if each model in that unit is 
within coherency range, measured horizontally, of at least 1 other model
in that unit (ignore diΟ·¬§β²§ erences in height between the two models).
For the majority of units, coherency range is Ο²¬Ϋ", though some units 
(particularly those with large models with overhanging parts) have a
longer coherency range noted on their warscroll for ease of play. While
there are 7 or more models in a unit, that unit is considered to be in a
coherent group if each model in that unit is within coherency range of 
atΟ²¬†least 2 other models in that unit.
If it is not possible for a unit to end a move in a single coherent group, that
move cannot be made.
T is Grey Seer is using the ΟΔβ²§Υ€Normal MoveΟΔβ²§β³Δ ability to change its position on
the battleΟ·¬§¬¹ eld. No part of that move can be within the combat range of the
Liberators, so the Grey Seer has to stay outside the area marked in blue.

## Page 16

As a model moves across terrain, its base is assumed to stay in contact 
with the terrain feature and parallel to the battleΟ·¬§¬¹ eld. Models can climb
up or down terrain. T ey can jump down too, but this ends their move.
15.2 MOVING ACROSS TERR IN
When a model moves, it can move over terrain features but not through
them. A model can be moved over terrain features that are 1" or less 
in height as if they were not there. A model can be moved vertically in
order to climb up or down any terrain features that are taller than 1",
counting the vertical distance as part of its move. Models cannot end a
move mid-climb.
15.2.1 JUMPING DOWN
When a model that is on a terrain feature moves, it can jump down
from the edge of the terrain feature to land on a lower part of the same 
or a diΟ·¬§β²§ erent terrain feature or to land on the battleΟ·¬§¬¹ eld. To do so, 
you must trace the path of the modelΟΔβ²§β³Δs move to the edge of the terrain 
feature. T en, that model can jump down any distance, but doing so
immediately ends their move. Models cannot end any kind of move 
mid-jump ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ if it is not possible to end the move as a result, that move 
cannot be made.

that are inaccessible to other troops.

and the combat ranges of enemy units. It cannot end its move in 
combat unless speciΟ·¬§¬¹ ed in the ability that allowed it to move. Ignore 
any vertical distance moved for this unit.

Some abilities, such as Fight abilities, allow a unit to make a short move
called a pile-in move to get into a better position for combat. To do so:
If your unit is in combat:
y

## Page 17

16.0 PICKING TARGETS
When declaring an Attack ability for a unit, you must pick the target 
unit(s) for its attacks. T e number of attacks each model can make is equal to 
the Attacks characteristic of the weapons it is using. In most cases, models 
attack with every weapon on their warscroll (melee weapons for combat
attacks, ranged weapons for shooting attacks), though some warscrolls
specify that certain models in the unit are armed with special weapons or 
that the unit must pick between multiple weapons when it attacks.
If the unit is in combat, it can only target units that are in combat with it. If 
a model has more than one attack, you can split the attacks between eligible
targets as you wish.
ΟΔβ²§¬Δ Combat attacks are made with melee weapons. T e target unit(s) must
be within the combat range of the attacking model and visible to it. T e 
model must attack with all of the melee weapons it is armed with.
ΟΔβ²§¬Δ Shooting attacks
g
 are made with ranged weapons. T e target unit(s) must
be within a distance equal to the Range characteristic of the weapon being
used and visible to the attacking model. Models cannot make shooting
attacks if their unit is in combat, unless otherwise speciΟ·¬§¬¹ ed (see 20.0
Weapon Abilities).
17.0 THE ATTACK SEQUENCE
Resolve steps 1-4 below for each attack made against a target unit, one attack 
at a time. If you picked more than one target unit for the Attack ability,
resolve all the attacks made against one unit before moving on to the next, in 
an order of your choosing. Once you are familiar with the attack sequence, 
see ΟΔβ²§Υ€Fast Dice RollingΟΔβ²§β³Δ (see 17.3) if you want to speed up play.
1.  Hit Roll: Roll a dice. If the roll equals or exceeds the attacking weaponΟΔβ²§β³Δs 
Hit characteristic, the attack scores a successful hit: move on to the next
step. If not, the attack fails and the attack sequence ends. UnmodiΟ·¬§¬¹ ed hit 
rolls of 1 always fail. If an unmodiΟ·¬§¬¹ ed hit roll for an attack made with a
weapon is a 6, that attack is a critical hit.
2.  Wound Roll: Roll a dice. If the roll equals or exceeds the attacking 
weaponΟΔβ²§β³Δs Wound characteristic, the attack successfully wounds: move
on to the next step. If not, the attack fails and the attack sequence ends.
UnmodiΟ·¬§¬¹ ed wound rolls of 1 always fail.
3.  Save Roll: T e commander of the target unit rolls a dice, subtracting the 
attacking weaponΟΔβ²§β³Δs Rend characteristic from the roll. UnmodiΟ·¬§¬¹ ed save 
rolls of 1 always fail. If the roll equals or exceeds the Save characteristic of 
the target unit, the attack fails and the attack sequence ends. If not, it is a 
successful attack: move on to the next step.
4.  Determine Damage:
g
T e attack inΟ·¬§βÄö icts a number of damageΟ²¬†points
on the unit equal to the Damage characteristic of the weapon. InΟ·¬§βÄö icted 
damage points are added to a temporary damage pool for the target unit.
Af er completing these steps for all of the attacks made as part of that 
Attack ability, the attacks for that ability are resolved and you can move on 
to the damage sequence (see 18.0).

## Page 18

17.1 ATTACK MODIFIERS AND CAPS
ModiΟ·¬§¬¹ ers to hit rolls, wound rolls and save rolls are capped to prevent
overwhelming combinations of abilities. When making a hit roll or a
wound roll, add up all positive and negative modiΟ·¬§¬¹ ers that apply to the
roll, then cap the result at a maximum of +1 (if positive) or a minimum of 
-1 (if negative).
When making a save roll, add up all the positive and negative modiΟ·¬§¬¹ ers
that apply to the roll, then cap the result at a maximum of +1. Note that,
unlike hit rolls and wound rolls, there is no cap on the amount that can 
be subtracted from save rolls.
17.2 MORTAL DAMAGE
Some abilities inΟ·¬§βÄö ict mortal damage. If an ability inΟ·¬§βÄö icts mortal damage
on a unit, add that number of damage points to the unitΟΔβ²§β³Δs damage pool for 
that ability (see 18.2 Allocating Damage).
17.3 FAST DICE ROLLING
In order to speed up play, it is of en possible to make several attacks at
once. If you choose to do so, all of the attacks must have the same Hit,
Wound, Rend and Damage characteristics, the same weapon abilities
(if any ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ see 20.0), and they must target the same enemy unit. If this is
the case, make all of the hit rolls at the same time, then all of the wound
rolls and Ο·¬§¬¹ nally all of the save rolls. T en, add up all the damage points
inΟ·¬§βÄö icted on the target unit and move on to the damage sequence (see 18.0).
If the attack sequence ends for an attack made while fast dice rolling, it
doesnΟΔβ²§β³Δt stop the attack sequence for all the attacks, just that speciΟ·¬§¬¹ c attack.k.
T e bold advance of the Stormcast Eternals enrages a pack of Rat Ogors, triggering a mad charge. T ese hulking Skaven 
aim to tear apart their foes with brute strength and ferocity before a counter-attack can be launched.

## Page 23

19.0 STRIKE-FIRST AND STRIKE-LAST
If there are any Strike-first units in combat at the start of the phase,
other units cannot be picked to use a Fight ability until those units have 
been picked to use a Fight ability. Af er all those Strike-first units have
fought, the active player picks the next unit to Ο·¬§¬¹ ght.
If there are any Strike-last units in combat, they cannot be picked to use a
Fight ability if there are any units in combat that do not have Strike-last
and have not yet used a Fight ability.
If a unit has Strike-first and Strike-last, treat it as if it had neither.
20.0 WEAPON ABILITIES
Some weapons have one or more of the passive weapon abilities below. If a 
weapon has multiple weapon abilities that apply on a criticalΟ²¬†hit, before the 
attack sequence starts, the commander of the attacking model must pick 1 of 
those abilities to use.
21.0 HEALING
Some abilities allow you to heal a unit. To heal a unit, remove a number of 
damage points from the unit equal to the number in brackets af er ΟΔβ²§Υ€HealΟΔβ²§β³Δ, e.g.
Heal (2).

22.0 RETURNING AND ADDINGΟ²¬†MODELS
Some abilities allow you to return slain models to a unit or add new models
to a unit. In either case, set up those models, one at a time, in coherency (see 
15.1) with the model(s) in that unit that were not returned or added this turn.
T e new models can only be set up in combat with an enemy unit if their unit 
is already in combat with that enemy unit.
23.0 TOKENS
Tokens are not considered to be models for rules purposes and can be
ignored or moved out of the way for purposes of visibility, coherency or
abilities. T ey cannot be picked as the target of abilities. T eir purpose is to
indicate persisting eΟ·¬§β²§ ects on units they are placed next to. Each time a unit 
with a token Ο·¬§¬¹ nishes a move or is set up, place the token next to the unit.
24.0 SETTING UP UNITS
Some abilities allow you to set up a unit on the battleΟ·¬§¬¹ eld. When doing so, you 
must set up all models in that unit. If this is impossible, you cannot use that 
ability. A unit set up on the battleΟ·¬§¬¹ eld in a phase other than the deployment
phase cannot use Move abilities in the movement phase of the same turn.
24.1 RESERVE UNITS
Some abilities allow you to set up units in reserve. T ese units are placed
to one side instead of being set up on the battleΟ·¬§¬¹ eld. At the start of the
fourth battle round, units that were set up in reserve using a Deploy
ability and that are still in reserve are destroyed.
24.2 REPLACEMENT UNITS
Some abilities allow you to set up a replacement unit. When setting up
that unit, it should have the same warscroll type, weapon options and
number of models as the original unit, unless otherwise speciΟ·¬§¬¹ ed in the
ability. Many of these abilities specify the proportion of models in the
replacement unit (e.g. half the number of models from the original unit).
In these cases, you can pick which models from the original unit are set up.
T e replacement unit is otherwise treated as a new unit; any keywords
or abilities the original unit gained during the battle, and any persisting
eΟ·¬§β²§ ects that applied to it, do not apply to the replacement unit. Each unit
can only be replaced once, and you cannot replace replacement units.
25.0 GUARDED HEROES
All Heroes that are not Monsters or War Machines have the
ΟΔβ²§Υ€GuardedΟ²¬†HeroΟΔβ²§β³Δ passive ability:
GUARDED HERO: T e leader is shielded by their warriors, who close 
ranks around them to protect them from harm.
EΟ·¬§β²§ ect: If this Hero is within the combat range of a friendly unit that is 
not a Hero:
ΟΔβ²§¬Δ  Subtract 1 from hit rolls for shooting attacks that target this Hero.
ΟΔβ²§¬Δ  If this Hero is Infantry, they cannot be picked as the target of 
shooting attacks made by units more than 12" from them.
Passive

if they are surrounded by

treated as a completely new 
unit, it could, for instance, 
use a Once Per Battle ability 
on its warscroll even if the 
unit it replaced used that 
ability earlier in the battle.

26.0 R NDOM CHAR CTERISTICS
Some warscrolls show a random characteristic roll instead of a Ο·¬§¬¹ xed 
value. When this is the case, the value of the characteristic is generated
v
by the commander of the unit in question each time an ability requires
that characteristic.
In the case of random weapon characteristics, generate a random Attacks
characteristic for each model in the attacking unit each time you declare an 
Attack ability, and generate a random Damage characteristic each time 
you inΟ·¬§βÄö ict damage with that weapon (roll once for each attack).
27.0 MODIFIER ORDER
T e eΟ·¬§β²§ ects of some abilities modify a characteristic or roll. Unless stated
otherwise, a characteristic or roll cannot be modiΟ·¬§¬¹ ed to less than 1. T e 
exception to this is Rend, which can be modiΟ·¬§¬¹ ed to a minimum of 0 (ΟΔβ²§Υ€-ΟΔβ²§β³Δ).
If a characteristic uses a random characteristic roll (see above), apply 
characteristic modiΟ·¬§¬¹ ers af er that characteristic has been generated.
If a characteristic or roll is halved or would be modiΟ·¬§¬¹ ed to a value that is not
a whole number, round it down to the nearest whole number.
Apply characteristic modiΟ·¬§¬¹ ers in the following order:
1.  ModiΟ·¬§¬¹ ers that set a characteristic to a Ο·¬§¬¹ xed value.
2.  ModiΟ·¬§¬¹ ers that multiply or
y y
divide a characteristic.
3.  ModiΟ·¬§¬¹ ers that add to or subtract from a characteristic.
If there are multiple modiΟ·¬§¬¹ ers of the same type (e.g. that set a characteristic),
apply them in the Order of EΟ·¬§β²§ ects (see 30.0).
Ο·¬¹¬¥When using a random 
characteristic, generate it 
each time it is needed for 
an ability.
Ο·¬¹¬¥When using random 
Damage, generate it for 
each attack made.
Skaven technology is infamously unpredictable. T eir warpstone-powered weapons are just as likely to belch out a 
S
thunderous volley as they are a single forlorn shot ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ or they might just explode without warning!

28.0 ADVANCED ABILITY RULES
ΟΔβ²§¬Δ  Unless stated otherwise, units using or picked as part of an ability must be 
onΟ²¬†the battleΟ·¬§¬¹ eld.
ΟΔβ²§¬Δ  If an eΟ·¬§β²§ ect states that a unit can do something, its commander can choose
whether to resolve that part of the eΟ·¬§β²§ ect or not.
ΟΔβ²§¬Δ  If an eΟ·¬§β²§ ect states that a unit must do something, its commander has no
choice and must resolve that part of the eΟ·¬§β²§ ect. If this is impossible, no part 
of the eΟ·¬§β²§ ect is applied but the ability is still considered to have been used.
ΟΔβ²§¬Δ  When ΟΔβ²§Υ€this unitΟΔβ²§β³Δ appears in ability text (most of en on warscrolls), it means
the unit that is using the ability.
ΟΔβ²§¬Δ  If an ability aΟ·¬§β²§ ects more than one unit, the player who used the ability can
choose the order in which units are aΟ·¬§β²§ ected by it.
ΟΔβ²§¬Δ  When resolving an eΟ·¬§β²§ ect, if you need to roll a dice for multiple aΟ·¬§β²§ ected 
units, roll and resolve the eΟ·¬§β²§ ect for one unit before moving on to the next.
ΟΔβ²§¬Δ  If an ability instructs you to pick more than 1 unit, each unit you pick must 
be a diΟ·¬§β²§ erent unit unless stated otherwise.
28.1 PERSISTING EFFECTS
Some abilities have eΟ·¬§β²§ ects that arenΟΔβ²§β³Δt immediately resolved (e.g. ΟΔβ²§Υ€add 1 to 
save rolls for this unit for the rest of the turnΟΔβ²§β³Δ or ΟΔβ²§Υ€this unit has WardΟ²¬†(5+)
for the rest of the turnΟΔβ²§β³Δ). T ese eΟ·¬§β²§ ects count as the eΟ·¬§β²§ ects of passive 
abilities (see 5.4) for their duration.
28.2 ΟΔβ²§Υ€ONCE PERΟΔβ²§β³Δ TIMINGS
T e timing part of some abilities says Once Per Phase, Once Per Turn or
Once Per Battle. If the ability is used by a unit, it can be used a maximum
of one time in that phase, turn or battle by each unit that can use the
ability. If the ability is used by a player, it can be used a maximum of one
time in that timing window by that player.
Some abilities that are used by units say Once Per Phase (Army), Once
Per Turn (Army) or Once Per Battle (Army). T ese abilities can be used
a maximum of one time in that phase, turn or battle regardless of the
number of units in the army that can use the ability.
29.0 CONTR DICTORY RULES
If two or more rules contradict, if one of those rules states that something 
cannot do something, this takes precedence over rules that state it can
or must do that thing, unless the second rule speciΟ·¬§¬¹ cally overrides the
restriction of the Ο·¬§¬¹ rst. For example, while the ΟΔβ²§Υ€Normal MoveΟΔβ²§β³Δ ability states
you cannot move into combat during that move, the ΟΔβ²§Υ€FlyΟΔβ²§β³Δ ability speciΟ·¬§¬¹ es to 
ignore the combat ranges of enemy models during a move.
Excepting the above, the eΟ·¬§β²§ ect of the most recently used ability 
takes precedence.
30.0 ORDER OF EFFECTS
T e eΟ·¬§β²§ ects of passive abilities are considered to be applied more recently than
the eΟ·¬§β²§ ects of other abilities and rules.
T e eΟ·¬§β²§ ects of the active playerΟΔβ²§β³Δs passive abilities are considered to be applied 
more recently than the eΟ·¬§β²§ ects of their opponentΟΔβ²§β³Δs passive abilities, whose
passiveΟ²¬†abilities are considered to be applied more recently than the eΟ·¬§β²§ ects
of neutral passive abilities (e.g. passive abilities on a terrain feature that is in 
neither playerΟΔβ²§β³Δs army). T e active player chooses the order in which neutral 
passive eΟ·¬§β²§ ects are applied.
WHO IS USING 
THEΟ²¬†ABILITY?
Most abilities are found on 
warscrolls, but many appear 
elsewhere ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ the best examples 
being the Universal Core 
Abilities (see 14.0).
While abilities on warscrolls 
are always used by the unit 
whose warscroll it is, some 
abilities that do not appear
on warscrolls will tell you 
explicitly to pick a unit to use
the ability. In both cases, it 
should be clear which unit is
using the ability.
Some abilities that do not 
appear on warscrolls, most 
commonly enhancements (see 
Army Composition, 4.1), are
given to certain units in your 
army. In such cases, the unit 
to which the enhancement
was given is the one using 
the ability.
Abilities that neither appear 
on a warscroll nor tell you to 
pick a unit to use the ability 
are used by you, the player.
Finally, for the purposes 
of the rules in this section, 
when a rule refers to a player 
using an ability, this includes 
abilities used by units in that 
playerΟΔβ²§β³Δs army.

31.0 END OF TURN OVERVIEW
At the end of each turn, follow these steps:
1.  T e active player can use any abilities with the Ο²¬†EndΟ²¬†ofΟ²¬†YourΟ²¬†TurnΟ²¬†
or Ο²¬†EndΟ²¬†ofΟ²¬†AnyΟ²¬†TurnΟ²¬† timing, in the order of their choosing, then
their opponent can use any abilities with the Ο²¬†EndΟ²¬†ofΟ²¬†EnemyΟ²¬†TurnΟ²¬†
or Ο²¬†EndΟ²¬†ofΟ²¬†AnyΟ²¬†TurnΟ²¬† timing, in the order of their choosing.
2.  Determine which player controls each objective (if any).
3.  T e active player scores victory points as described in the battleplan.
32.0 OBJECTIVES
Many battleplans award victory points for controlling objectives, which
are represented by objective markers. Unless otherwise speciΟ·¬§¬¹ ed, objective
markers are round and 40mm wide. Models can move over and end their 
moves on objective markers. If an objective marker is on the border between 
territories, it is within all those territories but wholly within none of them.
Objective markers donΟΔβ²§β³Δt block visibility.
32.1 CONTESTING OBJECTIVES
Unless otherwise speciΟ·¬§¬¹ ed, if an objective is within a modelΟΔβ²§β³Δs combat 
range, that model is contesting that objective. If any models in a unit are 
g g
contesting an objective, that unit is contesting that objective.
Each unit can only contest one objective per turn. If a unit would be
able to contest more than one objective, its player must pick one of those
objectives for it to contest. If both players have more than one unit that 
would be able to contest more than one objective, before determining
objective control, the active player must pick which of those objectives are
being contested by their units Ο·¬§¬¹ rst, then their opponent does the same.
32.2 OBJECTIVE CONTROL
At the start of the Ο·¬§¬¹ rst battle round and at the end of each turn, follow 
this sequence for each objective in an order chosen by the active player:
1.  Starting with the active player, each player determines the control score
of each of their units that is contesting that objective. A unitΟΔβ²§β³Δs control
score is the combined Control characteristics of all the models in that 
unit that are contesting the objective. Some abilities modify a unitΟΔβ²§β³Δs
control score, but it cannot be reduced to less than 1.
2.  Each player adds up the control scores of all of their units contesting 
that objective. T is is their army control score for that objective.
3.  T e players compare their army control scores for that objective. If one 
playerΟΔβ²§β³Δs score is higher, that player gains control of that objective. Once
a player gains control of an objective, it remains under their control
until their opponent gains control of it.
32.3 TERR IN CONTROL
Some battleplans require you to gain control of a terrain feature. Terrain
features are controlled in the same way as objectives, but terrain features
do not remain in your control if your units are no longer contesting them.
Each unit can contest 1 objective and 1 terrain feature at the same time.
33.0 END OF BATTLE ROUND
At the end of each battle round, the active player can use 
any Ο²¬†EndΟ²¬†ofΟ²¬†BattleΟ²¬†RoundΟ²¬† abilities, then their opponent can do the same. Each 
battleplan will specify the number of battle rounds that should be fought. If 
you have completed the number of battle rounds speciΟ·¬§¬¹ ed in your battleplan, 
the battle has ended, and you should follow the rules included in that
battleplan to determine the winner.
END OF TURN

## Page 28

ADVANCED RULES
Each Advanced Rules section is designed to be modular, 
allowing each battlepack to specify which Advanced 
Rules to use with that battlepack. Tis book contains the 
following Advanced Rules sections:
COMMANDS (pg 29-30)
Tese rules explain how you can earn command points
to use powerful commands that allow you to react to 
abilities and interact in your opponentΟΔβ²§β³Δs turn. If you are 
not using the Commands rules, ignore any abilities that 
have the command point symbol in the corner.
TERRIN (pg 31-32)
Tese rules go into more detail about how your models 
interact with terrain features, such as how they can take 
cover or even draw power from arcane nexuses.
MAGIC (pg 33-34)
If youΟΔβ²§β³Δd like to wield unlimited mystical power by 
including Wizards, Priests and manifestations in 
your battles, see Magic.
ARMY COMPOSITION (pg 35-38)
If youΟΔβ²§β³Δre ready to build your own army roster and 
customise your army with regiments, battle formations
and enhancements, see Army Composition.
COMMAND MODELS (pg 39)
Here you will Ο·¬§¬¹nd rules for special models called 
champions, standard bearers and musicians, which are 
found in many Warhammer Age of Sigmar units.
BATTLE TACTICS (pg 40)
Add an additional tactical challenge to your games by 
including secondary objectives called Battle Tactics.
BUILD YOUR OWN BATTLEPACK
Tere are many, many ways to play Warhammer 
Age of Sigmar, from Path to Glory battlepacks, 
which focus on narrative-driven battles linked in an 
ongoing campaign, to Spearhead and Matched Play
battlepacks, in which the emphasis is on balance 
and competitive play.
In addition to using oΟ·¬§Τ£cial battlepacks, weΟΔβ²§β³Δd 
encourage you to use the rules in this section as a 
toolbox, mixing and matching the elements within 
to build your own battleplan or battlepack to play 
with your friends.
Are you excited to Ο·¬§¬¹eld your entire collection 
against your friends or to see which monster reigns 
supreme? Maybe you want to design your own 
battleplans and special rules, or perhaps you want 
to keep things simple in order to teach a younger 
sibling how to play. Tese rules provide an open 
framework that makes each of these things possible.
Here are some ideas to get you started:
ΟΔβ²§¬Δ Instead of using the Advanced Rules for Army 
Composition, make an army based on your 
favourite Black Library story or based on a 
made-up scenario that sounds exciting to you. 
For instance, how would 5 diΟ·¬§β²§erent Stormcast 
Eternals heroes fare against a pair of massive 
Chaos monsters?
ΟΔβ²§¬Δ Design your own battleplan by dividing portions 
of the battleΟ·¬§¬¹eld into a territory for each player, 
placing objectives and thinking up a fun twist 
for the battle. Have fun and experiment with 
diΟ·¬§β²§erent ideas ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ why not try alternating placing 
objectives instead of having Ο·¬§¬¹xed locations, or 
try having two smaller battleΟ·¬§¬¹elds connected 
by realmgates?
ΟΔβ²§¬Δ Learn the Advanced Rules by introducing 
each module into your battles one at a time 
ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ for instance, start by experimenting with 
Commands, then try adding Magic into your 
battles, and Ο·¬§¬¹nally put them all together to outwit 
your opponent and score Battle Tactics.

1.0 COMMANDS OVERVIEW
Some abilities, called commands, require that you spend one or more 
command points (
) to use that ability. Any ability that has a command 
point cost (indicated in the top right corner of the ability) is a command.
1.1 EARNING COMMAND POINTS
At the start of each battle round, afer determining the underdog, each 
player gains 4 command points. If there is an underdog, they gain 1 extra 
command point. At the end of the battle round, the playersΟΔβ²§β³Δ command 
points are reset to 0 (any that have not been used are lost).
1.2 USING COMMANDS
Commands are used in a similar manner to any other ability. However, 
each unit can only use 1 command in each phase, each command can only 
be used 1 time by each army in each phase, and you must spend a number 
of command points equal to the command point cost to use a command.
2.0 HERO PHASE COMMANDS
MAGICAL INTERVENTION: A warrior learned 
in the mystic arts utters a rapid incantation to disrupt 
the enemyΟΔβ²§β³Δs plans.

this ability.
EΟ·¬§β²§ect: Tat friendly unit can use a Spell or Prayer
ability (as appropriate) as if it were your hero 
phase. If you do so, subtract 1 from casting rolls or 
chanting rolls made as part of that ability.
RLLY: With an inspiring call to action, wounded 
soldiers get a second wind and fresh troops take the 
Ο·¬§¬¹eld to replace their fallen comrades.
Declare: Pick a friendly unit that is not in combat to 
use this ability.
EΟ·¬§β²§ect: Make 6 rally rolls of D6. For each 4+, you 
receive 1 rally point. Rally points can be spent in the 
following ways:
ΟΔβ²§¬Δ For each rally point spent, Heal (1) that unit.
ΟΔβ²§¬Δ You can spend a number of rally points equal to the 
Health characteristic of that unit to return a slain 
model to that unit.
You can spend the rally points in any combination of 
the above. Unspent rally points are then lost.

1.0 TERRIN OVERVIEW
Terrain features add interest and tactical challenges to the battleΟ·¬§¬¹eld. Te 
battleplan or battlepack you are using will explain how to set up terrain.
1.1 WHOLLY ON TERRIN AND BEHIND TERRIN
A model is wholly on a terrain feature if its base is on that terrain feature 
and no part of its base extends past the edge of that terrain feature. A unit 
is wholly on a terrain feature if every model in that unit is wholly on that 
terrain feature.
When a unit is targeted by an attack, the unit is considered to be behind 
a terrain feature if it is impossible to draw a straight line from a model in 
the attacking unit to a model in the target unit without that line passing 
across that terrain feature. Ignore parts of the terrain feature within the 
attacking unitΟΔβ²§β³Δs combat range for the purposes of determining if the target 
is behind that terrain feature.
ACTIVATE PLACE OF POWER: Te hero draws 
on the power of a nearby mythical landmark.
Declare: Pick a friendly Hero within 3" of any 
Places of Power to use this ability.
EΟ·¬§β²§ect: Roll a dice. On a 1, inΟ·¬§βÄöict D3 mortal 
damage on that Hero. On a 2+:
ΟΔβ²§¬Δ If that Hero is a Wizard or Priest, add 
1 to casting rolls or chanting rolls for that 
Hero this turn.
ΟΔβ²§¬Δ If that Hero is not a Wizard or Priest, 
they can use the ΟΔβ²§Υ€UnbindΟΔβ²§β³Δ or ΟΔβ²§Υ€Banish 
ManifestationΟΔβ²§β³Δ ability this turn as if they had 
Wizard (1).

Start of Any Turn
TERRIN
1.2 UNIVERSAL TERRIN ABILITIES
Each terrain feature has one or more of the following 
passive abilities:
1.3 TERRIN SIZES
ΟΔβ²§¬Δ Terrain that Ο·¬§¬¹ts into an area no larger than 7" ΟÉβÄî 7" 
is small.
ΟΔβ²§¬Δ Terrain that is not small and Ο·¬§¬¹ts into an area no 
larger than 7" ΟÉβÄî 12" is medium.
ΟΔβ²§¬Δ Terrain that is too big to Ο·¬§¬¹t into an area 7" ΟÉβÄî 12" 
is large.
1.4 TERRIN TYPES
Each terrain feature in Warhammer Age of Sigmar is 
one of the following types:
ΟΔβ²§¬Δ Obstacle
ΟΔβ²§¬Δ Obscuring Terrain
ΟΔβ²§¬Δ Area Terrain
ΟΔβ²§¬Δ Place of Power
ΟΔβ²§¬Δ Faction Terrain
Before the battle begins, players must agree on which 
type applies to each terrain feature.
1.4.1 OBSTACLES
Examples: Ruins, debris, statues, barricades
Terrain Abilities: Cover, Unstable
1.4.2 OBSCURING TERRIN
Examples: Wyldwood, fortress wall
Terrain Abilities: Cover, Obscuring, Unstable
1.4.3  AREA TERRIN
Examples: Hills, Stormvault
Terrain Abilities: Cover
1.4.4 PLACES OF POWER
Examples: Realmgate, Cleansing Aqualith, 
Nexus Syphon
Terrain Abilities: Cover, Place of Power, Unstable
Cover: Subtract 1 from hit rolls for attacks that 
target a unit that is behind or wholly on this 
terrain feature, unless that unit charged or has the 
Fly keyword.
Impassable: Models cannot move across, be set up 
on or end moves on any part of this terrain feature.
Obscuring: A unit cannot be targeted by shooting 
attacks if it is behind or wholly on this terrain 
feature, unless it has the Fly keyword.
Place of Power: Heroes within 3" of this terrain 
feature can use the ΟΔβ²§Υ€Activate Place of PowerΟΔβ²§β³Δ ability 
(see below).
Unstable: Models can move across but cannot be 
set up on or end any type of move on any part of 
this terrain feature that is more than 1" tall.
Te Citadel Terrain List 
(pgΟ²¬†65) includes a list of 
Warhammer Age of Sigmar 
terrain features and tells you 
which terrain abilities each of 
them has.

1.5 FACTION TERRIN
Some factions have special terrain features called faction terrain 
features. Faction terrain features have their own warscrolls. Tey are not 
considered to be units, with the following exceptions:
ΟΔβ²§¬Δ In the charge phase and the combat phase, they are treated as if they 
were units for the purposes of movement, combat range and being in 
combat. Units can Ο·¬§¬¹nish a charge move within Ο²¬Ϋ" of an enemy faction 
terrain feature as if it were a unit.
ΟΔβ²§¬Δ Tey can be picked as targets of enemy abilities as if they were 
units. Tey are not aΟ·¬§β²§ected by enemy abilities that do not involve 
picking targets.
ΟΔβ²§¬Δ Damage points can be inΟ·¬§βÄöicted on them as if they were units and they 
can be destroyed.
1.6 CHARGING UNITS ON TERRIN
In some cases, it is not possible to reach an enemy unit using a Charge
ability when they are wholly on a terrain feature (e.g. a unit on top of a 
tower) because the charging unit is unable to end their move mid-way 
up the terrain feature and there is not enough room for the models to be 
placed at the top. In these cases, the charging unit can end their charge 
within Ο²¬Ϋ" of that terrain feature instead if this would result in the 
charging unit ending their move inΟ²¬†combat with any enemy units wholly 
on a terrain feature.
1.7 SCENERY PIECES
Some terrain features, such as wyldwoods or collections of smaller 
timeworn ruins, are made up of multiple scenery pieces. Each scenery 
piece that makes up a part of the same terrain feature must be set up 
so that all of the scenery pieces Ο·¬§¬¹t into the area that corresponds to the 
terrain featureΟΔβ²§β³Δs size (see 1.3). Te full collection of scenery pieces is 
considered to be a single terrain feature.

## Page 33

MAGIC
1.0 WIZARDS AND PRIESTS
Wizards are special units that can cast spells, and Priests are special units 
that can chant prayers. Spells and prayers are powerful abilities that can have 
a dramatic impact on the battle.
1.1 POWER LEVEL
Each Wizard and Priest has a power level, shown in brackets afer 
the keyword, e.g. Wizard (2). A Wizard or PriestΟΔβ²§β³Δs power level 
determines the number of Spell, Prayer or Banish abilities they can 
use per phase, in any combination. For example, a Wizard (2) unit could 
use 1 Spell and 1 Banish ability in their commanderΟΔβ²§β³Δs hero phase.
2.0 SPELLS
Te declare step of each spell will tell you to make a casting roll of 2D6. If 
the roll does not equal or exceed the spellΟΔβ²§β³Δs casting value (at the top-right 
corner of the spell), the spell fails and its eΟ·¬§β²§ect is not resolved.
If the unmodiΟ·¬§¬¹ed casting roll includes 2 or more rolls of 1, the spell is 
miscast: the spell fails, its eΟ·¬§β²§ect is not resolved, D3 mortal damage is 
inΟ·¬§βÄöicted on the Wizard that used it, and that Wizard cannot use any more 
spells in that phase.
If the casting roll equals or exceeds the spellΟΔβ²§β³Δs casting value, move on to the 
reaction step (Core Rules, 5.2). So long as the spell is not unbound (seeΟ²¬†4.0), 
then it is successfully cast: resolve its eΟ·¬§β²§ect.
3.0 PRYERS
Te declare step of each prayer will tell you to make a chanting roll ofΟ²¬†D6. On 
an unmodiΟ·¬§¬¹ed chanting roll of 1, the prayer fails, its eΟ·¬§β²§ect is not resolved and 
you must remove D3 ritual points from the Priest using the prayer.
On a chanting roll of 2 or more, pick one of the following:
ΟΔβ²§¬Δ Give a number of ritual points to the Priest equal to the chanting roll 
(ritualΟ²¬†points can be accumulated over multiple turns).
ΟΔβ²§¬Δ Add the PriestΟΔβ²§β³Δs ritual points to the chanting roll. If the chanting roll 
equals or exceeds the prayerΟΔβ²§β³Δs chanting value (at the top-right corner of 
the prayer), it is answered: resolve the eΟ·¬§β²§ect of the prayer, then reset the 
PriestΟΔβ²§β³Δs ritual points total to 0.
4.0 UNBINDING SPELLS
Each Wizard can use the ΟΔβ²§Υ€UnbindΟΔβ²§β³Δ reaction a number of times per 
phase equal to their power level. Tis is an exception to Te Rules of One 
(CoreΟ²¬†Rules, 5.3).

UNBIND: Te wizard saps the energy from an enemyΟΔβ²§β³Δs spell, nullifying 
its eΟ·¬§β²§ects.
Used By: A friendly Wizard within 30" of the enemy Wizard casting 
the spell.
EΟ·¬§β²§ect: Make an unbinding roll of 2D6. If the roll exceeds the castingΟ²¬†roll
for the spell, then the spell is unbound and its eΟ·¬§β²§ect is not resolved. Tis 
reaction cannot be used more than once per casting roll.

## Page 34

BUT KHORNE 
HATESΟ²¬†SORCERY!
Even though wizards and 
priests draw upon very 
diΟ·¬§β²§erent sources of power 
in the lore, the former 
harnessing the motes of 
magic that permeate the 
realms and the latter calling 
upon the divine might of 
the gods themselves, such 
nuances are lost on the 
average person in the realms! 
For rules purposes, the 
miraculous abilities of both 
wizards and priests are dealt 
with in the Magic module.
5.0 JEALOUS MAGES AND FICKLE GODS
No more than 1 friendly Wizard can cast the same spell per turn, unless that 
spell has the Unlimited keyword. Likewise, no more than 1 friendly Priest
can chant the same prayer per turn, unless that prayer has the Unlimited
keyword. Keep in mind that each unit can still only use any given spell or 
prayer ability once per phase (see Te Rules of One, CoreΟ²¬†Rules, 5.3).
6.0 KNOWN SPELLS AND PRYERS
Wizards and Priests can only use spells or prayers that they know. Each 
Wizard and Priest knows any spells or prayers on its warscroll, plus all of 
the spells and prayers in the spell lores, manifestation lores and prayerΟ²¬†lores
you take for your army, as appropriate (see the Army Composition module).
7.0 MANIFESTATIONS
Tere are two types of manifestation: endless spells, which can be 
summoned by Wizards, and invocations, which can be summoned 
by Priests. Each manifestation has its own warscroll, and the spell or 
prayer that allows that manifestation to be summoned will be found in the 
appropriate manifestation lore. No more than 1 friendly Wizard or Priest
can attempt to summon the same manifestation per turn. Manifestations are 
not considered to be units, with the following exceptions:
ΟΔβ²§¬Δ Tey are treated as if they were units for the purposes of movement, combat 
range, being in combat and setting up other units. Units can Ο·¬§¬¹nish a charge 
move within Ο²¬Ϋ" of an enemy manifestation as if it were a unit.
ΟΔβ²§¬Δ If they have any melee or ranged weapons, they can use the ΟΔβ²§Υ€FightΟΔβ²§β³Δ and 
ΟΔβ²§Υ€ShootΟΔβ²§β³Δ Core abilities as if they were units.
ΟΔβ²§¬Δ If they have a Move characteristic greater than 0" (ΟΔβ²§Υ€-ΟΔβ²§β³Δ), they can use Core
Move abilities as if they were units.
ΟΔβ²§¬Δ Tey can be picked as targets of enemy abilities as if they were units. Tey 
are not aΟ·¬§β²§ected by enemy abilities that do not involve picking targets.
ΟΔβ²§¬Δ Damage points can be inΟ·¬§βÄöicted on them as if they were units and they can 
be destroyed.
ΟΔβ²§¬Δ Manifestations that have a Move characteristic of 0" (ΟΔβ²§Υ€-ΟΔβ²§β³Δ) cannot move. For 
the purposes of movement, combat range, being in combat and setting up 
other units, they are only treated as if they were units in the charge phase 
and the combat phase.
ΟΔβ²§¬Δ Models can move through manifestations but cannot end a move on them.
7.1 SEVERED CONNECTION
If the Wizard or Priest that summoned a manifestation is slain, that 
manifestation is removed from the battleΟ·¬§¬¹eld.
7.2 BANISHING MANIFESTATIONS
All Wizards and Priests can use the ΟΔβ²§Υ€Banish ManifestationΟΔβ²§β³Δ ability:
BANISH MANIFESTATION: Te wizard or priest disrupts the arcane 
forces sustaining a manifestation, wiping it from existence.
Declare: Pick a friendly Wizard or Priest to use this ability, pick 
a manifestation within 30" of them to be the target, then make a 
banishment roll of 2D6.
EΟ·¬§β²§ect: If the banishment roll equals or exceeds the banishment value
listed on the manifestationΟΔβ²§β³Δs warscroll, it is banished and removed from 
play. You cannot pick the same manifestation as the target of this ability 
more than once per turn.

## Page 35

ARMY COMPOSITION
1.0 GETTING STARTED
1.1 ARMY ROSTER
Tese rules will explain how to create an army roster to prepare for 
a battle. You can go to warhammer-community.com to download a 
printable copy.
1.2 POINTS LIMIT
Before you start Ο·¬§¬¹lling your roster, you and your opponent must agree on 
a points limit for the battle. You can agree on any points limit, but most 
players use a limit of 1000 points, 2000 points or somewhere in between. 
No more than half of your points can be spent on a single unit.
1.2.1 UNDERSPENDING
In many cases, the total points cost of the units in your army will 
not add up to the exact points limit, but you might not have enough 
points lef to add another unit. If the points cost of your army is 50 or 
more points lower than the points limit of the battle, you gain 1 extra 
command point at the start of the Ο·¬§¬¹rst battle round.
1.3 BATTLE PROFILES
Te information youΟΔβ²§β³Δll need when building your army roster is found in 
each unitΟΔβ²§β³Δs battle proΟ·¬§¬¹le. Te battle proΟ·¬§¬¹le for a unit will be in the same 
publication as its warscroll (usually a battletome).
2.0 FACTIONS
Te Ο·¬§¬¹rst thing you need to do when building your army roster is to pick your 
faction (e.g. Stormcast Eternals or Skaven).
Each faction has its own warscrolls, battle proΟ·¬§¬¹les and faction rules. 
Tese can be found in various publications but most commonly in the 
factionΟΔβ²§β³Δs battletome.
2.1 BATTLE FORMATIONS
Many factions contain one or more battle formations. Each battle 
formation grants unique beneΟ·¬§¬¹ts to your army. Te faction rules for your 
faction will explain any battle formation options.
2.2 ARMIES OF RENOWN
Some factions have access to rules for themed armies called Armies of 
Renown. If you choose to use an Army of Renown, the rules for that 
Army of Renown replace the normal faction rules for that army.
3.0 ADDING UNITS
3.1 REGIMENTS
Armies are made up of one or more regiments, each of which is led by 
a Hero. You must have at least 1 regiment in your army, and you can 
include a maximum of 5 regiments. To add a regiment, pick 1 Hero from 
your faction, then pick up to 3Ο²¬†non-Hero units to accompany them.
Each HeroΟΔβ²§β³Δs battle proΟ·¬§¬¹le lists which units can be added to their 
regiment, and each non-Hero unitΟΔβ²§β³Δs battle proΟ·¬§¬¹le lists any relevant 
keywords it has. Te battle proΟ·¬§¬¹les of some Heroes (such as named 
characters) may say that they can be added to the regiment of 
another Hero.
Battle proΟ·¬§¬¹les are regularly 
updated to improve balance. 
When we republish them, the 
new version takes precedence 
over versions with an earlier 
publication date or no 
publication date.
Te latest points values are 
available in the Warhammer 
Age of Sigmar app, and 
points updates are available at 
warhammer-community.com
ADVANCED RULES

---

## Page 36

3.2 THE GENERL
You must pick 1 Hero in your army to be your general. If any units in 
your army have the Warmaster keyword, you must pick one of those 
units to be your general. Your generalΟΔβ²§β³Δs regiment can include up to 
4Ο²¬†non-Hero units in addition to your general.
3.3 REINFORCED UNITS
When you add a unit to your army roster, you can add it as a reinforced 
unit. A reinforced unit has twice as many models as its minimum unit 
size and costs twice as many points. If a unit has a minimum unit size 
ofΟ²¬†1, it cannot be reinforced.
3.4 UNIQUE UNITS
You cannot include the same Unique unit more than once in your army. 
Unique units cannot be reinforced.
3.5 REGIMENTS OF RENOWN
In addition to creating your own regiments, you can also spend points 
to include 1 Regiment of Renown. Regiments of Renown are pre-built 
regiments, each with their own special abilities. Te rules for each 
Regiment of Renown will specify which factions can include it. A unit in a 
Regiment of Renown cannot be your general even if it is a Warmaster.
3.6 AUXILIARY UNITS
Auxiliary units allow you to take any units from your faction without the 
constraints of regiments, at the cost of potentially giving your opponent 
an advantage. You can add any number of units to the auxiliary units 
section of your army roster. However, the player with the fewest auxiliary 
units on their roster gains 1 extra command point at the start of each 
battle round (if the players have the same number of auxiliary units, 
neither player receives an extra command point). Heroes that have 
compulsory regiment options cannot be taken as auxiliary units.
3.7 FACTION TERRIN FEATURES
If your faction has a faction terrain feature, or a set of faction terrain 
features, you can pick 1 to include on your army roster. Faction terrain 
features do not cost any points.
4.0 FINISHING TOUCHES
4.1 ENHANCEMENTS
Each set of faction rules include a number of enhancements that can be 
given to units, e.g. heroic traits, mount traits and artefacts of power. 
You can take 1 enhancement from each enhancement table in your 
factionΟ²¬†rules. Each enhancement table lists which units are eligible to be 
given that enhancement. Unique units cannot be given enhancements.
While some abilities allow you to take extra enhancements, the same unit 
can never have more than 1 enhancement of the same type, and you can 
never include the same enhancement in your army more than once.
4.2 LORES
You can pick 1 spell lore available to your faction. If you do, all Wizards
in your army know all spells from that lore.
You can pick 1 prayer lore available to your faction. If you do, all Priests
in your army know all prayers from that lore.
You can pick 1 manifestation lore available to your faction. If you do, all
Wizards in your army know all spells from that lore, and all Priests
know all prayers from that lore.

units that arenΟΔβ²§β³Δt part of a

has more auxiliary units 
than you, you receive 1 
extra command point each

Units with the Champion keyword have one or more champion models. If 
there is a number afer the Champion keyword, that number indicates the 
proportion of models in that unit that can be champions. In any other case, 
1 model in the unit can be a champion. Units with the Champion keyword 
have the following passive ability:

control score.

ever onward.
EΟ·¬§β²§ect: While this unit contains any musicians, if it uses the ΟΔβ²§Υ€RallyΟΔβ²§β³Δ 
command, you can make one additional rally roll of D6.

## Page 40

BATTLE TACTICS
1.0 BATTLE TACTICS OVERVIEW
During a battle, each player can score extra victory points by completing 
battle tactics.
2.0 PICKING BATTLE TACTICS
Battle tactics are picked by using the ΟΔβ²§Υ€Tactical GambitΟΔβ²§β³Δ ability, below.
2.1 COMPLETING BATTLE TACTICS
If, at the end of your turn, you have completed the battle tactic you chose 
to attempt this turn, you score 4 victory points.
3.0 UNIVERSAL BATTLE TACTICS
TACTICAL GAMBIT: You order your troops to seize an opportunity that 
has arisen amidst the Ο·¬§βÄöow of battle.
You cannot use this ability if you went second in the previous battle 
round and chose to go Ο·¬§¬¹rst in the current battle round.
EΟ·¬§β²§ect: Pick 1 battle tactic that you have not yet attempted. You can 
attempt to complete that battle tactic this turn.
 Once Per Battle Round, Start of Your Turn
DO NOT WAVER: We must not falter on any front. Stand fast, and keep 
the enemy at bay.
You complete this battle tactic at the end of your turn if 2 or more friendly 
units fought this turn and no friendly units were destroyed this turn.
SLAY THE ENTOURGE: Leave the enemyΟΔβ²§β³Δs commander exposed by 
targeting members of their personal guard.
Pick a unit in the enemy generalΟΔβ²§β³Δs regiment. You complete this battle 
tactic if that unit is destroyed this turn.
ATTACK ON TWO FRONTS: Divide and conquer by approaching the 
foe from two directions.
You complete this battle tactic at the end of your turn if you control 2 or 
more objectives that you did not control at the start of your turn and at 
least 1 of those objectives was controlled by your opponent at the start of 
your turn.
TAKE THEIR LAND: Secure a foothold within enemy territory.
Pick a terrain feature wholly or partially within enemy territory and 
wholly outside friendly territory. You complete this battle tactic if you 
control that terrain feature at the end of your turn.
SEIZE THE CENTRE: Secure a strong central position from which your 
forces can dictate the Ο·¬§βÄöow of battle.
You complete this battle tactic at the end of your turn if 2 or more 
friendly units are within 3" of the centre of the battleΟ·¬§¬¹eld and are not 
in combat.
TAKE THE FLANKS: Surround the enemy, then close in for the kill.
You complete this battle tactic at the end of your turn if you have at least 
1 friendly unit within 6" of each short battleΟ·¬§¬¹eld edge, none of those 
units are wholly within friendly territory, and none of those units were 
set up this turn.
Ο·¬¹¬¥Battle tactics are picked 
using the ΟΔβ²§Υ€Tactical GambitΟΔβ²§β³Δ 
ability at the start of your 
turn and are completed at 
the end of your turn.
Ο·¬¹¬¥Each battle tactic is worth 
4Ο²¬†victory points.

## Page 41

MATCHED PLAY BATTLEPACK
BORDER WAR
1. PICK YOUR ARMIES
Each player picks an army using the rules in Army Composition (pg 35).
2. SET UP THE BATTLEFIELD
Te players roll oΟ·¬§β²§. Te winner chooses which player is the attacker and 
which is the defender.
Next, the defender sets up objectives in the locations indicated by gold circles 
on the battleΟ·¬§¬¹eld map 
p
. Ten, the defender sets up terrain features. Each 
terrain feature must be set up more than 3" from the battleΟ·¬§¬¹eld edge, more 
than 6" from all other terrain features and more than 3" from all objectives.
Afer objectives and terrain have been set up, the attacker picks which 
territory is their territory. Te other territory is the defenderΟΔβ²§β³Δs territory. Te 
players then resolve the deployment phase. Te attacker begins deployment
(CoreΟ²¬†Rules, 10.0).
VICTORY POINTS
Each player scores victory points at the end of each of their turns as follows:
ΟΔβ²§¬Δ Score 2 victory points if you control at least 1 objective.
ΟΔβ²§¬Δ Score 2 victory points if you control 2 or more objectives.
ΟΔβ²§¬Δ Score 2 victory points if you control more objectives than your opponent.
ADVANCED RULES
In addition to the Core 
Rules, Matched Play: Border 
War uses the following 
Advanced Rules:
ΟΔβ²§¬Δ Commands (pg 29)
ΟΔβ²§¬Δ Terrain (pg 31)
ΟΔβ²§¬Δ Magic (pg 33)
ΟΔβ²§¬Δ Army Composition (pg 35)
ΟΔβ²§¬Δ Command Models (pg 39)
ΟΔβ²§¬Δ Battle Tactics (pg 40)
BATTLEFIELD SIZE
For a 1000-point battle, 
we recommend a 
30"Ο²¬†ΟÉβÄîΟ²¬†44" battleΟ·¬§¬¹eld with 
4Ο²¬†terrain features. 
For a 2000-point battle, 
we recommend a 
44"Ο²¬†ΟÉβÄîΟ²¬†60" battleΟ·¬§¬¹eld with 
8Ο²¬†terrain features.
If you have agreed on a 
points limit outside these 
bounds, feel free to adjust the 
battleΟ·¬§¬¹eld size and number of 
terrain features appropriately.

GLORIOUS VICTORY
Te player with the most victory points at the end of the battle wins a 
majorΟ²¬†victory.
If the players are tied on victory points at the end of the battle, and only one 
playerΟΔβ²§β³Δs general has survived the battle, that player wins a minor victory. If 
the players are tied on victory points and both generals or neither general 
survived the battle, the battle is a draw.
