## Page 33

1.5 FACTION TERRIN
Some factions have special terrain features called faction terrain 
features. Faction terrain features have their own warscrolls. Tey are not 
considered to be units, with the following exceptions:
ΟΔβ²§¬Δ In the combat phase, they are treated as if they were units for the 
purposes of movement, combat range and being in combat. Units can 
Ο·¬§¬¹nish a charge move within Ο²¬Ϋ" of an enemy faction terrain feature as if 
it were a unit.
ΟΔβ²§¬Δ Tey can be picked as targets of enemy abilities as if they were 
units. Tey are not aΟ·¬§β²§ected by enemy abilities that do not involve 
picking targets.
ΟΔβ²§¬Δ Damage points can be inΟ·¬§βÄöicted on them as if they were units and they 
can be destroyed.
ΟΔβ²§¬Δ If a terrain feature has any melee or ranged weapons, it can use the 
ΟΔβ²§Υ€FightΟΔβ²§β³Δ and ΟΔβ²§Υ€ShootΟΔβ²§β³Δ Core abilities as if it were a unit.
ΟΔβ²§¬Δ Terrain features that have a Move characteristic of 0" (ΟΔβ²§Υ€-ΟΔβ²§β³Δ) cannot move 
(note that pile-in moves are a type of move).
Some faction terrain features allow you to place a unit on them (this will 
be clearly speciΟ·¬§¬¹ed in that terrain featureΟΔβ²§β³Δs rules). When doing so, instead 
of measuring range or visibility to and from the unit that is on that terrain 
feature, measure to and from the terrain feature instead. Unless otherwise 
speciΟ·¬§¬¹ed, units on a faction terrain feature cannot use Fight abilities.
If a unit placed on a terrain feature is removed from it (e.g. if an ability 
removes them from the battleΟ·¬§¬¹eld), that unit counts as having lef that 
terrain feature and loses any special role or beneΟ·¬§¬¹ts it had as a result of 
being placed on it.
1.6 CHARGING UNITS ON TERRIN
In some cases, it is not possible to reach an enemy unit using a Charge
ability when they are wholly on a terrain feature (e.g. a unit on top of a 
tower) because the charging unit is unable to end their move mid-way 
up the terrain feature and there is not enough room for the models to be 
placed at the top. In these cases, the charging unit can end their charge 
within Ο²¬Ϋ" of that terrain feature instead if this would result in the 
charging unit ending their move inΟ²¬†combat with any enemy units wholly 
on a terrain feature.
1.7 SCENERY PIECES
Some terrain features, such as wyldwoods or collections of smaller 
timeworn ruins, are made up of multiple scenery pieces. Each scenery 
piece that makes up a part of the same terrain feature must be set up 
so that all of the scenery pieces Ο·¬§¬¹t into the area that corresponds to the 
terrain featureΟΔβ²§β³Δs size (see 1.3). Te full collection of scenery pieces is 
considered to be a single terrain feature.

that list, agree with your 
opponent before setting up 
the battleΟ·¬§¬¹eld how many 
scenery pieces make up each

## Page 34

MAGIC
1.0 WIZARDS AND PRIESTS
Wizards are special units that can cast spells, and Priests are special units 
that can chant prayers. Spells and prayers are powerful abilities that can have 
a dramatic impact on the battle.
1.1 POWER LEVEL
Each Wizard and Priest has a power level, shown in brackets afer 
the keyword, e.g. Wizard (2). A Wizard or PriestΟΔβ²§β³Δs power level 
determines the number of Spell, Prayer or Banish abilities they can 
use per phase, in any combination. For example, a Wizard (2) unit could 
use 1 Spell and 1 Banish ability in their commanderΟΔβ²§β³Δs hero phase.
2.0 SPELLS
Te declare step of each spell will tell you to make a casting roll of 2D6. If 
the roll does not equal or exceed the spellΟΔβ²§β³Δs casting value (at the top-right 
corner of the spell), the spell fails and its eΟ·¬§β²§ect is not resolved.
If the unmodiΟ·¬§¬¹ed casting roll includes 2 or more rolls of 1, the spell is 
miscast: the spell fails, its eΟ·¬§β²§ect is not resolved, D3 mortal damage is 
inΟ·¬§βÄöicted on the Wizard that used it, and that Wizard cannot use any more 
spells in that phase.
Enemy reactions can only be used if the casting roll equals or exceeds the 
spellΟΔβ²§β³Δs casting value. So long as the spell is not unbound (seeΟ²¬†4.0), then it is 
successfully cast: resolve its eΟ·¬§β²§ect.
3.0 PRYERS
Te declare step of each prayer will tell you to make a chanting roll ofΟ²¬†D6. On 
an unmodiΟ·¬§¬¹ed chanting roll of 1, the prayer fails, its eΟ·¬§β²§ect is not resolved and 
you must remove D3 ritual points from the Priest using the prayer.
On an unmodiΟ·¬§¬¹ed chanting roll of 2 or more, pick one of the following:
ΟΔβ²§¬Δ Give a number of ritual points to the Priest equal to the chanting roll 
(ritualΟ²¬†points can be accumulated over multiple turns).
ΟΔβ²§¬Δ Add the PriestΟΔβ²§β³Δs ritual points to the chanting roll. If the chanting roll 
equals or exceeds the prayerΟΔβ²§β³Δs chanting value (at the top-right corner of 
the prayer), it is answered: resolve the eΟ·¬§β²§ect of the prayer, then reset the 
PriestΟΔβ²§β³Δs ritual points total to 0.
4.0 UNBINDING SPELLS
Each Wizard can use the ΟΔβ²§Υ€UnbindΟΔβ²§β³Δ reaction a number of times per 
phase equal to their power level. Tis is an exception to Te Rules of One 
(CoreΟ²¬†Rules, 5.3).
Ο²¬ç Wizards can cast spells; 
Priests can chant prayers.

can use a number of spells 
or prayers per phase equal

Ο²¬ç Each Wizard can use

abilities per phase equal to

only be used once per turn.
UNBIND: Te wizard saps the energy from an enemyΟΔβ²§β³Δs spell, nullifying 
its eΟ·¬§β²§ects.
Used By: A friendly Wizard within 30" of the enemy Wizard casting 
the spell.
EΟ·¬§β²§ect: Make an unbinding roll of 2D6. If the roll exceeds the castingΟ²¬†roll
for the spell, then the spell is unbound and its eΟ·¬§β²§ect is not resolved. Tis 
reaction cannot be used more than once per casting roll.

## Page 35

BUT KHORNE 
HATESΟ²¬†SORCERY!
Even though wizards and 
priests draw upon very 
diΟ·¬§β²§erent sources of power 
in the lore, the former 
harnessing the motes of 
magic that permeate the 
realms and the latter calling 
upon the divine might of 
the gods themselves, such 
nuances are lost on the 
average person in the realms! 
For rules purposes, the 
miraculous abilities of both 
wizards and priests are dealt 
with in the Magic module.
5.0 JEALOUS MAGES AND FICKLE GODS
No more than 1 friendly Wizard can cast the same spell per turn, unless that 
spell has the Unlimited keyword. Likewise, no more than 1 friendly Priest
can chant the same prayer per turn, unless that prayer has the Unlimited
keyword. Keep in mind that each unit can still only use any given spell or 
prayer ability once per phase (see Te Rules of One, CoreΟ²¬†Rules, 5.3).
6.0 KNOWN SPELLS AND PRYERS
Wizards and Priests can only use spells or prayers that they know. Each 
Wizard and Priest knows any spells or prayers on its warscroll, plus all of 
the spells and prayers in the spell lores, manifestation lores and prayerΟ²¬†lores
you take for your army, as appropriate (see the Army Composition module).
7.0 MANIFESTATIONS
Tere are two types of manifestation: endless spells, which can be 
summoned by Wizards, and invocations, which can be summoned 
by Priests. Each manifestation has its own warscroll, and the spell or 
prayer that allows that manifestation to be summoned will be found in the 
appropriate manifestation lore. No more than 1 friendly Wizard or Priest
can attempt to summon the same manifestation per turn, and a friendly 
Wizard or Priest cannot attempt to summon a friendly manifestation that 
was removed from play in the same turn. Manifestations are not considered 
to be units, with the following exceptions:
ΟΔβ²§¬Δ Tey are treated as if they were units for the purposes of movement, combat 
range, being in combat and setting up other units. Units can Ο·¬§¬¹nish a charge 
move within Ο²¬Ϋ" of an enemy manifestation as if it were a unit.
ΟΔβ²§¬Δ If they have any melee or ranged weapons, they can use the ΟΔβ²§Υ€FightΟΔβ²§β³Δ and 
ΟΔβ²§Υ€ShootΟΔβ²§β³Δ Core abilities as if they were units.
ΟΔβ²§¬Δ If they have a Move characteristic greater than 0" (ΟΔβ²§Υ€-ΟΔβ²§β³Δ), they can use Core
Move abilities as if they were units.
ΟΔβ²§¬Δ Tey can be picked as targets of enemy abilities as if they were units. Tey 
are not aΟ·¬§β²§ected by enemy abilities that do not involve picking targets.
ΟΔβ²§¬Δ Damage points can be inΟ·¬§βÄöicted on them as if they were units and they can 
be destroyed.
ΟΔβ²§¬Δ Manifestations that have a Move characteristic of 0" (ΟΔβ²§Υ€-ΟΔβ²§β³Δ) cannot move. For 
the purposes of movement, combat range, being in combat and setting up 
other units, they are only treated as if they were units in the combat phase. 
Units can Ο·¬§¬¹nish a charge move within Ο²¬Ϋ" of an enemy manifestation that 
has a Move characteristic of 0" (ΟΔβ²§Υ€-ΟΔβ²§β³Δ) instead of within Ο²¬Ϋ" of an enemy unit.
ΟΔβ²§¬Δ Models can move through manifestations but cannot end a move on them.
7.1 SEVERED CONNECTION
If the Wizard or Priest that summoned a manifestation is slain, that 
manifestation is removed from the battleΟ·¬§¬¹eld.
7.2 BANISHING MANIFESTATIONS
All Wizards and Priests can use the ΟΔβ²§Υ€Banish ManifestationΟΔβ²§β³Δ ability:
BANISH MANIFESTATION: Te wizard or priest disrupts the arcane 
forces sustaining a manifestation, wiping it from existence.
Declare: Pick a friendly Wizard or Priest to use this ability, pick 
a manifestation within 30" of them to be the target, then make a 
banishment roll of 2D6. Add 1 to the banishment roll for each additional 
enemy manifestation on the battleΟ·¬§¬¹eld afer the Ο·¬§¬¹rst.
EΟ·¬§β²§ect: If the banishment roll equals or exceeds the banishment value
listed on the manifestationΟΔβ²§β³Δs warscroll, it is banished and removed from 
play. You cannot pick the same manifestation as the target of this ability 
more than once per turn.

## Page 36

ARMY COMPOSITION
1.0 GETTING STARTED
1.1 ARMY ROSTER
Tese rules will explain how to create an army roster to prepare for 
a battle. You can go to warhammer-community.com to download a 
printable copy.
1.2 POINTS LIMIT
Before you start Ο·¬§¬¹lling your roster, you and your opponent must agree on 
a points limit for the battle. You can agree on any points limit, but most 
players use a limit of 1000 points, 2000 points or somewhere in between. 
No more than half of your points can be spent on a single unit.
1.2.1 UNDERSPENDING
In many cases, the total points cost of the units in your army will 
not add up to the exact points limit, but you might not have enough 
points lef to add another unit. If the points cost of your army is 50 or 
more points lower than the points limit of the battle, you gain 1 extra 
command point at the start of the Ο·¬§¬¹rst battle round.
1.3 BATTLE PROFILES
Te information youΟΔβ²§β³Δll need when building your army roster is found 
in each unitΟΔβ²§β³Δs battle proΟ·¬§¬¹le. Battle proΟ·¬§¬¹les for each unit are available 
at warhammer-community.com.
2.0 FACTIONS
Te Ο·¬§¬¹rst thing you need to do when building your army roster is to pick your 
faction (e.g. Stormcast Eternals or Skaven).
Each faction has its own warscrolls, battle proΟ·¬§¬¹les and faction rules. 
Tese can be found in various publications but most commonly in the 
factionΟΔβ²§β³Δs battletome.
2.1 BATTLE FORMATIONS
Many factions contain one or more battle formations. Each battle 
formation grants unique beneΟ·¬§¬¹ts to your army. Te faction rules for your 
faction will explain any battle formation options.
2.2 ARMIES OF RENOWN
Some factions have access to rules for themed armies called Armies of 
Renown. If you choose to use an Army of Renown, the rules for that 
Army of Renown replace the normal faction rules for that army.
3.0 ADDING UNITS
3.1 REGIMENTS
Armies are made up of one or more regiments, each of which is led by 
a Hero. You must have at least 1 regiment in your army, and you can 
include a maximum of 5 regiments. To add a regiment, pick 1 Hero from 
your faction, then pick up to 3Ο²¬†non-Hero units to accompany them.
Each HeroΟΔβ²§β³Δs battle proΟ·¬§¬¹le lists which units can be added to their 
regiment, and each non-Hero unitΟΔβ²§β³Δs battle proΟ·¬§¬¹le lists any relevant 
keywords it has. Te battle proΟ·¬§¬¹les of some Heroes (such as named 
characters) may say that they can be added to the regiment of 
another Hero.
Battle proΟ·¬§¬¹les are regularly 
updated to improve balance. 
When we republish them, the 
new version takes precedence 
over versions with an earlier 
publication date or no 
publication date.
Te latest points values are 
available in the Warhammer 
Age of Sigmar app, and 
points updates are available at 
warhammer-community.com
ADVANCED RULES

---

## Page 37

3.2 THE GENERL
You must pick 1 Hero in your army that is leading a regiment to be your 
general. If any units in your army have the Warmaster keyword, you 
must pick one of those units to be your general. Your generalΟΔβ²§β³Δs regiment
can include up to 4Ο²¬†non-Hero units in addition to your general.
3.3 REINFORCED UNITS
When you add a unit to your army roster, you can add it as a reinforced 
unit. A reinforced unit has twice as many models as its minimum unit 
size and costs twice as many points. If a unit has a minimum unit size 
ofΟ²¬†1, it cannot be reinforced.
3.4 UNIQUE UNITS
You cannot include the same Unique unit more than once in your army. 
Unique units cannot be reinforced.
3.5 REGIMENTS OF RENOWN
In addition to creating your own regiments, you can also spend points 
to include 1 Regiment of Renown. Regiments of Renown are pre-built 
regiments, each with their own special abilities. Te rules for each 
Regiment of Renown will specify which factions can include it. A unit in a 
Regiment of Renown cannot be your general even if it is a Warmaster.
ΟΔβ²§¬Δ You cannot include more than 1 Regiment of Renown in your army 
unless otherwise specified in the notes column of that regimentΟΔβ²§β³Δs 
battle profile.
ΟΔβ²§¬Δ Units in a Regiment of Renown cannot use (but can be picked as the 
target of, if otherwise eligible) any faction rules from the faction they 
are allied into, including enhancements and lores, unless they have a 
keyword that matches that factionΟΔβ²§β³Δs name. 
ΟΔβ²§¬Δ If an ability allows you to set up a replacement unit (Core Rules, 24.2) for 
a unit in a Regiment of Renown, that unit also counts as being part of 
that Regiment of Renown.
Ο²¬ç Pick a points limit: 1000 
points or 2000 points. If 
other points limit, agree 
with opponent.
Ο²¬ç Pick your faction.
Ο²¬ç Pick your battle formation 
(if any).
Ο²¬ç Form regiments. Each 
regiment has 1 Hero and 
0-3 non-Hero units (0-4 if 
your generalΟΔβ²§β³Δs regiment).
Ο²¬ç Add auxiliary units (if 
any). Auxiliary units are 
units that arenΟΔβ²§β³Δt part of a 
regiment. If your opponent 
has more auxiliary units 
than you, you receive 1 
extra command point each 
battle round.
Ο²¬ç Add faction terrain features 
(if any).
Ο²¬ç Pick enhancements (1 from 
each enhancement table).
Ο²¬ç Pick up to 1 spell lore, 
1 prayer lore and 1 
manifestation lore.
3.6 AUXILIARY UNITS
Auxiliary units allow you to take any units from your faction without the constraints of regiments, at the cost of 
potentially giving your opponent an advantage. You can add any number of units to the auxiliary units section of 
your army roster. However, the player with the fewest auxiliary units on their roster gains 1 extra command point 
at the start of each battle round (if the players have the same number of auxiliary units, neither player receives an 
extra command point). Heroes that have compulsory regiment options cannot be taken as auxiliary units.
3.7 FACTION TERRIN FEATURES
If your faction has a faction terrain feature, or a set of faction terrain features, you can pick 1 to include on your 
army roster. Faction terrain features do not cost any points.
4.0 FINISHING TOUCHES
4.1 ENHANCEMENTS
Each set of faction rules include a number of enhancements that can be given to units, e.g. heroic traits, mount 
traits and artefacts of power. You can take 1 enhancement from each enhancement table in your factionΟ²¬†rules. 
Each enhancement table lists which units are eligible to be given that enhancement. Unique units cannot be 
given enhancements.
While some abilities allow you to take extra enhancements, the same unit can never have more than 1 
enhancement of the same type, and you can never include the same enhancement in your army more than once.
4.2 LORES
You can pick 1 spell lore available to your faction. If you do, all Wizards in your army know all spells from that lore.
You can pick 1 prayer lore available to your faction. If you do, all Priests in your army know all prayers from that lore.
You can pick 1 manifestation lore available to your faction. If you do, all Wizards in your army know all spells from 
that lore, and all Priests know all prayers from that lore.

1.0 CHAMPIONS
Units with the Champion keyword have one or more champion models. If 
there is a number afer the Champion keyword, that number indicates the 
proportion of models in that unit that can be champions. In any other case, 
1 model in the unit can be a champion. Units with the Champion keyword 
have the following passive ability:

control score.

ever onward.
EΟ·¬§β²§ect: While this unit contains any musicians, if it uses the ΟΔβ²§Υ€RallyΟΔβ²§β³Δ 
command, you can make one additional rally roll of D6.

## Page 41

BATTLE TACTICS
1.0 BATTLE TACTICS OVERVIEW
During a battle, each player can score extra victory points by completing 
battle tactics.
2.0 PICKING BATTLE TACTICS
Battle tactics are picked by using the ΟΔβ²§Υ€Tactical GambitΟΔβ²§β³Δ ability, below.
2.1 COMPLETING BATTLE TACTICS
If, at the end of your turn, you have completed the battle tactic you chose 
to attempt this turn, you score 4 victory points.
3.0 UNIVERSAL BATTLE TACTICS
TACTICAL GAMBIT: You order your troops to seize an opportunity that 
has arisen amidst the Ο·¬§βÄöow of battle.
You cannot use this ability if you went second in the previous battle 
round and chose to go Ο·¬§¬¹rst in the current battle round.
EΟ·¬§β²§ect: Pick 1 battle tactic that you have not yet attempted. You can 
attempt to complete that battle tactic this turn.
 Once Per Battle Round, Start of Your Turn
DO NOT WAVER: We must not falter on any front. Stand fast, and keep 
the enemy at bay.
You complete this battle tactic at the end of your turn if 2 or more friendly 
units fought this turn and no friendly units were destroyed this turn.
SLAY THE ENTOURGE: Leave the enemyΟΔβ²§β³Δs commander exposed by 
targeting members of their personal guard.
Pick a unit in the enemy generalΟΔβ²§β³Δs regiment. You complete this battle 
tactic if that unit is destroyed this turn.
ATTACK ON TWO FRONTS: Divide and conquer by approaching the 
foe from two directions.
You complete this battle tactic at the end of your turn if you control 2 or 
more objectives that you did not control at the start of your turn and at 
least 1 of those objectives was controlled by your opponent at the start of 
your turn.
TAKE THEIR LAND: Secure a foothold within enemy territory.
Pick a terrain feature wholly or partially within enemy territory and 
wholly outside friendly territory. You complete this battle tactic if you 
control that terrain feature at the end of your turn.
SEIZE THE CENTRE: Secure a strong central position from which your 
forces can dictate the Ο·¬§βÄöow of battle.
You complete this battle tactic at the end of your turn if 2 or more 
friendly units are within 3" of the centre of the battleΟ·¬§¬¹eld and are not 
in combat.
TAKE THE FLANKS: Surround the enemy, then close in for the kill.
You complete this battle tactic at the end of your turn if you have at least 
1 friendly unit within 6" of each short battleΟ·¬§¬¹eld edge, none of those 
units are wholly within friendly territory, and none of those units were 
set up this turn.
Ο²¬ç Battle tactics are picked 
using the ΟΔβ²§Υ€Tactical GambitΟΔβ²§β³Δ 
ability at the start of your 
turn and are completed at 
the end of your turn.
Ο²¬ç Each battle tactic is worth 
4Ο²¬†victory points.
40
ADVANCED RULES

## Page 42

MATCHED PLAY BATTLEPACK
BORDER WAR
1. PICK YOUR ARMIES
Each player picks an army using the rules in Army Composition (pg 35).
2. SET UP THE BATTLEFIELD
Te players roll oΟ·¬§β²§. Te winner chooses which player is the attacker and 
which is the defender.
Next, the defender sets up objectives in the locations indicated by gold circles 
on the battleΟ·¬§¬¹eld map 
p
. Ten, the defender sets up terrain features. Each 
terrain feature must be set up more than 3" from the battleΟ·¬§¬¹eld edge, more 
than 6" from all other terrain features and more than 3" from all objectives.
Afer objectives and terrain have been set up, the attacker picks which 
territory is their territory. Te other territory is the defenderΟΔβ²§β³Δs territory. Te 
players then resolve the deployment phase. Te attacker begins deployment
(CoreΟ²¬†Rules, 10.0).
VICTORY POINTS
Each player scores victory points at the end of each of their turns as follows:
ΟΔβ²§¬Δ Score 2 victory points if you control at least 1 objective.
ΟΔβ²§¬Δ Score 2 victory points if you control 2 or more objectives.
ΟΔβ²§¬Δ Score 2 victory points if you control more objectives than your opponent.
ADVANCED RULES
In addition to the Core 
Rules, Matched Play: Border 
War uses the following 
Advanced Rules:
ΟΔβ²§¬Δ Commands (pg 29)
ΟΔβ²§¬Δ Terrain (pg 31)
ΟΔβ²§¬Δ Magic (pg 33)
ΟΔβ²§¬Δ Army Composition (pg 35)
ΟΔβ²§¬Δ Command Models (pg 39)
ΟΔβ²§¬Δ Battle Tactics (pg 40)
BATTLEFIELD SIZE
For a 1000-point battle, 
we recommend a 
30"Ο²¬†ΟÉβÄîΟ²¬†44" battleΟ·¬§¬¹eld with 
4Ο²¬†terrain features. 
For a 2000-point battle, 
we recommend a 
44"Ο²¬†ΟÉβÄîΟ²¬†60" battleΟ·¬§¬¹eld with 
8Ο²¬†terrain features.
If you have agreed on a 
points limit outside these 
bounds, feel free to adjust the 
battleΟ·¬§¬¹eld size and number of 
terrain features appropriately.

BATTLE LENGTH
Border War battles last for 4 battle rounds.
GLORIOUS VICTORY
Te player with the most victory points at the end of the battle wins a 
majorΟ²¬†victory.
If the players are tied on victory points at the end of the battle, and only one 
playerΟΔβ²§β³Δs general has survived the battle, that player wins a minor victory. If 
the players are tied on victory points and both generals or neither general 
survived the battle, the battle is a draw.

## Page 45

SPEARHEAD

---

## Page 46

Te Age of Sigmar grows fraught. On horizons yonder, the forces of Chaos 
loom, threatening to engulf the Mortal Realms in mayhem and violence 
once more. In the wake of these troubling times, Spearhead forces are 
dispatched across the realms to seek out ancient sites of power that still 
reside deep within the uncharted hinterlands. Will you take up the call to 
battle and lead a Spearhead force to victory?
SPEARHEAD
Welcome to the world of Spearhead. In this game mode, players engage in 
small-scale battles that are fast and tactical, suitable for newcomers and 
veterans alike. In Spearhead, each army is available as a self-contained 
boxΟ²¬†set, allowing players to quickly muster a force. In addition, Spearhead 
battles are fought upon a compact battleΟ·¬§¬¹eld, meaning you can set up a game 
even with limited space. Spearhead uses specially tailored rules to ensure 
that every battle is a tense and exciting aΟ·¬§β²§air that comes right down to the 
Ο·¬§¬¹nal moments.
SPEARHEAD BATTLEPACKS
Veteran players will notice similarities between the rules on Spearhead 
warscrolls and those found in battletomes. However, they have been tweaked 
to make sure they work speciΟ·¬§¬¹cally with Spearhead. Tis means aΟ²¬†battletome 
is not required to play Spearhead ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ all the rules you need to play are available 
to download for free from warhammer-community.com. Tis makes 
Spearhead the ideal format for newcomers to Warhammer Age of Sigmar, 
as well as for players looking for a quick and casual format with lots of re-
playability and tactical depth.
SPEARHEAD BOX SETS
Fighting a Spearhead battle could not be easier. Each player picks their 
army by choosing one of the many Spearhead box sets available. Each of 
these is a complete army, including all the miniatures a player will need 
to do battle. Te rules for 25 diΟ·¬§β²§erent Spearhead box sets are available to 
download for free from warhammer-community.com, allowing you to 
get stuck in as soon as your models are assembled.

---

## Page 47

SPEARHEAD AND BEYOND
Once you have mastered one Spearhead army, you 
may Ο·¬§¬¹nd yourself looking for a fresh challenge. Some 
players choose to ΟΔβ²§Υ€collect broadΟΔβ²§β³Δ ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ this means picking 
up diΟ·¬§β²§erent Spearhead box sets to use with Spearhead 
battlepacks. Perhaps you want to see how a diΟ·¬§β²§erent 
faction plays in Spearhead, or you want to collect a 
Spearhead army from each of the Grand Alliances: 
Order, Chaos, Death and Destruction.
Other players choose to ΟΔβ²§Υ€collect deepΟΔβ²§β³Δ. Tis means 
picking up further units from their Spearhead armyΟΔβ²§β³Δs 
faction to build an army they can use with diΟ·¬§β²§erent 
battlepacks, such as Path to Glory and Matched Play 
battlepacks. If you choose to do so, picking up your 
factionΟΔβ²§β³Δs battletome should be your Ο·¬§¬¹rst port of call.
BATTLETOMES
Battletomes are the ultimate guide to the diΟ·¬§β²§erent 
factions of the Mortal Realms. Each is Ο·¬§¬¹lled with 
detailed lore, inspiring artwork and awesome 
showcases of miniatures. Crucially, they contain the 
faction rules and warscrolls youΟΔβ²§β³Δll need to use your 
army in most games of Warhammer Age of Sigmar.
HOW SHOULD I BUILD MYΟ²¬†MODELS?
When assembling the miniatures in your Spearhead 
box, you may Ο·¬§¬¹nd options to build one of two 
diΟ·¬§β²§erent units. Check the Spearhead rules for the 
army to see which unit to build.
Many more units will have options, such 
as adding a champion, standard 
bearer and musician, or 
perhaps a special weapon 
or two. While the rules 
for these options have 
in most cases been 
simpliΟ·¬§¬¹ed in Spearhead, 
we still recommend you 
assemble your units with these 
extras. Not only will they look 
glorious on the battleΟ·¬§¬¹eld, if 
you decide to use the army 
in other battlepacks, youΟΔβ²§β³Δll 
be able to take advantage 
of the additional rules 
they provide!
Tree-Revenant Musician
Sylvaneth
With Spearhead, setting up a game of Warhammer Age of Sigmar with friends has never been quicker or simpler. 
JustΟ²¬†put your Spearhead force on the table, shuΟ·¬§βÄûe your cards and get playing!

EachΟ²¬†has a corresponding box set that contains all the models you need.

Spearhead battles are fought on realm battleΟ·¬§¬¹elds. Each side of a realm battleΟ·¬§¬¹eld is themed around one of the 
eight Mortal Realms (see Realm BattleΟ·¬§¬¹elds, pg 50).
GETTING STARTED

of Treelords ofen sees them act

3. PICK YOUR REGIMENT ABILITY AND ENHANCEMENT
Each Spearhead army has a choice of 2 diΟ·¬§β²§erent regiment abilities and 4 diΟ·¬§β²§erent enhancements for your 
general. At the start of the battle, you must pick 1 of each for your army (see Spearhead Armies, pg 49).
5. FIGHT THE BATTLE!
Spearhead battles usually last around an hour and a half. During the battle, the players will each try to score 
victory points, and at the end of the battle, the player with the most is declared the winner!
4. PREPARE YOUR WARSCROLLS
Each set of Spearhead rules includes the warscrolls for all the units used in your Spearhead army. A warscroll 
details how fast and tough a unit is, as well as what weapons it is armed with and any special abilities it has.
ΟΔβ²§¬Δ Every Spearhead army has a set of 
battle traits, regiment abilities
and enhancements.
ΟΔβ²§¬Δ Te battle traits are rules and 
abilities for your army that you will 
use in every battle.
ΟΔβ²§¬Δ Te regiment abilities and 
enhancements are picked at the start 
of every battle. You can experiment 
with diΟ·¬§β²§erent combinations to 
see what works best for you. Some 
regiment abilities and enhancements 
are better against certain foes, so 
it pays to study the strengths and 
weaknesses of the enemy before 
making your choice!
ΟΔβ²§¬Δ Every unit in a Spearhead army has 
its own warscroll, where you can see 
its characteristics and weapons.
ΟΔβ²§¬Δ Any unique abilities the unit can use 
will also be found on its warscroll.
ΟΔβ²§¬Δ At the bottom of every warscroll you 
can Ο·¬§¬¹nd its keywords, such as Hero, 
Wizard, Monster or Infantry.

anything they collide with. Each 
ill-tempered squig has a cackling 
grot clinging to its back, who 
Ο·¬§βÄöails wildly at nearby enemies as 
he tries vainly to steer his weird 
steed. Only the most madcap of 
grots will attempt to enter the 
Squig HoppersΟΔβ²§β³Δ bounding ranks, 
for each fang-Ο·¬§¬¹lled mount is 
nigh uncontrollable ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ its rider 
must simply cling to its bumpy 
body and hope not to be bucked 
oΟ·¬§β²§ amidst the mayhem.
KEYWORDS
BOING! BOING! BOING!: Squig Hoppers can cause 
considerable damage simply by bouncing on top of their 
foes as they make their way across the battleΟ·¬§¬¹eld.

10
SPEARHEAD BATTLEPACK
FIRE AND JADE
1.0 SPEARHEAD ARMIES
Each player in a Spearhead battle is the commander of a Spearhead army. 
A Spearhead army is a set of units that comes with its own rules and 
warscrolls. You can download the rules for 25 diΟ·¬§β²§erent Spearhead armies for 
free from warhammer-community.com.
1.1 ARMY COMPOSITION
On the Ο·¬§¬¹rst page of every Spearhead armyΟΔβ²§β³Δs rules, you will Ο·¬§¬¹nd the army 
composition. Tis lists which units are included in the Spearhead army 
and who the general is. Unlike other Warhammer Age of Sigmar armies, 
the composition of a Spearhead army is Ο·¬§¬¹xed and cannot be changed.
1.2 BATTLE TRITS
Every Spearhead army has one or more battle traits. Tese are powerful 
and thematic abilities that apply to some or all of the units in the army. 
For example, the ΟΔβ²§Υ€Holy OrdersΟΔβ²§β³Δ battle trait enables the Stormcast Eternals 
to use powerful once-per-battle abilities to smite their foes, while the 
ΟΔβ²§Υ€Gnawhole AmbushΟΔβ²§β³Δ battle trait allows Skaven warriors to arrive on the 
battleΟ·¬§¬¹eld behind enemy lines.
1.3 REGIMENT ABILITIES
Every Spearhead army has a choice of 2 regiment abilities, each of which 
provides the army with a diΟ·¬§β²§erent strength in battle.
1.4 ENHANCEMENTS
Every Spearhead army has a set of 4 enhancements to pick from. Tese 
are upgrades for the armyΟΔβ²§β³Δs general that make them more powerful in a 
variety of ways.
1.5 SPEARHEAD WARSCROLLS
Te warscrolls for the units in each Spearhead army are included with 
the other rules for the army. Although Spearhead warscrolls are similar 
to the warscrolls found in battletomes, they have been tailored to work 
speciΟ·¬§¬¹cally with Spearhead and cannot be used with other battlepacks.
WIZARDS AND 
PRIESTS IN 
SPEARHEAD
Some units in Spearhead 
have the Wizard or Priest
keyword. If you are familiar 
with other battlepacks, 
it is important to note 
the Magic module in the 
Advanced Rules is not used in 
Spearhead. As a result, there 
are no unbinding rolls and no 
chance of miscasts (among 
other things). Spells and 
prayers are resolved like any 
other ability.
THE CORE RULES
To play a game using this 
battlepack, youΟΔβ²§β³Δll need 
the Core Rules. Te Core 
Rules explain the basics of 
Warhammer Age of Sigmar, 
such as how to move your 
models and how to use 
abilities. You can Ο·¬§¬¹nd the 
Core Rules on pages 5-27.
You donΟΔβ²§β³Δt need the Advanced 
Rules to use this battlepack.

PRE-BATTLE SEQUENCE
1. Roll oΟ·¬§β²§. Te winner chooses who is the attacker and who is the defender.
2. Te attacker picks their regiment ability and their enhancement. 
TenΟ²¬†the defender does the same.
3. Te defender chooses which side of the realm battleΟ·¬§¬¹eld the players will 
Ο·¬§¬¹ght on: Aqshy or Ghyran.
4. Te defender picks 1 of the deployment maps on the right and chooses 
which territory belongs to which player.
5. Te defender sets up their terrain features, followed by the attacker. 
Each terrain feature must be set up wholly within friendly territory, more 
than 6" from all other terrain features and more than 3" from both long
battleΟ·¬§¬¹eld edges and enemy territory. Terrain features cannot be set up 
on top of the objectives (either wholly or partially).

BATTLEPLAN
FIRE AND JADE
As the Hour of Ruin dawns, the Ο·¬§¬¹ght to secure sacred sites and 
geomantic nexuses rages Ο·¬§¬¹ercer than ever. It is in Aqshy and Ghyran 
that the Ο·¬§¬¹rst battles of this new age are fought, each faction sending out 
vanguard forces to take and hold these places of power in the name of 
their deities ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ or even the godbeasts of myth. So unpredictable are these 
frontiers of war that the land itself can lend its blessing to the chosen or 
send wracking curses against those who incur its wrath.
DEPLOYMENT
Do not deploy armies as described in the Core Rules. Instead, the attacker
sets up all the units in their army Ο·¬§¬¹rst, followed by the defender. Each 
unit must be set up wholly within friendly territory and more than 6" from 
enemy territory.
BATTLE LENGTH
Te battle lasts for 4 battle rounds.
SPECIAL RULES
PREPARING THE SPEARHEAD DECK
Tis battle uses the Fire and Jade Spearhead deck. One player takes the 
twist deck that corresponds to the side of the battleΟ·¬§¬¹eld the players are 
Ο·¬§¬¹ghting on, shuΟ·¬§βÄûes it and places it face down next to the battleΟ·¬§¬¹eld (the 
other twist deck is not used).
Ten, each player takes 1 battle tactic deck, shuΟ·¬§βÄûes it and places it face 
down where they can reach it during the battle.

START OF THE BATTLE ROUND SEQUENCE
Te following sequence is resolved at the start of 
eachΟ²¬†battle round:
1. If it is the Ο·¬§¬¹rst battle round, the attacker chooses who 
will take the Ο·¬§¬¹rst turn. In any other battle round, the 
players make a priority roll (CoreΟ²¬†Rules, 12.0).
2. Determine the underdog (CoreΟ²¬†Rules, 12.0).
3. Te twist card is drawn (pg 50).
4. Te players draw battle tactic cards (see below).
5. Any abilities with the Ο²¬†StartΟ²¬†ofΟ²¬†BattleΟ²¬†RoundΟ²¬† timing 
are used.
TWIST CARDS
One twist card is drawn at the start of each battle 
round. Follow the rules on it as soon as it is drawn.
BATTLE TACTIC 
CARDS
At the start of the Ο·¬§¬¹rst 
battle round, each 
player draws a hand of 
3Ο²¬†battleΟ²¬†tacticΟ²¬†cards.
At the start of each 
subsequent battle round, 
each player draws battle 
tactic cards until they once 
more have a hand of 3 battle tactic cards. Before they do 
so, they can discard any number of battle tactic cards 
inΟ²¬†their hand. Cards are discarded face-up.
VICTORY POINTS
Each player scores victory points at the end of each of 
their turns as follows:
ΟΔβ²§¬Δ Score 1 victory point if you control at least 
one objective.
ΟΔβ²§¬Δ Score 1 victory point if you control two or 
more objectives.
ΟΔβ²§¬Δ Score 1 victory point if you control more objectives 
than your opponent.
ΟΔβ²§¬Δ Score 1 victory point for each battle tactic you 
completed this turn.
GLORIOUS VICTORY
Te player with the most victory points at the end of 
the battle is the winner. If the players are tied on victory 
points, the battle is a draw.
SEIZING THE INITIATIVE
If the player who went second in the previous battle 
round wins the priority roll and chooses to go Ο·¬§¬¹rst, 
it is called seizing the initiative. When a player 
seizes the initiative, they do not draw any battle 
tactic cards for that battle round unless they are 
the underdog and the diΟ·¬§β²§erence in victory points 
between the two players is 5 or greater.

2.0 REALM BATTLEFIELDS
Spearhead battles are fought on realm battleΟ·¬§¬¹elds. Once the players have 
picked their Spearhead armies, they must agree on a realm battleΟ·¬§¬¹eld to use. 
Each realm battleΟ·¬§¬¹eld comes with a matching SpearheadΟ²¬†deck, along with a
battleplan that explains how to Ο·¬§¬¹ght a battle on that realm battleΟ·¬§¬¹eld.
A realm battleΟ·¬§¬¹eld is 30"Ο²¬†ΟÉβÄîΟ²¬†22" and double-sided, with each side 
corresponding to a diΟ·¬§β²§erent Mortal Realm. Te Ο·¬§¬¹rst realm battleΟ·¬§¬¹eld is Fire
and Jade, which has one side representing Aqshy, the Realm of Fire, and the 
y,y
reverse representing Ghyran, the Realm of Life.
3.0 SPEARHEAD DECKS
Spearhead decks are sets of cards that are used in Spearhead battles. Each
realm battleΟ·¬§¬¹eld has its own Spearhead deck. Each SpearheadΟ²¬†deck consists 
of 2 twist decks (one for each side of the realm battleΟ·¬§¬¹eld) and 2 battle tactic 
decks (one for each player).
3.1 TWIST CARDS
Each twist deck is made up of 
k
twist cards. A twist is a special rule
that applies to the battle round being played. Each deck of twist cards
corresponds to a speciΟ·¬§¬¹c side of the realm battleΟ·¬§¬¹eld.
3.2 BATTLE TACTIC CARDS
Each battle tactic deck is made up of 
k k
battle tactic cards. Te rules on
each are split into 2 parts: a battle tactic and a command. You can either 
score the battle tactic at the end of your turn if you meet its conditions (to
gain victory points) or you can use the command on the card for a more
immediate beneΟ·¬§¬¹t. You cannot do both, so choose wisely!
Each command will say when it can be used and what eΟ·¬§β²§ect it has. Afer
the eΟ·¬§β²§ect has been resolved, the card is discarded. You can issue multiple
commands to the same unit in the same phase.
4.0 SPEARHEAD BATTLEPLANS
Each realm battleΟ·¬§¬¹eld has a corresponding battleplan. Te battleplan is a set 
of instructions on how to Ο·¬§¬¹ght the battle and how to determine the winner.
You can Ο·¬§¬¹nd the battleplan for the Fire and Jade realm battleΟ·¬§¬¹eld on 
pages 55-56.

## Page 52

5.0 TERRIN
In every Spearhead battle, the armies Ο·¬§¬¹ght around 2Ο²¬†large terrain features and 2 small terrain features.
Before the armies are deployed, each player will set up 1 large terrain feature and 1 small terrain feature wholly 
within their territory. Each terrain feature has certain passive abilities depending onΟ²¬†its size:
Large Terrain Feature: Cover, Obscuring, Unstable
Small Terrain Feature: Cover, Unstable
COVER: Subtract 1 from hit rolls for attacks that target a unit that is behind or wholly on this terrain feature, 
unless that unit charged or has the Fly keyword.
OBSCURING: A unit cannot be targeted by shooting attacks if it is behind or wholly on this terrain feature, unless 
it has the Fly keyword.
UNSTABLE: Models can move across but cannot be set up on or end any type of move on any part of this terrain 
feature that is more than 1" tall.

7.0 REINFORCEMENTS
Some units have the Reinforcements keyword. Tese units can be 
brought back to the battleΟ·¬§¬¹eld afer being destroyed as replacement units
(CoreΟ²¬†Rules, 24.2).
If any of your units have the Reinforcements keyword, you can use the 
following ability:
CALL FOR REINFORCEMENTS: A second wave of troops enters 
the fray, replacing those lost to the rigours of battle.
Declare: Pick a friendly Reinforcements unit that has been destroyed.
EΟ·¬§β²§ect: Set up an identical replacement unit on the battleΟ·¬§¬¹eld, wholly 
within friendly territory, wholly within 6" of the battleΟ·¬§¬¹eld edge and 
notΟ²¬†in combat.
Each Reinforcements unit can only be replaced once. Replacement 
units cannot themselves be replaced.

Once Per Turn, Your Movement Phase
6.0 OBJECTIVES
In Spearhead battles, objectives are printed on the surface of each realm 
battleΟ·¬§¬¹eld (the objective is the whole circle, not just the symbol in the centre).
6.1 CONTESTING OBJECTIVES
In Spearhead battles, while a model is on an objective (wholly or 
partially), it is contesting it.
Units with the 
Reinforcements 
keyword have this 
icon in the top-right 
corner of their warscrolls for 
ease of use.

SPEARHEAD REFERENCE
DEPLOYMENT
Attacker sets up their army Ο·¬§¬¹rst, followed by 
the defender. Each unit must be set up wholly 
within friendly territory and more than 6" from 
enemy territory.
SEIZING THE INITIATIVE
If the player who went second in the previous battle 
round wins the priority roll and chooses to go 
Ο·¬§¬¹rst in the current battle round, they do not draw 
any battle tactic cards for that battle round unless 
they are the underdog and the diΟ·¬§β²§erence in victory 
points between the players is 5 or more.
VICTORY POINTS
At the end of each of your turns:
ΟΔβ²§¬Δ Score 1 victory point if you control at least 
one objective.
ΟΔβ²§¬Δ Score 1 victory point if you control two or 
more objectives.
ΟΔβ²§¬Δ Score 1 victory point if you control more 
objectives than your opponent.
ΟΔβ²§¬Δ Score 1 victory point for each battle tactic you 
completed this turn.
BATTLE TACTICS CARDS
ΟΔβ²§¬Δ Start of Ο·¬§¬¹rst battle round: draw 3 battle 
tactic cards.
ΟΔβ²§¬Δ Start of second or later battle round: discard any 
battle tactic cards in your hand if you wish. Ten 
if you have less than 3 battle tactic cards in your 
hand, keep drawing them until you have 3.

SEQUENCE
ΟΔβ²§¬Δ First battle round: attacker chooses who takes 
the Ο·¬§¬¹rst turn. Later battle rounds: players make a 
priority roll (CoreΟ²¬†Rules, 12.0).
ΟΔβ²§¬Δ Determine underdog (Core Rules, 12.0).
ΟΔβ²§¬Δ Draw twist card.

CALL FOR REINFORCEMENTS
Declare: Pick a friendly Reinforcements
unit that has been destroyed.
EΟ·¬§β²§ect: Set up an identical replacement unit on 
the battleΟ·¬§¬¹eld, wholly within friendly territory, 
wholly within 6" of the battleΟ·¬§¬¹eld edge and 
notΟ²¬†in combat.
Each Reinforcements unit can only be 
replaced once. Replacement units cannot 
themselves be replaced.
Keywords
Core
Once Per Turn, Your Movement Phase
TERRIN
Large Terrain Feature: Cover, Obscuring, Unstable
Small Terrain Feature: Cover, Unstable
Cover: Subtract 1 from hit rolls for attacks that 
target a unit that is behind or wholly on this 
terrain feature, unless that unit charged or has the 
Fly keyword.
Obscuring: A unit cannot be targeted by shooting 
attacks if it is behind or wholly on this terrain 
feature, unless it has the Fly keyword.
Unstable: Models can move across but cannot be 
set up on or end any type of move on any part of 
this terrain feature that is more than 1" tall.
PRE-BATTLE SEQUENCE
ΟΔβ²§¬Δ Roll oΟ·¬§β²§. Winner chooses who is attacker and who is defender.
ΟΔβ²§¬Δ Attacker picks regiment ability and enhancement, then defender does so.
ΟΔβ²§¬Δ Defender chooses Aqshy or Ghyran side of realm battleΟ·¬§¬¹eld.
ΟΔβ²§¬Δ Defender picks deployment map (horizontal or diagonal) and chooses which territory is theirs.
ΟΔβ²§¬Δ Defender sets up 1 large and 1 small terrain feature. Ten attacker does the same. Each terrain feature must 
be set up wholly within friendly territory, more than 6" from all other terrain features and more than 3" from 
both long battleΟ·¬§¬¹eld edges and enemy territory. Terrain features cannot be set up on objectives.

UNIVERSAL WEAPON ABILITIES
Anti-X (+1 Rend): Add 1 to this weaponΟΔβ²§β³Δs Rend characteristic if the target has the keyword afer ΟΔβ²§Υ€Anti-ΟΔβ²§β³Δ or 
fulΟ·¬§¬¹ls the condition afer ΟΔβ²§Υ€Anti-ΟΔβ²§β³Δ. Multiples of this ability are cumulative. For example, if a weapon has both 
Anti-charge (+1 Rend) and Anti-Hero (+1 Rend), then add 2 to the Rend characteristic of the weapon for 
attacks that target a Hero that charged in the same turn.
Charge (+1 Damage): Add 1 to this weaponΟΔβ²§β³Δs Damage characteristic if the attacking unit charged this turn.
Companion: Unless otherwise speciΟ·¬§¬¹ed, this weapon is not aΟ·¬§β²§ected by friendly abilities that aΟ·¬§β²§ect weapon 
characteristics or the attack sequence, except for those that apply negative modiΟ·¬§¬¹ers to it (e.g.Ο²¬†ΟΔβ²§Υ€Covering FireΟΔβ²§β³Δ).
Crit (2 Hits): If an attack made with this weapon scores a criticalΟ²¬†hit, that attack scores 2 hits on the target 
unit instead of 1. Make a wound roll for each hit.
Crit (Auto-wound): If an attack made with this weapon scores a criticalΟ²¬†hit, that attack automatically wounds 
the target. Make a save roll as normal.
Crit (Mortal): If an attack made with this weapon scores a criticalΟ²¬†hit, that attack inΟ·¬§βÄöicts mortal damage on 
the target unit equal to the Damage characteristic of that weapon and the attack sequence ends.
Shoot in Combat: Tis weapon can be used to make shooting attacks even if the attacking unit is in combat.

GLOSSARY
abilities: Te things that units can

are common to all units; others 
are speciΟ·¬§¬¹c to certain units and

use abilities. (CoreΟ²¬†Rules, 5.0)
active player: Te player whose turn 
is taking place. Tere are 2 turns in

use abilities with the ΟΔβ²§Υ€Your PhaseΟΔβ²§β³Δ 
timing. (CoreΟ²¬†Rules, 12.0)
allocate: Damage points are 
allocated one at a time to a unit afer

All-out Attack: Command, 1CP, 
reaction. Attacking unit adds 1 to 
hit rolls. (Commands, 6.0)
All-out Defence: Command, 1CP, 
reaction. Target unit adds 1 to save 
rolls. (Commands, 7.0)
Anti-X (+1 Rend): Weapon ability. 
+1 Rend for attacks that target units 
that fulΟ·¬§¬¹l the condition afer ΟΔβ²§Υ€Anti-ΟΔβ²§β³Δ. 
(CoreΟ²¬†Rules, 20.0)
Any (ΟΔβ²§¬Π) Phase: Timing. Either

Army of Renown: A special type 
of army that uses a modiΟ·¬§¬¹ed 
set of faction rules. (Army 
Composition, 2.2)
army: All of a playerΟΔβ²§β³Δs units are 
collectively referred to as their army. 
(Core Rules, 1.0)
army composition: Te step before 
a battle in which the players build 
their armies. Te battlepack may 
specify in more detail what they can 
and canΟΔβ²§β³Δt include in their armies.
army roster: Te list of units in 
a playerΟΔβ²§β³Δs army, and any other 
important information like 
enhancements. Players can check 
each otherΟΔβ²§β³Δs rosters any time they 
like. (Army Composition, 1.1)
artefact of power: Enhancement. 
You can give 1 Hero in your 
army an artefact of power. (Army 
Composition, 4.1)
At the Double: Command, 
1CP, reaction. Add 6 to Move

(Commands, 3.0)

wound roll and save roll, then 
determine damage points inΟ·¬§βÄöicted. 
(CoreΟ²¬†Rules, 17.0)
auxiliary unit: A unit on your army

(Army Composition, 3.6)
Banish Manifestation: Your Hero 
Phase, 30", banishment roll of 2D6. 
Equal or beat banishment value of 
the manifestation to remove it from 
play. (Magic, 7.2)
battle proΟ·¬§¬¹le: Important 
information about a unit that you 
will need to refer to if you wish to 
include it in your army, such as its 
points value and minimum unit 
size. (Army Composition, 1.3)
battle round: Te battle lasts a 
number of battle rounds speciΟ·¬§¬¹ed in 
the battleplan. Each battle round has 
2 turns (1 for each player), and each 
turn has 7 phases. (CoreΟ²¬†Rules, 11.0)
battle tactics: Attempt by using the 
ΟΔβ²§Υ€Tactical GambitΟΔβ²§β³Δ ability at the start 
of your turn. Complete at the end 
of your turn for 4 victory points. 
(Battle Tactics, 1.0)
battle trait: A type of faction rule 
that usually aΟ·¬§β²§ects your army as 
a whole and reΟ·¬§βÄöects the way your 
faction does battle, as opposed to

battleΟ·¬§¬¹eld: Te Ο·¬§βÄöat surface on which

short edges and 2 long edges, which 
may collectively be referred to as 
ΟΔβ²§Υ€the battleΟ·¬§¬¹eld edgeΟΔβ²§β³Δ. It is divided

begins deployment: Te player who

behind a terrain feature: Impossible

Tis glossary is an alphabetised list of rules deΟ·¬§¬¹nitions. It is intended to be a useful resource if you need a quick

Charge (+1 Damage): Weapon

coherency range: Ο²¬Ϋ" horizontally 
and any distance vertically of 1 or

combat attack: An attack made with

combat range: ModelΟΔβ²§β³Δs combat 
range = 3" horizontally and 
any distance vertically from 
model. UnitΟΔβ²§β³Δs combat range = 3" 
horizontally and any distance 
vertically from all models in unit.

command points: Each player 
gets 4 command points at start 
of round. Underdog gets 1 extra. 
(Commands, 1.0)
commander: You are the 
commander of your army and 
everything in it (units, models, etc.).
commands: Abilities that require

(Commands, 1.0)
Companion: Weapon ability.

contesting: Models contest

control: Determined at the start of

Control characteristic: Determines

(CoreΟ²¬†Rules, 4.0)
control score: A unitΟΔβ²§β³Δs control 
score is determined by adding 
all the Control characteristics of 
models in the unit contesting the 
objective in question. An armyΟΔβ²§β³Δs 
control score for that objective is

(CoreΟ²¬†Rules, 32.2)
Core: Basic ability that can only 
be used by each unit once per 
phase, e.g. Move, Shoot, Fight. 
(CoreΟ²¬†Rules, 5.1)
Counter-charge: Command, 2CP. 
Charge in enemy charge phase. 
(Commands, 5.0)
Cover: -1 to hit rolls for attacks 
that target a unit behind or wholly 
on this terrain feature, unless unit 
charged or has Fly. (Terrain, 1.2)
Covering Fire: Command, 1CP. 
Shoot in enemy shooting phase, but 
-1 to hit rolls and must target nearest 
enemy unit. (Commands, 4.0)
Crit (2 Hits): Weapon ability. 
UnmodiΟ·¬§¬¹ed hit roll of 6 scores 2 hits 
on target. (CoreΟ²¬†Rules, 20.0)
Crit (Auto-wound): Weapon ability. 
UnmodiΟ·¬§¬¹ed hit roll of 6 skips

Crit (Mortal): Weapon ability. 
UnmodiΟ·¬§¬¹ed hit roll of 6 inΟ·¬§βÄöicts an

the Damage characteristic of the

(CoreΟ²¬†Rules, 20.0)
critical hit: UnmodiΟ·¬§¬¹ed hit roll of 6. 
(CoreΟ²¬†Rules, 17.0)
D3: Roll a D6 and halve the result,

damaged: A unit is damaged if it

modiΟ·¬§¬¹ers (characteristics):

phases: Tere are 7 phases in each

pile-in: A short move, usually

prayer: A special type of ability

prayer lore: If taken, all eligible

Priest: Unit that can use prayers. 
(Magic, 1.0)
Rally: Command, Any Hero 
Phase, 1CP. Roll 6 dice; for each 4+, 
gain 1 rally point. For each rally 
point, Heal (1) or spend a number

slain: A model in a unit is slain

Models, 3.0)
straight line: If a rule asks you to

1mm wide.

If both players have units with 
Strike-first, they alternate 
Ο·¬§¬¹ghting with those units before

Strike-last: Units with this

summon: Manifestations are

target (attacks): You must declare

terrain features: Models that 
reΟ·¬§βÄöect the battleΟ·¬§¬¹eld environment.

territory (within/wholly within):
A unit is within a territory if any 
part of the base of any model in the 
unit is within that territory. A unit 
is wholly within a territory if all 
parts of the bases of all models in

token: Tokens are not considered

are otherwise ignored and can be

that speciΟ·¬§¬¹es when it can be used.

(CoreΟ²¬†Rules, 5.2)
turns: Each battle round consists

whose turn is taking place is the

Unbind: Reaction to a Spell ability.

underdog: Player with fewest 
victory points. If tied, there is no

(CoreΟ²¬†Rules, 12.0)

Unique: A unit with this keyword

used: An ability has been used once

visible: An observing model can

on the observing model to the 
target model without it crossing 
any objects except other models 
in the observing modelΟΔβ²§β³Δs unit. A

(CoreΟ²¬†Rules, 20.0)
wholly on a terrain feature: A unit

wholly within (model): model x is

within (model): model x is within

within (unit): unit x is within a 
speciΟ·¬§¬¹ed distance of y if any part

within that distance of y.
Wizard: Unit that can use spells 
(Magic, 1.0)
wound roll modiΟ·¬§¬¹ers: Add up all

## Page 2

DEPLOY FACTION TERRAIN: 
Declare: Pick a friendly faction terrain feature that 
has not been deployed to be the target.
Effect: Set up the target faction terrain feature wholly 
within friendly territory, more than 3" from all 
objectives and other terrain features. After you have 
done so, it has been deployed.
Keywords
Deploy Terrain
Deployment Phase
DEPLOY REGIMENT: 
Declare: Pick a regiment from your army 
roster to be the target. No units in that regiment 
can have already been deployed.
Effect: Keep using Deploy abilities without 
alternating until all units in that regiment have 
been deployed. You cannot pick units that are 
not in that regiment as the target of any of those 
Deploy abilities.
Keywords
Deploy
Deployment Phase
DEPLOY UNIT: 
Declare: Pick a unit from your army roster that has 
not been deployed to be the target.
Effect: Set up the target unit wholly within friendly 
territory and more than 9" from enemy territory. 
After you have done so, it has been deployed.
Keywords
Deploy
Deployment Phase
A regiment is a collection of units. It is one of 
the main building blocks of an army (seeΟ²¬†ΟΔβ²§Υ€Army 
CompositionΟΔβ²§β³Δ in the Advanced Rules).
Some factions have special Deploy abilities, 
such as the ΟΔβ²§Υ€Scions of the StormΟΔβ²§β³Δ ability of the 
Stormcast Eternals.
DEPLOYMENT PHASE
(ONE PER BATTLE)
BATTLE ROUNDS 1-5
END OF BATTLE
USING ABILITIES
1. Declare the ability 
2. Use Reactions: alternate, starting with the 
active player
3. Resolve the effect
A unit cannot use more than 1 Core ability per phase. 
Passive abilities are not declared. The effects of passive 
abilities always apply if the conditions of the ability are 
met, and they must be applied if it is possible to do so.
A unit cannot be affected by the same passive ability 
more than once at the same time.
Commands are abilities that cost command points 
(
). You must spend the number of command points 
specified on a command to use it. 
Each unit can only use 1 command in each phase, and 
each command can only be used 1 time by each army 
in each phase.
DEPLOYMENT PHASE
In each step, the player who begins deployment uses 
their abilities first, then their opponent does the same. 
1. Deploy faction terrain features using Deploy 
Terrain abilities
2. Deploy units using Deploy abilities
3. Use  DeploymentΟ²¬†Phase  abilities that are not 
Deploy or Deploy Terrain abilities.
Make sure you follow the instructions in the 
battleplan you are playing when deploying your 
armies and faction terrain.
RULES REFERENCE
This guide will help you get started with games of Warhammer Age of Sigmar. For more information, see the 
Core Rules. Warhammer Age of Sigmar battles are played in a series of battle rounds. In each of these, both 
players have a turn, broken into different phases that must be completed in order.
2
RULES REFERENCE

UNBIND:
Used By: A friendly Wizard within 30" of the 
enemy Wizard casting the spell.
Effect: Make an unbinding roll of 2D6. If the roll 
exceeds the castingΟ²¬†roll for the spell, then the spell is 
unbound and its effect is not resolved. This reaction 
cannot be used more than once per casting roll.
Keywords
Unbind
START OF TURN ABILITIES
HERO PHASE ABILITIES
TACTICAL GAMBIT:
You cannot use this ability if you went second in the 
previous battle round and chose to go first in the 
current battle round.
Effect: Pick 1 battle tactic that you have not yet 
attempted. You can attempt to complete that battle 
tactic this turn.
 Once Per Battle Round, Start of Your Turn
ACTIVATE PLACE OF POWER:
Declare: Pick a friendly Hero within 3" of any 
Places of Power to use this ability.
Effect: Roll a dice. On a 1, inflict D3 mortal damage 
on that Hero. On a 2+:
ΟΔβ²§¬Δ	 If that Hero is a Wizard or Priest, add 1 to 
casting rolls or chanting rolls for that Hero 
this turn.
ΟΔβ²§¬Δ	 If that Hero is not a Wizard or Priest, they 
can use the ΟΔβ²§Υ€UnbindΟΔβ²§β³Δ or ΟΔβ²§Υ€Banish ManifestationΟΔβ²§β³Δ 
ability this turn as if they had Wizard (1).
Keywords
Core
Start of Any Turn
RALLY:
Declare: Pick a friendly unit that is not in combat to 
use this ability.
Effect: Make 6 rally rolls of D6. For each 4+, you 
receive 1 rally point. Rally points can be spent in the 
following ways:
ΟΔβ²§¬Δ For each rally point spent, Heal (1) that unit.
ΟΔβ²§¬Δ You can spend a number of rally points equal to the 
Health characteristic of that unit to return a slain 
model to that unit.
You can spend the rally points in any combination of 
the above. Unspent rally points are then lost.
Any Hero Phase
BANISH MANIFESTATION:
Declare: Pick a friendly Wizard or Priest to use 
this ability, pick a manifestation within 30" of them 
to be the target, then make a banishment roll of 2D6.
Effect: If the banishment roll equals or exceeds 
the banishment value listed on the manifestationΟΔβ²§β³Δs 
warscroll, it is banished and removed from play. You 
cannot pick the same manifestation as the target of 
this ability more than once per turn.
Keywords
Banish
Your Hero Phase
MUSICIAN:
Effect: While this unit contains any musicians, 
if it uses the ΟΔβ²§Υ€RallyΟΔβ²§β³Δ command, you can make one 
additional rally roll of D6.
Passive

those attacks.

FORWARD TO VICTORY:

CHARGE: 
Declare: Pick a friendly unit that is not inΟ²¬†combat 
and has not used a Run or Retreat ability this turn 
to use this ability. Then, make a charge roll of 2D6.
Effect: That unit can move a distance up to the value 
of the charge roll. That unit can move through the 
combat ranges of any enemy units and must end that 
move within Ο²¬Ϋ" of a visible enemy unit. If it does so, 
the unit using this ability has charged.
Keywords
Core, Move, Charge
Your Charge Phase
MOVEMENT PHASE ABILITIES
NORMAL MOVE: 
Declare: Pick a friendly unit that is not inΟ²¬†combat to 
use this ability.
Effect: That unit can move a distance up to its Move 
characteristic. That unit cannot move into combat 
during any part of that move.
Keywords
Core, Move
RUN:
Declare: Pick a friendly unit that is not inΟ²¬†combat to 
use this ability.
Effect: Make a run roll of D6. That unit can move a 
distance up to its Move characteristic added to the 
run roll. That unit cannot move into combat during 
any part of that move.
Keywords
Core, Move, Run
RETREAT:
Declare: Pick a friendly unit that is inΟ²¬†combat to use 
this ability.
Effect: Inflict D3 mortal damage on that unit. 
That unit can move a distance up to its Move 
characteristic. That unit can move through the 
combat ranges of any enemy units but cannot end 
that move within an enemy unitΟΔβ²§β³Δs combat range.
Keywords
Core, Move, Retreat
Your Movement Phase
Your Movement Phase
Your Movement Phase
REDEPLOY: 
Declare: Pick a friendly unit that is not inΟ²¬†combat to 
use this ability.
Effect: Each model in that unit can move up to D6". 
That move cannot pass through or end within the 
combat range of an enemy unit.
Keywords
Move, Run
Enemy Movement Phase

in that unit can move as part of that Run ability.

## Page 6

OBJECTIVE CONTROL 
ΟΔβ²§¬Δ An objective marker is a 40mm round marker.
ΟΔβ²§¬Δ A model contests an objective if the objective 
marker is within its combat range.
ΟΔβ²§¬Δ A player gains control of an objective if the sum 
of the Control characteristics of friendly models 
contesting that objective is higher than that of 
enemy models.
ΟΔβ²§¬Δ Check if you gain control of objectives at the start 
of the first battle round and at the end of each turn.
ΟΔβ²§¬Δ An objective remains in your control until your 
opponent gains control of it.
ΟΔβ²§¬Δ Terrain features are controlled in the same way as 
objective markers but do not remain in your control 
if no friendly models are contesting them.
START OF BATTLE ROUND
1. Determine the Active Player: If it is the first battle round, the player who finished setting up their army first 
decides who will take the first turn (unless otherwise specified).
If it is not the first battle round, the players make a roll-off called the priority roll and the winner decides who 
will take the first turn. If the roll-off is a tie, the player who took the first turn in the previous battle round 
decides who will take the first turn in the current battle round.
When it is a playerΟΔβ²§β³Δs turn, regardless of whether they take the first or second turn, they are referred to as the 
active player.
2. Determine the Underdog: Whichever player has the fewest victory points is the underdog for the battle round. 
If tied, there is no underdog, unless otherwise specified. 
3. Earn Command Points (see Commands 1.1): At the start of each battle round, after determining the underdog, 
each player gains 4 command points. If there is an underdog, they gain 1 extra command point. At the end of 
the battle round, the playersΟΔβ²§β³Δ command points are reset to 0 (any that have not been used are lost).
4. Start of Battle Round Abilities: The active player can use any  StartΟ²¬†ofΟ²¬†BattleΟ²¬†Round  abilities, then their 
opponent can do the same.
UNIVERSAL WEAPON ABILITIES
Anti-X (+1 Rend): Add 1 to this weaponΟΔβ²§β³Δs Rend 
characteristic if the target has the keyword after 
ΟΔβ²§Υ€Anti-ΟΔβ²§β³Δ or fulfils the condition after ΟΔβ²§Υ€Anti-ΟΔβ²§β³Δ. Multiples 
of this ability are cumulative. For example, if a 
weapon has both AntiΟΔβ²§βÄ‰chargeΟ²¬†(+1Ο²¬†Rend) and 
Anti-HeroΟ²¬†(+1Ο²¬†Rend), then add 2 to the Rend 
characteristic of the weapon for attacks that target a 
Hero that charged in the same turn.
Charge (+1 Damage): Add 1 to this weaponΟΔβ²§β³Δs Damage 
characteristic if the attacking unit charged this turn.
Companion: This weapon is not affected by abilities 
used by a friendly unit that affect the Attacks 
characteristic or the attack sequence.
Crit (2 Hits): If an attack made with this weapon 
scores a criticalΟ²¬†hit, that attack scores 2 hits on 
the target unit instead of 1. Make a wound roll for 
each hit.
Crit (Auto-wound): If an attack made with this 
weapon scores a criticalΟ²¬†hit, that attack automatically 
wounds the target. Make a save roll as normal.
Crit (Mortal): If an attack made with this weapon 
scores a criticalΟ²¬†hit, that attack inflicts mortal 
damage on the target unit equal to the Damage 
characteristic of that weapon and the attack 
sequence ends.
Shoot in Combat: This weapon can be used to 
make shooting attacks even if the attacking unit is 
in combat.
UNIVERSAL TERRAIN ABILITIES
Cover: Subtract 1 from hit rolls for attacks that target 
a unit that is behind or wholly on this terrain feature, 
unless that unit charged or has the Fly keyword.
Impassable: Models cannot move across, be set up on 
or end moves on any part of this terrain feature.
Obscuring: A unit cannot be targeted by shooting 
attacks if it is behind or wholly on this terrain feature, 
unless it has the Fly keyword.
Place of Power: Heroes within 3" of this terrain 
feature can use the ΟΔβ²§Υ€Activate Place of PowerΟΔβ²§β³Δ ability.
Unstable: Models can move across but cannot be set 
up on or end any type of move on any part of this 
terrain feature that is more than 1" tall.

Permission to download/print for personal use only. Ο²¬© Copyright Games Workshop Limited 2025
Spearhead is a fast, fun way to play small skirmish-style games of Warhammer Age 
of Sigmar with a friend. But what if you have three friends who all want to play 
Spearhead with you? Worry not, as on the following pages, you can find rules for playing 
Spearhead Doubles! 
These rules explain how to use your existing Spearhead sets to team up with a friend and battle 
another two-player team in Aqshy, Ghyran or Shyish.
YouΟΔβ²§β³Δll need 2 Spearhead Gaming Packs ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ either Fire and Jade or Sand and Bone ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ and each 
player will need a Spearhead army.
SPEARHEAD DOUBLES 
SPEARHEAD DOUBLED!
Spearhead Doubles is also an easy way to simply playΟΔβ²§¬Π a bigger game of Spearhead! ItΟΔβ²§β³Δs 
very common for 2 players to each have a copy of Fire and Jade or Sand and Bone, and 
if you wanted to, you could simply follow the rules on these pages to allow you and your 
opponent to battle one another using 2 Spearhead armies each. This is a great way to get 
all those armies youΟΔβ²§β³Δve been collecting onto the tabletop ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ indeed, if you have 2 Spearhead 
armies from the same faction, like Cities of Sigmar or Ogor Mawtribes, then youΟΔβ²§β³Δll have a 
thematic force like no other!

Permission to download/print for personal use only. Ο²¬© Copyright Games Workshop Limited 2025
PRE-BATTLE SEQUENCE
1.	 Each player picks a Spearhead army to command. 
Players on the same team must pick different 
Spearhead armies.
2.	 The players on each team roll off to determine their 
teamΟΔβ²§β³Δs overlord. Whenever a team cannot agree on a 
decision, the overlord makes the final decision.
3.	 The players on each team roll a dice and add their 
rolls together. The team that rolls highest wins. 
This is called a Doubles roll-off. The winner of the 
Doubles roll-off chooses which team is the attacking 
team and which is the defending team.
4.	 Each player on the attacking team picks their 
regiment ability and their enhancement for their 
Spearhead army. Then each player on the defending 
team does the same.
5.	 The defending team chooses where the players will 
be fighting. If you are using Fire and Jade, pick either 
Aqshy or Ghyran:
If you are using SandΟ²¬†and Bone, pick either Ossia 
or Dolorum: 

The defending team then sets up 2 realm battlefields 
for that location as shown in the corresponding 
realm map (pg 3).
6.	 The defending team picks 1 of the deployment 
maps (pg 3) and chooses which territory belongs to 
which team. 
7.	 If you are using Sand and Bone, the defending 
team sets up the mystical terrain as shown on the 
deployment map (if any).
8.	 If you are using Fire and Jade, the defending team 
sets up 2 large terrain features and 2 small terrain 
features. Then the attacking team does the same. 

If you are using Sand and Bone, the defending team 
sets up both Terrain Group A (Large Wall and Small 
Fence) and Terrain Group B (Small Wall and Large 
Fence). Then the attacking team does the same. 

In all cases, terrain features must be set up wholly 
within friendly territory, more than 6" from all other 
terrain features and more than 3" from both long 
battlefield edges and enemy territory. Terrain features 
cannot be set up on top of the objectives (either 
wholly or partially).
DEPLOYMENT
Do not deploy armies as described in the Core Rules. 
Instead, one player on the attacking team sets up all 
the units in their army first, then one player onΟ²¬†the 
defending team, followed by the other player on 
theΟ²¬†attacking team and finally the other player onΟ²¬†the 
defending team. Each unit must be set up wholly within 
friendly territory and more than 6" from enemy territory.
BATTLE LENGTH
The battle lasts for 4 battle rounds.
BATTLEPLAN
SPEARHEAD DOUBLES
After a deadly battle, two generals find common cause in a shared set of foes. 
WithΟ²¬†little choice and even less trust, they take to the field with their depleted 
forces to rout the enemy and claim glory.
In Spearhead Doubles, you will team up with a friend to take on another team of 2 players. As a group, youΟΔβ²§β³Δll need 
to decide which Spearhead battlepack to use: Fire and Jade or Sand and Bone. Once youΟΔβ²§β³Δve decided, follow the 
rules below.

## Page 4

Permission to download/print for personal use only. Ο²¬© Copyright Games Workshop Limited 2025
SPECIAL RULES
DOUBLES
The following rules are used in Spearhead 
Doubles battles:
ΟΔβ²§¬Δ	 Units controlled by the other player on your team are 
friendly units, but any rules for your Spearhead army 
that affect or target friendly units only affect or target 
units in your own Spearhead army.
ΟΔβ²§¬Δ	 Units in the opposing teamΟΔβ²§β³Δs Spearhead armies are 
enemy units.
ΟΔβ²§¬Δ	 For battle tactics that require a named objective to be 
controlled (e.g. ΟΔβ²§Υ€Control the Dracothion DaisΟΔβ²§β³Δ), your 
team must control both objectives with that name in 
order to score that battle tactic.
ΟΔβ²§¬Δ	 For battle tactics that require a terrain feature to 
be controlled (e.g. ΟΔβ²§Υ€Control the large terrain feature 
in enemy territoryΟΔβ²§β³Δ), your team must control both 
of those terrain features in order to score that 
battle tactic.
ΟΔβ²§¬Δ	 Where a rule says ΟΔβ²§Υ€the players roll offΟΔβ²§β³Δ, use the 
Doubles roll-off instead.
ΟΔβ²§¬Δ	 When alternating picking units to use a Fight ability, 
one team picks 1 unit from 1 of their Spearhead 
armies, then the other team picks 1 unit from 1 of 
their Spearhead armies, and so on. 
PREPARING THE SPEARHEAD DECK
One player takes the twist deck that corresponds to the 
Spearhead battlepack you are using and the realm map 
that was chosen, shuffles it and places it face down next 
to the battlefield (the other twist deck is not used).
Then, each team takes 1 battle tactic deck that 
corresponds to the Spearhead battlepack you are using, 
shuffles it and places it face down where they can reach it 
during the battle.
START OF THE BATTLE ROUND SEQUENCE
If it is the first battle round, the attacking team chooses 
who will take the first turn. In any other battle round, 
the players make a priority roll (Core Rules, 12.0).
1.	 Determine the underdog (Core Rules, 12.0).
2.	 The twist card is drawn (see opposite).
3.	 The players draw battle tactic cards (see opposite).
4.	 Any abilities with the Ο²¬†StartΟ²¬†ofΟ²¬†BattleΟ²¬†RoundΟ²¬† timing 
are used.
TWIST CARDS
One twist card is drawn at the start of each battle 
round. Follow the rules on it as it is drawn.
BATTLE TACTIC CARDS
At the start of the first battle round, each team draws a 
shared hand of 3 battle tactic cards. 
At the start of each subsequent battle round, each 
team draws battle tactic cards until they once more 
have a shared hand of 3 battle tactic cards. Before they 
do so, they can discard any number of battle tactic 
cards in their hand. Cards are discarded face up. Both 
players on a team can contribute to scoring their teamΟΔβ²§β³Δs 
battle tactics.
VICTORY POINTS
Each team scores victory points at the end of each of 
their turns as follows:
ΟΔβ²§¬Δ	 Score 1 victory point if you control two or 
more objectives.
ΟΔβ²§¬Δ	 Score 1 victory point if you control four or 
more objectives.
ΟΔβ²§¬Δ	 Score 1 victory point if you control more objectives 
than the opposing team.
ΟΔβ²§¬Δ	 Score 1 victory points for each battle tactic your team 
completed this turn.
GLORIOUS VICTORY
The team with the most victory points at the end of 
the battle is the winner. If the teams are tied on victory 
points, the battle is a draw. 
SEIZING THE INITIATIVE
If the team that went second in the previous battle 
round wins the priority roll and chooses to go first, 
it is called seizing the initiative. When a team 
seizes the initiative, they do not draw any battle 
tactic cards for that battle round unless they are 
the underdog and the difference in victory points 
between the two teams is 5 or greater.

## Page 1

TOURNAMENT 
ORGANISERΟ²¬†PACK 2025-26
WHY RUN A WARHAMMER AGE OF 
SIGMAR EVENT?
Organised events are a hugely enjoyable and exciting 
way to play Warhammer Age of Sigmar, but without 
tournament organisers (TOs), no one would get to 
experience them. Running an event is not only fun for 
the TO, it is also a different way to enjoy the hobby. 
There are few things as rewarding as watching over a 
group of enthusiastic hobbyists playing competitive 
Warhammer Age of Sigmar at an event that you have 
organised. Moreover, itΟΔβ²§β³Δs a well-known fact that TOs are 
the true heroes of the Mortal Realms!
But what does it mean to be a TO? Ultimately, a TO 
is in charge of setting up and running an event. This 
includes organising the venue, preparing a ΟΔβ²§Υ€packΟΔβ²§β³Δ that 
sets out everything players need to know to prepare for 
and attend the event, welcoming players to the venue, 
making decisions on rules queries (or bringing in an 
appointed judge) and compΟÉ¬®ring the closing ceremony 
while the awards are being handed out.
This section of the GeneralΟΔβ²§β³Δs Handbook provides you 
with all the information you need to be a TO for your 
own event. It contains lots of hints and tips on how you 
can customise the event to your own preferences as well 
as an example tournament pack.
We have split the information into two main categories: 
what a TO needs to consider when planning an event 
and what a TO will need to do during the event. 
Remember, though, these are simply guidelines intended 
to assist you in planning and running your event; they 
are not hard and fast rules.
PLANNING YOUR EVENT
There is a saying that TOs in the Warhammer Age of 
Sigmar event community often use: ΟΔβ²§Υ€Run the event that 
you would like to play inΟΔβ²§β³Δ.
THE PACK
Every event should have a document called a ΟΔβ²§Υ€packΟΔβ²§β³Δ. The 
pack provides the players with all the details about the 
event, such as where it is, what battlepack will be played 
and what awards can be won. An example pack can be 
downloaded from warhammer-community.com.
THE BATTLEPACK
One of your first decisions should be what battlepack 
you want to use for your event ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ and there are many 
different battlepacks published by Games Workshop 
for you to choose from! We also periodically publish 
new battlepacks in WhiteΟ²¬†Dwarf or on warhammer-
community.com.
The GeneralΟΔβ²§β³Δs Handbook 2025-26 battlepack is designed 
for Matched Play events; however, there are multiple 
ways to play Warhammer Age of Sigmar, and you 
should pick a battlepack that reflects the type of event 
you wish to run. For example, the Spearhead battlepack 
Sand and Bone is fantastic for small, quick games of 
Warhammer Age of Sigmar using fixed army rosters, 
the Matched Play battlepack First Blood is excellent 
for introducing players to competitive play, while the 
Path to Glory battlepack Ravaged Coast provides a 
framework for narrative play and campaigns.
MAXIMUM POINTS LIMIT
The maximum points limit will determine how large 
each playerΟΔβ²§β³Δs army can be. The higher you set the 
maximum points limit, the longer it will take players to 
finish their games.
The most common points limit for Matched Play events 
is 2000 points; however, feel free to set a higher or lower 
points limit if it would better suit your event.
ARMY ROSTERS
With regard to army rosters, it is worth bearing in mind 
that new rules can change the points of existing units or 
otherwise change army composition options. As both 
events and new miniature and battletome releases often 
take place on a weekend, this means that new rules can 
come into effect after the first round of an event has 
started. Therefore, many TOs will specify a ΟΔβ²§Υ€cut-off dateΟΔβ²§β³Δ 
for rules ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ any rules introduced after this date cannot 
be used at the event. The timing of the cutΟΔβ²§βÄ‰off date is 
entirely at your discretion, but we suggest either the 
same day as the pre-event list submission deadline (see 
below) or 1-2 weeks before the event to give players time 
to adjust their lists.
As TO, you might be happy for players to bring their 
army rosters to their event and show them to their 
opponents before each game, or you might prefer players 
to submit their army rosters a week or so prior to the 
event. Having a pre-event list submission deadline 
means that you have time to independently check army 
BATTLEPACK PREP
ItΟΔβ²§β³Δs worth having a good read of each battlepack to 
make sure that it fits your vision for your event. Not 
only will this ensure that you understand the rules 
of the battlepack, it will also give you an indication 
of how much pre-event preparation is required. For 
instance, creating the opening narrative of a Path to 
Glory event will take a little more effort to prepare 
than an off-the-shelf First Blood event, but it will be 
no less exciting or rewarding.
TOURNAMENT ORGANISER PACK

---

## Page 2

rosters for mistakes and allows you to share the lists 
among all the players, should you wish. This kind of 
event is often called an ΟΔβ²§Υ€open-list eventΟΔβ²§β³Δ.
One advantage of open-list events is that the players 
also get a chance to look over and discuss their 
potential opponentsΟΔβ²§β³Δ lists, so they can help to identify 
any inconsistencies that might need to be fixed before 
the event.
Whichever option you decide is best for your event, we 
recommend that you ask players to use the Warhammer 
Age of Sigmar App to build their army roster so that the 
rosters are clear and all follow the same format.
NUMBER OF ROUNDS
You will need to choose how many rounds you 
want to have at your event. Each player will play 
1 game of Warhammer Age of Sigmar per round, 
so at a three-round event, each player will play 
3 games. The table below gives an indication of 
how long a typical 2000-point event will take 
depending on the suggested number of rounds. 
Note that your event can contain as many rounds 
as you like; the numbers below are indications on 
how long an event with that many rounds will take.
SCHEDULING ROUND TIMINGS
You will need to decide how much time will be allotted 
for each round and how this will determine the schedule 
for your event. For a 2000-point game, we suggest 
allowing around 2 to 3 hours per round. If your players 
are relatively experienced at playing Matched Play 
games, they should be able to finish more quickly and 
you can have shorter round times.
Players will need a break between each round and you, 
as the TO, will need enough time after all the games 
have finished to collate the results and work out the 
pairings for the next round. We suggest a minimum of 
30 minutes between rounds.
BATTLEPLANS
You can pick battleplans before your event or randomly 
determine them before each round. If you pick them 
before the event and make them public, players can 
tailor their lists to be the most efficient for those 
battleplans. Conversely, using ΟΔβ²§Υ€secretΟΔβ²§β³Δ or randomly 
determined battleplans will mean that players will likely 
need to bring ΟΔβ²§Υ€all-rounderΟΔβ²§β³Δ army rosters in order to 
do well.
You will also need to consider terrain placement. The 
GeneralΟΔβ²§β³Δs Handbook 2025-26 battlepack contains 2 rules 
sets for terrain placement: in the first, placement is 
determined by the attacker/defender roll-off, while the 
second uses preset terrain layouts.
SUGGESTED GAME SPEED MILESTONES 
AND TIME BREAKDOWN
Outside of tournaments, many players do not 
complete their games against a set clock. As a result, 
when a loudspeaker shouts ΟΔβ²§Υ€Two hours remaining!ΟΔβ²§β³Δ, 
most players do not naturally think ΟΔβ²§Υ€We should 
almost be done with the entire first battle round!ΟΔβ²§β³Δ. 
To help with this, rather than simply calling time 
left, we recommend calling time based upon the 
game speed milestone players should have (on 
average) arrived at. These time calls exist to help 
keep the flow of play moving, and are not binding. 
The suggested milestones are:
3:00	 ΟΔβ²§βÄ€	Start round (formally)
2:50	 ΟΔβ²§βÄ€	Complete pre-game discussions and decisions
2:30	 ΟΔβ²§βÄ€	Deployment complete, begin Round 1
1:50	 ΟΔβ²§βÄ€	First Battle Round complete, begin Round 2
1:10	 ΟΔβ²§βÄ€	Second Battle Round complete, begin Round 3
0:40	 ΟΔβ²§βÄ€	Third Battle Round complete, begin Round 4
0:20	 ΟΔβ²§βÄ€	Fourth Battle Round complete, begin Round 5
0:10	 ΟΔβ²§βÄ€	Do not begin a new Battle Round without a 
judgeΟΔβ²§β³Δs permission
For clarity, this provides each player with, on 
average, the following time breakdown for the 
typical game:
ΟΔβ²§¬Δ	 Pre-game activities, review army lists, select 
secondaries: 5 minutes
ΟΔβ²§¬Δ	 Deploying their models: 10 minutes
ΟΔβ²§¬Δ	 Playing their First Turn: 20 minutes
ΟΔβ²§¬Δ	 Playing their Second Turn: 20 minutes
ΟΔβ²§¬Δ	 Playing their Third Turn: 15 minutes
ΟΔβ²§¬Δ	 Playing their Fourth Turn: 10 minutes
ΟΔβ²§¬Δ	 Playing their Fifth Turn: 10 minutes
We also recommend letting the players know that 
if they are concerned about finishing their game 
on time, they should not hesitate to call a judge. It 
is a good approach that, to ensure all games finish 
within the allotted time, judge assistance is an 
active part of time-constrained games.
